Michelle Roach] Trying to create ACH prenote file but there is nothing in it
Michelle Roach] I need a call back ASAP Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah P.O. Box 143 1126 S 13th St Tekamah, NE 68061 (402)374-2521

Susan Thomas] Hi Michelle, The most likely cause of the prenote file being empty is that the ""Create prenotification file"" option only includes customers whose Prenoted checkbox is not yet checked in their bank information. If all of your ACH customers already have the Prenoted flag checked, the prenote file will come out empty. To verify, go into a customer's ACH tab and check whether the Prenoted checkbox is checked. If it is, those customers won't appear in a prenote file — they would only appear in a regular ACH file. If you have customers that are newly set up for ACH and their Prenoted box is unchecked, those are the ones that should be showing up. Let me know what you find and we can go from there! Please let me know if you need any further assistance.

Susan Thomas] Dear Michelle, The resolution for this ticket has been provided. If the issue has not been resolved please update the ticket as soon as possible by responding in this ticket. (Please note: You have up to 7 days to notify inHANCE of any issues or concerns related to this ticket. If no issues or concerns have been noted within the 7-day window, the issue is considered complete and this ticket will automatically close.) However, if the issue has been resolved, please close the ticket and take a moment to provide your rating of the service you received.

Tristan Plessel] Accidentally deleted meter #77210024 while trying to do a meter change out - I do not know what account this meter belonged to and am hoping maybe this information could be recovered.

Susan Thomas] I reached out and connected remotely with Tristan. We were unable to locate the account the removed meter belonged to. She mentioned that she completed a meter change-out and accidentally removed the incorrect meter. I will need to do additional research and will follow up once I have more information.

Susan Thomas] Hi Tristan, You can find the account that meter #77210024 belonged to by running the Meter Changes report. Here's how: Go to Utility Billing > Reports > User Defined. In the left-hand pane of the User Defined Reports Explorer, select the Sample Reports folder. In the right-hand pane, locate the report titled ""Meter Changes"" and double-click it to open the Report Preview. This report lists all meter changes (both installed and removed) within the date range shown at the bottom left of the explorer, so you should be able to find the account that meter was associated with. Once you've identified the account, let us know if you need help getting the meter restored!

Tricia Kuszak] I don""t think the ACH payments posted to the customers accounts. When I ran bills it appears that it did not. Is there possibly a step I forgot?

Mitch Dahl] You could spot check a few bank pay customers in Customer Lookup on the History tab and see if a payment shows up or not. If not, then do the ACH Pay from UB-Processing-Enter Payments. After that, re-compute the bank pay customers in Compute Charges with the OK to calculate printed bills checkbox checked, but only for the ACH customers. Finally print the bills for those customers again.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Tricia, We recommend spot checking a few customers to see if their payment shows on their account. You can look via Customer Lookup on the History tab for a payment. If you don't see those payments, you might've missed the second step. Remember when you process ACH payments created with PowerManager's ACH File, you must post the payments on the account after sending the file to the bank. You post those payments by going to UB --> Processing --> Enter Payments, click the ACH Pay button, select the ACH file, and click Ok. After that, re-compute the bank pay customers in Compute Charges with the OK to calculate printed bills checkbox checked, but only for the ACH customers. Finally print the bills for those customers again.
Tricia Kuszak] Tricia Kuszak Deputy Clerk City of Loup City"

Michelle Roach] Water bills did not print gallons used on them and they are showing a past due amount for customers that do not have a past due. I have customers calling.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Good morning Michelle, Can you please send an example of a bill that shows a past due amount that shouldn't be there? Can you also please send a screenshot of that customer's account history and balance?
Michelle Roach] This is one but I believe it happened to mostly all of them. Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Can you also please send a screenshot of that customer's account history from Customer Lookup and their account balance?
Michelle Roach] Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Mitch Dahl] This is usually associated with the rate setup in the Add To # column. The usage component is probably adding to something for which there is no charge. Typically the Add To # for the usage component is blank and other components add to it.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Michelle. Changed the Add To setup per Mitch. Previewed a bill and it's corrected now. Also confirmed the programming change we did to their bill for the late fee is correct.
Michelle Roach] Some customers have the 2/20/26 bill on the right side and some do not. Why is this? Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Hi Michelle, Did you know if Bev Hansen had a bill printed at all?
Michelle Roach] Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] I'm glad to see it did print a bill for her. Are there many accounts that are like this? It'd be good to double check the bill printing setup. If you go to UB --> Processing --> Print Bills --> Setup. Under the PDF Archive Files we should see both options checked (create pdf archive files AND create pdf archive files when reprinting). In the PDF archive file template, we should see Use the following template and your bill selected in the drop down field.

Michelle Roach] Yes there are quite a few like this. Also we didn’t talk about the dates on the bills. They read 1/20-2/20. Do I manually change this and where? Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] If the pdf archive checkboxes weren't checked in setup, it's possible that's why some customers don't have the record. I think as long as you can still access the bill information, it is ok. Make sure to double check those bill printing settings and if it continues to happen, we can try to see what else might be causing the issue. As for the dates, those are correct. If I remember correctly, your last charges date or bill printing date in January was 1/20. So the bill is recording the correct service from service to dates based on the date those previous transactions were generated.

Michelle Roach] No those dates are not correct. They should be from when we read which is usually the first week of the month to the next month around the same time. It cannot be the 20th. That is the date I do bills and mail them out. The service is prior to that. This has to change. Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] If you can do me a favor and open the Meter Readings history for someone's account and send a screenshot? You can access this by going to UB --> Customer Lookup --> Select your customer --> Meter --> Readings.
Michelle Roach] Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521
Mitch Dahl] Typically, the service from and service to dates come from the meter reading dates. Might have to look at the bill design to see if there is an issue there.

Michelle Roach] Emelia I need you to call me today. On Paystar we noticed the new bills are not on there. When I told you the bills were not showing up on the right side of each customers account on the account lookup page, that was a problem. Please call Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Called, Michelle stepped out to run errands. I will call back in ~20 minutes.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Michelle. There was not Monthly Bill File created with Feb's billing, so the statements weren't updated on Paystar's portal. Walked Michelle through the steps again to create the export file to be manually uploaded. She asked how to upload it to Paystar, I advised she'd have to contact Paystar for that information. She also said the balances were incorrect in the Paystar portal. I confirmed the daily balance upload has been processing daily at 8:15am CST and uploading successfully to Paystar's FTP site. We also confirmed the balances in that file are correct. It might be an issue on Paystar's end as to why the balances aren't updating correctly. Sent a follow up email to Abel and CC'd Michelle. We also looked at the dates of service on the bill. For every bill, the dates were what they should be: the prior and present reading dates from the meter. I don't know where Michelle saw the date of 1/20, but that wasn't on any of the Jan or Feb bill statements. Confirmed with Michelle they want to see the meter reading dates there, so no corrections were needed as it was functioning as intended.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Pasting from Outlook, sent to Mitch M: Hey Mitch, I have a bit of a conundrum with Tekamah’s most recent billing. I’ll do my Michelle printed bills and the majority of people don’t have a pdf record in their Bill tab in Customer History. Some do, so I don’t think it’s a setup issue in Bill Printing. She originally said the service from/to dates were wrong. One bill she sent as an example in the ticket showed dates of 1/20-2/20. When I checked the bill design today, the dates were correct from the meter reading record. Not sure how she produced a bill with 1 set of dates and then I see something different today. When producing the paystar daily balance file (with the “All” option selected for which charges to include), the balances for most customers were 0.00, even though their account had a balance. I change the charges selection to “Finalized”, produced a new file, and the balances were correct. I have a feeling this has something to do with the strange bill pdfs & date issues. Do you know what could’ve happened to cause these issues? I find it all very suspect. I wonder if Michelle did something or missed something? More importantly, do these sound like 1-off issues to you or a potential recurring problem?

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Good morning Michelle, I wanted to update you on what might be the issue with this month's bills. It's possible this happened if the bills were printed from the preview screen. What I'd recommend to fix it is to print bills (not reprint, just regular print) to a pdf file on your computer to record all bills as printed and to also create the pdf bills in Customer Lookup history.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Michelle. We printed bills for all and saved to a PDF. There were about 1/2 the customers in this group. Confirmed the accounts that were missing the history before now have the PDF."

DORI HUCK] Is there a way we can make a report that is a time off request sheet for employees that give them there balance in vacation sick and comp? Dori Doretta “Dori” Huck City Clerk / Treasurer City of Bridgeport PO Box 280 Bridgeport NE 69336 Office: 308.262.1623 Fax: 308.262.1599 Email: dhuck@cityofbport.com

Sreya Johny] Dori, You can use the built-in Time Off report in PowerManager's Payroll module to create a time off request sheet showing employee balances for vacation, sick, and comp time. Navigate to Payroll > Reports > Employee > Time Off In the PR - Time Off Form that appears, you'll see these options: Select a report format from the Report Grid Choose to run for current pay period or specify a date range Option to include department subtotals Option to include inactive employees Sort employees by various criteria Click the Preview button to view the report, which will show Start, Earned, Used, and Balance values for each time off type Please let us know if you have any questions.

Sachin Yadav] Comment added in TFS by Billy Stamps **DO 7003 - B.S. 3/17/2026 - Inventory Transfer/Adjustment Retry Logic** Added retry tracking for GP inventory transfers and adjustments that fail. Previously, a failed record stayed in the queue (status 0) and was retried indefinitely on every run, and one failure would block all remaining records from processing. Changes: - New `T_RetryCount` column added to `TGO_InvTransfer_Inbound_Data_Hdr` and `TGO_InvTransaction_Inbound_Data_Hdr` tables (INT, default 0). - On failure, the retry count is incremented and the error message is recorded in `T_ErrorMessage`. - When retry count reaches the configured maximum, `T_IntegrationStatus` is set to 9 (permanently failed/skipped). - Maximum retries is configurable via `sa_enumerations` (type=`MAX_GP_INV_RET`, valid range 1-15, defaults to 5 if not set). - Failed individual records no longer stop the entire batch — remaining records continue processing. Files modified: TransferToGPProcessor.cs, TransferToGPData.cs, TransferToGPData.designer.cs, TransferToGPData.dbml SQL scripts to run before deployment: - `alter_table_TGO_InvTransfer_Inbound_Data_Hdr_Add_RetryCount.sql` - `alter_table_TGO_InvTransaction_Inbound_Data_Hdr_Add_RetryCount.sql` Optional config: `INSERT INTO sa_enumerations (type, switch_value) VALUES ('MAX_GP_INV_RET', '5')`

Tricia Kuszak] I have An emplyee whose ACH won't print off. I want to make sure our machine operator gets paid.

Susan Thomas] Proposed Solution This appears to be an issue with the ACH payment process in the Payroll module. The problem could be related to missing bank account information for the employee, incorrect setup in the Print Checks ACH process, or an issue with the ACH file generation. Steps to complete Verify that the employee has proper bank account information entered in their employee record. Missing bank account information is a common cause of ACH printing issues. Navigate to Payroll/Processing menu and select Print Checks. In the Payment Method section, select ACH (for direct deposit). Enter the Check Date, Pay Period Start Date, and Pay Period End Date. Click Next or Select Employees to proceed with employee selection. After selecting employees, proceed to the ACH setup form (Step 3) where you'll need to enter: Transmission date Effective settlement date ACH file name Click Next to proceed to the final step where you'll verify the employee list. Make sure there's a check mark in the ""OK To Print"" box for the employee in question. Click Print to generate the direct deposit forms and create the ACH file. Confirm that the forms printed correctly when prompted. Additional Notes If the issue persists, it may be necessary to recalculate the payroll amounts before attempting to print the ACH forms again. Based on similar tickets, sometimes adding or verifying bank accounts for employees and then recalculating can resolve the issue.

Tony Williams] Leslee, I understand your concern about the Oasis database size. We have some other customers with very large databases. This is typically caused by large log files that don't automatically shrink after backups. The log file (oasis.ldf) can grow significantly over time, especially if: Your database is in ""Full"" recovery mode Large reports or queries are run regularly Backups aren't clearing logs properly To resolve this: Check if your database is in ""Simple"" recovery model Use SQL Management Studio to shrink the log file: Right-click the database Select Tasks > Shrink > Files Choose ""Log"" as the file type Shrink the file This should free up enough space to allow test database restoration in emergency situations.

BRANDY BOLTER] We are looking at adding a senior citizen discount to our utility bills. I am needing someone to contact me or send me instructions on how we would go about adding this to the bills.

Susan Thomas] Option 1: Create a Discount Component in Rate Maintenance Navigate to Utility Billing → Maintenance → Rate → Components Create a new component specifically for senior citizen discounts Set the component type as ""Discount"" Enter an appropriate description (e.g., ""Senior Citizen Discount"") Set the calculation method (typically percentage-based) Enter the discount percentage amount Ensure the ""Active"" checkbox is selected Navigate to Utility Billing → Maintenance → Rate → Rates Edit each rate where you want to apply the senior discount Add the senior discount component to the rate Set the ""Season"" to ""ALL"" to apply year-round Option 2: Apply Discounts Through Adjustments For individual accounts that qualify for the senior discount: Navigate to Utility Billing → Processing → Adjustments Select the customer account On the ""Charges"" tab, enter the adjustment amount for the discount If the discount affects taxable charges, use the ""Calc Tax"" button to properly calculate tax adjustments Save the adjustment Identifying Senior Citizens in the System To track which customers qualify for the senior discount: Navigate to Utility Billing → Maintenance → Customer → Customer Edit the customer account You can use one of these methods to identify senior citizens: Add a note in the customer's account Use a custom field if available Create a specific customer type for senior citizens

Tony Williams] Good morning Patty, I've looked into the issue you're experiencing with applying non-metered accounts. This appears to be related to a custom setting in the Impresa database that's preventing the apply function from working properly, even though verify apply shows the correct information. We've seen this exact issue before with other customers. There's a database switch called ""ignore_save_nm"" that might be turned on in your system. When this switch is active, it can cause the ""sorry no data for this report"" error during the apply process. To resolve this, we'll need to run a simple database script to turn this switch off. I can help coordinate this with you. Before we proceed, could you confirm if you've closed your reading books first? This is sometimes required before applying non-metered accounts. Let me know when would be a good time to connect and address this issue, as I understand you need to apply these accounts today.
Chris Holloway] Hi Patty, I called a few minutes ago and will schedule to give you a call back Monday around 9am your time to help with this.

Patty McGee] Chris, Never mind. I needed help on Feb. 24th. We reset or restarted the server, and I closed the books again. We don’t know why it didn’t work the first time. I did find out there was a report to see it didn’t work. Out of curiosity what would happen if I tried to close the books twice. Patty

Chris Holloway] Hi Patty, If any account's showed up in the list when you close the reading books as an error, then the books would not close for any of the accounts at all. If you close the reading books, and it is successful, then close them again, all the accounts would show up as errors. I have attached a short video that shows how to print a report to show which books have been closed in impresa. Download and unzip the .zip file to view the video.

Tana Valle] We have a final bill on an account that had the reading that was entered incorrectly. It shows 9112000 usage from pump station 7. Is there a way we can go in and get this changed?

Tony Williams] Yes, you can correct the final reading. You'll need to use the Re-read feature in the Final Bill process. As long as no new readings have been applied to the meter at the service location that was attached to the final bill account. Also, you cannot have already close the reading books again for the book the meter is in. Attach the Service Location that was previously attached. Go to the customer's account Click the Final Bill button on the Process menu Proceed through the wizard to the screen where you enter readings Check the Re-read box Enter the correct reading This will recalculate the charges based on the new reading. Note that this will only work if you haven't closed the reading books or done an apply since the final bill was generated. If those actions have already been done, you'll need to adjust the account manually.

AMBER WYNN] Please see attached document for reference.When printing reminders, the final account has been listed twice. This has happened more than once. If I delete one of the two duplicates, both are deleted. Please review and advise.

Susan Thomas] Hello Amber, Proposed Solution The duplicate entry problem may be related to how accounts are being selected or filtered when generating reminder notices. Similar issues have been resolved by adjusting date parameters or checking specific account selection criteria. Steps to Complete Access the Reminders feature through Processing
column3: Reminders in the Utility Billing menu Review the ""Type of Included Accounts"" selection in the Reminders-Setup form to ensure appropriate filtering Check the date parameters (particularly the Ending Date) to confirm they are set correctly Verify whether the ""Ignore ending date for payments"" checkbox is appropriately set for your needs Run a test print with these adjusted settings to see if the duplicate entry issue is resolved If the issue persists, consider selecting specific customers manually rather than using automated filters Additional Information The duplicate entries issue appears to be related to how the system processes the final account in the reminder notice batch. This could be a software bug that needs to be addressed in a future update. In the meantime, the steps above should provide a workable solution.

Joe Sheehan] Issue The customer from City of Kimball entered an incorrect batch number (4134) when posting a batch on 2/18/26. The correct batch number should have been 4136. The receipts and other information are correct, but the posted batch number is incorrect and needs to be updated. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, there are two possible approaches to resolve this issue. Since the batch has already been posted, we cannot simply reopen it as we might with a closed but unposted batch. Instead, we will need to use the Table Utilities program to update the batch number in the relevant database tables. Steps to complete Connect remotely with the customer to verify the details of the incorrect batch posting. Run the Transaction Item Report and UB-Payment Detail Report to confirm which transactions need to be corrected and verify the current batch number. Use the Table Utilities program to identify all payments associated with batch #4134 that should be updated to batch #4136. Execute an update query to change the batch number from 4134 to 4136 in the appropriate tables (likely including the CRTransPay table and possibly UB payment tables). Run reports after the update to verify that the batch number has been correctly changed to 4136. Confirm with the customer that the reports now show the correct batch number and that everything balances properly. Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Dahl - Has extensive experience with batch corrections and has helped with similar issues in multiple tickets. Susan Thomas - Has assisted with batch number corrections and worked with Mitch on similar issues. Additional Notes This issue appears to be purely a data correction and should not affect the actual payment processing or customer accounts, as the customer mentioned that ""Receipts etc. are okay."" The correction is needed to ensure accurate record-keeping and reporting. Based on similar tickets, this type of correction is routinely handled using the Table Utilities program to update the relevant database tables. Chris Holloway] Hi Heather, We were able to set up a COA Distribution Code for this. This is found in System Administration > GL Configuration > Distribution Codes. We put in the 1013 account in the search field, then set the Alternate COA string to 1015. doing this send 1015 in place of any 1013 on the export to GL report. There is also a flag on the Distribution Codes screen that will display this changed GL on the reports as well. The alternative would be to go to each screen that 1013 is set up as a distribution or COA/GL and change each of those over to the 1015 one by one. This includes any rate codes, adjustment codes, direct GL codes, groups and income centers, Penalty codes, the default suspense account on the payment information screen, and any other screen that has distribution codes like in the inventory if you use it. Susan Thomas] Issue Michelle McBride from the City of Fairbury needs help modifying a report called ""Outifi Report"". She wants to remove the city/state/zip columns because they are pulling information from the mailing address instead of the location. She would like to replace these columns with Service Address 2 from the location section of the accounts. The report already correctly pulls Service Address 1 into the Location Address column, which she wants to keep unchanged. Proposed Solution Based on similar tickets, this issue can be resolved by modifying the report using the PowerManager Report Designer. We need to remove the city/state/zip columns that currently pull data from the mailing address and add a field for Service Address 2 from the location section instead. This will ensure the report shows the correct location information rather than mailing information. LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe, I waited until today to check on those balances and it is still not uploading the balances. The account i checked said they owed 122.27 and they actually have a credit of 63.77. If we can look at that again that would be great.
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, I tried calling but it just rang and rang with no answering machine. Feel free to give me a call back at your convenience. My number is 401-900-5617. Joe
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, Correction my number is 401-900-5671 sorry about that.

Joe Sheehan] Lacy called me back. We're thinking she had site specific changes on her old machine that are not on her new machine. She's going to see if she can get her old machine back up and running and we'll see about transferring those site-specific changes.
Joe Sheehan] Found the site-specific change exe file. Still need to get onto the old computer and find out what the settings were.

Joe Sheehan] We were able to get the exe file in place and ran without error attaching updated one here. Uploaded the file to Payport via FTP and waiting on a response from an email Lacy sent out. : We sent a file via FTP and we noticed that the balances are not changing online. Attached is a screen shot of the FTP window and the daily balances file.
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, Did you ever hear anything back from Payport support? Joe

LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe this is what i received from them yesterday afternoon.Hey I might be finding an issue. Have you changed how your files are uploaded to our server lately? It appears that the balance files started showing up to us in a different format after Feb 23rd. I can put you in contact with the power manager people that handle most of the generation of your balance files and sending them over to our server. I apologize for the inconvenience you are facing and just want to be sure I load the data in properly. It looks like you had different file formats on Feb 23rd (the original file format you were using), feb 24th (a new format I had not seen before), and feb 25 (a second new format different from the one on the 24th). I just believe something changed in how your files are generated and sent to us somewhere within that time. If you need to contact the power manager people who handle this sort of issue, my contact for them is Emelia De Smet-Bacon and she can be reached at EDeSmet-Bacon@harriscomputer.com. If you have any other questions or concerns please let me know. SamTyler Nebraska Support

Joe Sheehan] @Emelia De Smet-Bacon, this is a strange one. When I went into the batch file, I noticed that the batch file was trying to run a program with ""My"" prepended to it. But when I looked in TeamSupport it wasn't showing they have any site-specific changes and they didn't have the MyUBICDailyExport application on their computer. So, with Mitch D's help I located the site-specific install file on the server. After I installed the site-specific changes, I keep getting this error when trying to launch the MyUBICDailyExport.exe file. We found the files in this location on the server: So, what I did was found another company with the MyUBICDailyExport.exe application and copied that from the PManAD2020 folder and that got us to the point where we could use the application and enter in the fields. Now Tyler Nebraska Support is saying the format has changed in the file export. Can we ask them to adapt or how should we go about this? Joe

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Good morning Joe, Interesting. The typical app we use for NE Payport daily balance files is called ""UBNIDailyExport"". There is a difference in the format between UBNIDailyExport and UBICDailyExport. I don't really know anything about the custom ""MyUBICDailyExport"" app. It's strange that they had MyUBIC and it was working before but now it's not... Do we know what or why we used a custom MyUBICDailyExport? If there's no reason why, I think we could change them to the standard UBNIDailyExport.

Joe Sheehan] @Emelia De Smet-Bacon, we migrated their PowerManager computers a little while ago and since we didn't have any documentation about them having site-specific changes they never got installed on their new computers. So the MyUBICDailyExport stopped working. I tried changing them back to the standard UBICDailyExport but the file format is completely different and Sam from Tyler Nebraska Support is saying the format has changed. I also tried to install the site-specific changes but they weren't working, so I grabbed a myUBICDailyExport from another customer's folder and I got that working but Sam is still saying the format is not right. Should we ask Sam to adapt or have Mitch M look at this one?
Joe Sheehan] Oh wait I see what your saying now. Use the UBNIDailyExport application right?

Joe Sheehan] Called and remoted into Lacy's computer. We updated the batch file to run the UBNIDailyExport.exe rather than the myUBICDailyExport and the file size was the same as it was before leading us to think Payport will be able to accept this file from now on. Lacy is going to check in about 15 minutes and see if the balance on one of the accounts is update and let me know.
LACY MCLAUGHLIN] I just checked the balances and they are still not correct. I think its been about 25 minutes or so.

Joe Sheehan] Lacy, Are you able to reach out to Sam for Tyler Nebraska Support and verify they got the file? Maybe he can share some more insight as to how often they scrape those directories. Or maybe we still don't have the correct format? If you can CC me on the email: Jsheehan@harriscomputer.com Joe
LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe,I will send them a message and find out more info. I feel like it should work.

LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe,The pull that happens at 8:15 didn't update the totals but Sam did another manual pull and it updated the totals. So i think this is fixed but confused at why it didn't update with the 8:15 pull. But looks like the online totals are correct right now.
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, That's wonderful news. I guess it just depends on when their system runs the script to pick up the file. Glad it's working now. I'll go ahead and mark this as closed. Joe"

Steps to complete Perform a system update and reboot the computer. This simple step resolved the issue in ticket #107587. If the error persists, try using the Data File Maintenance utility to fix any index issues: Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > GL 2 Data File Maintenance Select the correct company Choose the ""Reindex tables"" option Complete the process and try printing checks again If the system shows checks have already been printed when they haven't, try: Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > PR Data File Maintenance Go to File > Un-print non-updated payroll checks Select the appropriate employees Click OK and try printing again If you're getting a specific error related to report generation, try clicking ""Ignore All"" on any error messages and proceed. SELECT DISTINCT ublocation.Route, ublocation.AcctNr, ubcustomer.CustNr, scaddress.Name, ubmeter.MeterNr, ubbillinginfo.RateKey FROM ublocalcust ublocalcust INNER JOIN ubcustomer ubcustomer ON (ubcustomer.""Key"" = ublocalcust.CustomerKey) INNER JOIN scaddress scaddress ON (scaddress.""Key"" = ubcustomer.AddressKey) LEFT OUTER JOIN ubbillinginfo ubbillinginfo ON (ubbillinginfo.LocalCustKey = ublocalcust.""Key"") INNER JOIN ublocation ublocation ON (ublocation.""Key"" = ublocalcust.LocalKey) INNER JOIN ubmeter ubmeter ON (ubmeter.LocalKey = ublocalcust.LocalKey) INNER JOIN ubmeterbif ubmeterbif ON (ubmeterbif.MeterKey = ubmeter.""Key"" AND ubmeterbif.BifKey = ubbillinginfo.""Key"") WHERE ( ubbillinginfo.Status = 0 ) AND ( ubbillinginfo.RateKey = 13 ) ORDER BY ublocation.SortedRoute, ublocation.SortedAcctNr

column1: 21040348,"Utility Bills - Light meter reading and consumption not showing","[2026-02-20 00:34
column2: Sandra Schendt] Last month when we printed out the utility bills there were multiple lines for water, and lights. So we fixed the problem so it will show 1 line for each. When looking over the utility bills for this month. The water shows the meter reading and the consumption, but the light does not. The light shows the charge only. I've attached a pdf file showing you what I'm talking about. Any suggestions on how to fix this? I will reprint out the post cards if need be.
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Hi Sandra, This one I'm going to need to talk to the developers about. I've reached out to them and will let you know the answer as soon as I hear back.

Sandra Schendt] Would it be possible to go back into the utility billing rates for lights and take out the part that puts all of the rates together? Form example: base rate and consumption rate separated. I would rather have multiple listings of light rates so the meter readings and consumption show vs see the dollar amount only? That will give the programmers more time to figure it out, and I can get the utility bills in the mail?

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Yes, that's absolutely possible. To make that change, you can edit the rates. Go to UB --> Maintenance --> Rate --> Rates. Double click each rate to edit. In this section, you can delete the entry to remove the Add To #. Make sure you make the change for all rates.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Hi Sandra, Will you please provide us with a copy of your bill file? If you open File Explorer and go to your PowerManager folder, we're looking for a file called something like ""Nelson Postcard Bill.rtm"". Can you please attach a copy of that to this ticket?
Sandra Schendt] It's dated November 2025. Is there a way to make a more current file?
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Yes, there is a way to make sure it has the most recent changes. When we connect tomorrow for the other ticket, I will update & grab this file.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] The issue with the readings & usage not showing up has to do with the Add To column. We've changed that so it should show the readings now. However, Nelson has double components in their rates. Need to ask the Mitchs about Advised Sandra I would reach out early next week with an answer.

Mitch Dahl] Are you saying they have winter/summer usage components, but only a single base charge with the Season set to All? If that's the case, yes, they should have winter/summer base charge components also.
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Meeting schedule 3/12 to go over outstanding issues: 2 customers with bills who didn't print adding a 2nd, duplicate flat rate to add to summer vs winter usage components

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Sandra. Used a SQL select query to find all meters with ReadNrMax of 2. These are the meters that would use the electric components with ""R2."" We confirmed the accounts with those demand meters are all assigned the demand rate, not the other electric rates. So, we removed the R2 components from the other electric rates. We then added a summer flat rate and added that to the summer components. We edited the existing flat component to be set for winter. Advised of the 2 individuals without bills to see if it happens again next month. It could be a fluke. If it happens again next month, we will investigate. Sandra also asked about one customer's budget billing amount, we edited the amount from 298 to 260. Finally, we reviewed the ACH file for 2 customers with $0.00. That customer is new to ACH and the Prenoted option was not checked. This prompted PowerManager to attempt to verify the ACH information by sending a $0.00 charge. I advised when entering new ACH information, to check the prenoted to prevent the $0.00 verification charge. Sandra can edit the ACH file to draft those account balances.

Since she had already computed charges for February, I advised her to uncompute the February charges, finalize January first, and then, when she is ready, recompute the February charges and print the bills for February.I walked her through each step, and she will reopen the ticket if she needs further assistance.

I reviewed the meter tab for several accounts, and the February readings appear incorrect—January’s reading is missing, and the February reading looks like a combined value. I’m unsure what changes were made on Friday. Denise mentioned she is not in the office every day, but she can come in at 11:00 AM on Wednesday to review this further when Mitch Dahl is available.

Mitch Dahl] At this point, there are basically three options: Find and restore a backup dated prior to when the ticket was originally submitted and preferably, prior to when the February readings were entered. If need be, change the February readings back to the January readings and finalize the January bills. Then proceed with the February billling. Void all of the Printed 1/20, Due 2/10 bills, re-enter the January readings, compute, print, finalize the January bills. Enter the February readings and compute and print the February bills. Get R&D to delete all of the 1/15 charges and February meter readings. Start over with the January billing. Complete that process and then do the February billing.
Susan Thomas] Reached out and spoke with Denise, scheduled a call with Mitch Dahl tomorrow.

Tony Williams] Using the Layout Button on the Customer Screen Overview The Layout button on the Customer screen in Impresa allows users to customize how information is displayed. You can rearrange panels, save layouts for yourself, or create layouts that are shared across your entire utility. This article explains how layouts work, how to manage them safely, and what to expect when making changes. Layout Types Global Layouts (Shared) Available to all users within the same utility Any user can create, modify, or delete a global layout Changes take effect for everyone ⚠️ Important: Global layouts do not have permission controls. Changes or deletions impact all users immediately. My View (Personal Layout) Applies only to your user account Does not affect other users Recommended for personal preferences or testing changes Accessing Layout Options Open the Customer screen. Select the Layout button from the menu bar. Creating a Global Layout Open the Layout menu. Select New Global Layout. Rearrange panels or resize sections as needed. Select Save. What happens next: The layout is added to the list of available layouts It becomes the default layout It is available to all users 💡 Tip: After creating a new global layout, you may need to exit and reopen the Customer screen to see it display correctly. Updating an Existing Global Layout Select the global layout you want to change. Adjust the screen layout. The updated layout immediately applies to all users. Switching Between Layouts Use the layout list or checkboxes to switch between Global Layouts and My View. The last selected layout automatically loads the next time you open the Customer screen. Deleting a Global Layout Select the global layout. Select Delete. What to know: The layout is deleted for all users This action cannot be undone ⚠️ Use caution when deleting global layouts. Exporting a Layout You can save a copy of a layout to your computer as an image file. Select the layout you want to export. Select Export. Choose a folder location. Result: An image of the layout is saved to the selected folder. Importing a Layout Importing replaces the current layout with a previously saved layout image. Select My View or a global layout. Select Import. Choose the folder containing the saved layout. Select OK. Select Save to keep the imported layout. Important: Importing immediately overrides the current layout Importing behaves the same as manually changing the screen Known Limitations New global layouts may require closing and reopening the screen Exporting or importing layouts may cause temporary display issues In rare cases, the menu bar or Layout button may disappear and require a reset to defaults Use My View for personal layouts or experimentation Communicate before changing or deleting global layouts Export layouts before making major changes Summary Layouts allow flexible customization of the Customer screen in Impresa. Because global layouts affect all users and have no permission controls, they should be managed carefully. Using personal layouts for testing and shared layouts for standardization helps ensure a consistent and reliable experience."

LYNN GROSELY] I need to have the overtime pay amount displayed in box 14. From what I just learned the ""half"" amount in the time and a half can be deducted and needs to go in box 14. I will then need to resubmit corrected forms.
Joe Sheehan] We can either check box 14 in Earnings Overtime and add description FLSA OT or do a Employee History Report for calendar year 2025.

Susan Thomas] Hi Lynn, Please follow the steps. Option 1: Configure Overtime Earnings to Display in Box 14 The most direct solution is to modify the overtime earning code in the Payroll module to report in Box 14 of the W-2 form with an appropriate description. Option 2: Use Employee History Report An alternative approach is to run an Employee History Report for calendar year 2025 to capture the overtime information needed. Steps to complete Go to Payroll > Setup > Earnings/Deductions Maintenance Find and select your Overtime earning code Edit the earning and navigate to the W-2 reporting options Check the option to ""Print in Box 14"" for the overtime earning Enter an appropriate description for the Box 14 entry (such as ""FLSA OT"") Save your changes Test this by previewing a W-2 for an employee with overtime to ensure it appears correctly in Box 14 Once verified, you can print the corrected W-2 forms for submission Additional Information Box 14 on the W-2 form is designated for ""Other"" information that employers want to report to employees. This box is commonly used for reporting items that don't have a specific box elsewhere on the W-2 form. The W-2/W-4 page in the Employee Edit Form allows for configuration of what information appears on W-2 forms, and the earnings/deductions setup controls what values get reported in specific boxes.

JENNIFER WATERHOUSE] I am having an error message (see incorrect federal deduction error message below) after updating an employee's W-4 information. The top screen shot was what it was before the change. The bottom two are error messages after deleting out the $2000 in step 3 and checking the box in step 2 to match his new w4. Please help. Before the change- After the change & with the error message. I also tried entering zeros in the screen shot below this one and got the same outcome. Jennifer Jennifer Waterhouse City Clerk PO Box 504, 314 South 10th Ave Broken Bow, NE 68822 Office: 308-872-5831 Fax: 308-872-6885

Sreya Johny] Jennifer, This is a common issue when W-4 information is updated but the corresponding federal withholding deduction type is not properly aligned with the W-4 settings. The employee needs to have the correct federal withholding deduction type assigned that corresponds to their updated W-4 information with the Step 2 checkbox selected. Steps to Resolve Go to PR > Maintenance > Earnings/Deductions Use the Fed Update button to create the necessary federal withholding deduction types Since the employee has checked the Step 2 checkbox, they need to be assigned the deduction listed in the bottom section - 2020 or Later W-4 Step 2 Box Checked for Federal Married. If that is blank (as it is in the screen shot), you will need to add a new deduction. Go to the employee's record in Payroll and click Edit Navigate to the Deductions tab Check which federal withholding deduction is currently assigned to the employee If needed, click the + button to add a new deduction From the dropdown in the Deduct# column, select the appropriate federal withholding deduction that corresponds to ""Fed Married Step 2"" (or the appropriate filing status with Step 2 checked) Remove the previous federal withholding deduction if it's no longer applicable Save the changes Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

JENNIFER WATERHOUSE] I followed the steps and am getting the same message. Below is the screen shot of what was already listed in the 2020 or later section, so I did not have to enter anything. I updated the employee's deductions to use ""48. 2020 federal married"" instead of ""8. Federal married"" (see screenshot at the bottom) and I am getting the same error message. Also, I just realized that figures that are entered in the Federal income Tax Deduction #48 have not been updated for many years since we have not used this before. Do I need to update them to match all the figures in the #8 federal income tax deduction that we did at the start of the year? See below This has old figures from a few years ago... This was updated at the first of the year. Do I make #48 match these figures as well? Jennifer Jennifer Waterhouse City Clerk PO Box 504, 314 South 10th Ave Broken Bow, NE 68822 Office: 308-872-5831 Fax: 308-872-6885

Mitch Mullins] It appears the employee's active federal income tax deduction is correct. Deduction #48 should be used for married and step 2 box checked. I also noted the employee has a 2nd federal income tax deduction that is not checked to be computed and has $0.000 for the total. This will work, however, the preferred way when changing an employee's federal income tax deduction number is to replace the old with the new value instead of adding a new record for the change in federal income tax deduction number. This warning may be ignored without issue. Checking the ""Hide"" box on the no longer used federal deduction will prevent this warning from being displayed.

Sreya Johny] Jennifer, Instead of adding a second federal withholding deduction, replace the existing one (#8) with the appropriate ""Step 2"" deduction type (#48). If the employee has two federal income tax deductions listed, you should either: Delete the unused deduction, or Check the ""Hide"" box on the unused deduction to prevent the ""Incorrect Federal Deduction"" warning and click Save. This warning may be ignored without issue. There are different federal income tax tables depending on whether the Step 2 box is checked or not. Therefore, Deduction #8 and Deduction #48 are not supposed to be the same. Your tax tables should be up to date as long as you run the Federal Update option (PR > Maintenance > Earnings/Deductions > Fed Update > Update). Sreya
JENNIFER WATERHOUSE] This worked. Jennifer Jennifer Waterhouse City Clerk PO Box 504, 314 South 10th Ave Broken Bow, NE 68822 Office: 308-872-5831 Fax: 308-872-6885"

Susan Thomas] Hi Bev, I tried calling you earlier and left a message. I was informed that this will need to be completed manually. If you would like to remove the extra payments, please follow the steps below. To void unwanted payments in PowerManager Utility Billing EZ, follow these steps: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the correct Posting Date for the adjusting entries (this date will be used for both Accounts Receivable and General Ledger transactions) Click Adjust Select the customer whose payment you want to void Go to the Payments tab In the Payment Selection Grid, click on the row containing the payment you wish to void (the selected payment will be highlighted) You can identify payments by their PayDate, RefNr, Amount, PayMethod, BatchNr, and Comment Click the Void button at the bottom right (only click ONCE) The selected payment will appear in the Adjustments section at the bottom of the screen Click Save to complete the void process Important Notes: Only click the Void button once to avoid the ""Records already exist"" error message Voiding a payment generates reversing entries that cancel out the original amount If Accounts Receivable is interfaced with General Ledger, reversing entries will also be posted to General Ledger This process should be used when a check does not clear the bank or when a payment is posted to an incorrect customer

LESLIE NIELSEN] I am working with our auditors and they want me to close out the books as of 9/30/2024. I have never done this before and do not want to make a problem with everything!

Tony Williams] Leslie, I understand you need to close out your books as of 9/30/2024 for your auditors. Since this is your first time doing this process in PowerManager, I'll provide you with step-by-step instructions to help you complete this task safely. Before proceeding, here are the key steps for closing out your fiscal year: Make a complete backup of your dataThis is critical in case you need to restore your data for any reason. Run any year-end reports needed by your auditorsGenerate these reports before closing the year (Trial Balance, Income Statement, Balance Sheet, etc.) Ensure all transactions are properly entered and finalizedVerify all transactions for the fiscal year have been entered and finalized. Close the last month of your fiscal yearMake sure September 2024 has been closed in the system. Go to go to General Ledger > Processing > Close Period. Close the fiscal year From the PowerManager Main Menu, go to General Ledger > Processing > Close Year Click the Error Test button to check for any issues If the Error Test passes, the button will change to End Year Select your options for keeping history and click End Year to complete the process The Error Test will verify: All current year transactions are finalized The year is in balance (debits = credits) Valid close-to accounts are assigned The last month of the fiscal year has been closed Many customers also create a backup copy of their fiscal year data in a separate folder before closing. This preserves the data for the closed year while allowing you to continue working in the current year. If you encounter any errors during this process or would like assistance with the year-end close, please let us know and we can schedule a remote session to help you through it.
LESLIE NIELSEN] The clerk prior to me did do closeouts. I just have never done it since I have been working here. Does this change any of the steps? Also how do you recommend doing a backup? Leslie

Sreya Johny] Leslie, Steps to create a backup before closing the year: Go to File in PowerManager > Menu Editor > Company. Copy your company Data Location path. Open your Windows File Explorer and paste the path you copied to the address bar. Select all files in that location except the the Customer Bills PDF folder and copy them. Go back to the previous page and create a new folder for the backup. Make sure PowerManager is closed on all machines. Open the new folder and paste all the files. If any errors pop up during the copy process, please make sure all PowerManager related windows are closed on all machines, then click Try Again Once the copying is complete, copy the path of this backup folder from the address bar. Open PowerManager and go to File > Menu Editor > Company. Click ""+"" to insert new record for the backup. Enter a Company Name for the backup and paste the path you copied to the Data Location field. Click on the checkmark to save and then Close. Select Users from Menu Editor and click Copy. Choose the actual company file as the Source Company and select the backup company as the Destination Company. Select the desired user (the one who needs access to the backup company) for both the source and destination, then click Copy. Close. You can follow the same steps outlined in the previous response to complete the Year End Close process. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Pasting from Outlook: Good morning Shelly, This is correct! When the converter tool is processing the gWorks trial balances for each month, that’s exactly what it’s doing. You’re also correct about the addition or subtraction. If the number is positive, it should be entered as a debit. If it’s negative, it should be entered as a credit. Emelia, I sat down with it today and I think that I figured it out. There are 3 columns YTD BAL OCT YTD BAL NOV YTD Diff I entered the ‘YTD BAL NOV’ column numbers. So, for example, if 100-01-1000 is a “D” (debit balance) account, I entered the positive number (IE: 25.00) as a debit and if it was a negative (IE: -25.00) I entered as a credit for that account. I went back and looked at how the data conversion numbers were entered from the trial balance and I think that I am correct. Susan Thomas] When hours are showing incorrectly in the Payroll Journal report, this is typically caused by duplicate or orphaned hour entries that aren't properly associated with earnings. Resolution Steps: Check for Non-Updated Entries in Void Paychecks Navigate to Void Paychecks Look for entries where ""updated and posted to GL"" columns are false If these entries are not the top line, delete them Check for Orphaned Hour Entries Use table grid to review PREmplPayHrs table Look for posted hours not associated with an earning Delete these orphaned records Reprocess the Payroll Go back to Post Hours and re-enter or verify the correct hours Calculate the amounts again Review the Journal report to verify hours are now displaying correctly If you continue to experience issues after following these steps, please provide more details about what specific hours are showing incorrectly so we can further assist you.

Chris Holloway] Previous tickets: 98549, 98628. Here are the scripts for this process from previous years. We need to make sure that we do not reset the times penalized between each billing cycle. /**********Confirm the rates first*********************/ -- run before refunding the deposit interest update ub_deposit_def set compute_interest = 1 -- run to set the interest for all deposit typesUPDATE dbo.ub_deposit_def SET interest_rate = 0.22 /************************************************************************************************************************ ************************************************************************************************************************ ********** Refund deposits through impresa************************************************************************** ************************************************************************************************************************/ -- run after the deposit refunds have been processed. -- run to set the interest back to 0 for all deposit types, after the deposit interest is refunded. UPDATE dbo.ub_deposit_def SET interest_rate = 0 set compute_interest = 0 select * from ub_deposit_def DELETE FROM sa_user_spid WHERE spid = @@spidINSERT INTO sa_user_spid ( user_id, spid ) SELECT 1, @@spid update ub_customer set times_penalized = 0 select * from ub_customerwhere times_penalized 0 The attached contains 3 different ways to accomplish this. We have seen this error on the Verify Apply process, but makes sense that you may get it when trying to estimate as well.

Lori Rezny] I tried to apply deposit to the customer balance and instead of subtracting it from the account it added an additional $250 to the account. I need that fixed and instructions on how to apply the deposit to the customer account.

Sreya Johny] Self Note: First, reverse the incorrect transaction that added $250 to the customer's account by creating a charge adjustment for -$250 to bring the account back to its original balance. To properly apply the deposit to the customer's account, go to Processing > Deposit in the Utility Billing module. Select the customer account that needs the deposit applied. Use the ""Calculate"" function to determine the deposit amount to be applied or refunded. Finalize the deposit transaction by clicking ""Finalize"" which will properly credit the customer's account. Sreya Johny] Hi Shea, To interface Payroll with the General Ledger in PowerManager, please follow the steps below: 1. Open Payroll Setup Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Setup, then navigate to the GL Accounts tab. 2. Add Payroll GL Accounts Enter all General Ledger accounts that will be used in Payroll, such as: Payroll Cash Payroll Liability accounts (taxes and deductions) Payroll Expense accounts Note: If an account is not listed here, it will not appear as an option in the lookup sections. 3. Enable GL Integration In the GL Accounts tab, make sure “Use Actual GL Account Numbers” is checked so Payroll can interface with the General Ledger. 4. (Optional) Use Fill GL Account If you have multiple funds or departments: Enter the GL account for one fund Click Fill GL Account to automatically populate the same account for the other funds. 5. Set Up Cash Lookup Go to the Cash Lookup tab and select the GL account used for payroll net pay for each fund or department. This account represents the cash account payroll payments come from. 6. Set Up Liability Lookup In the Liability Lookup tab, assign the appropriate GL liability accounts for employee deductions and tax withholdings (e.g., Federal Tax, Social Security, Medicare, etc.). These accounts will track amounts withheld from employee paychecks. 7. Set Up Employer Expense Lookup In the Employer Expense Lookup tab, select the GL expense accounts used for employer payroll expenses such as employer Social Security, Medicare, retirement, or benefits. Note: Employers do not contribute to Federal or State Income Tax, so those accounts are not required here. Once these are configured and saved, Payroll will properly post transactions to the General Ledger. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Tony Williams] Sherna, We apologize that no one has responded to your inquiry about batch payments in Impresa. When processing payments made by customers on a weekend (January 31) and pulling those payments on the following business day (February 2), the payment date is determined by what you enter in the ""Batch payment date"" field during processing. By default, the system will use the current processing date (February 2), but you have the option to change this to the actual payment date (January 31). Any reports you generate will show the date you entered in this field. If you want the payments to be summarized with January payments, you should enter January 31 as the batch payment date when processing. If you enter February 2, they will be summarized with February payments. The payment date field is primarily for reporting purposes, so it's important to use the date that aligns with your accounting requirements.

Sacha Pizzimenti] Hello, We have a custom for applying sanitation charges. When we apply charges at the end of the month, we apply sanitation charges for the entire month. If a customer starts, for example, on the 20th of January, the charges we apply on January 27 would be for sanitation charges only (we apply the sanitation charge at the end of the month for that entire month of service). We manually go back and change the WATER and SEWER service types pro-rate status to NEW and leave SANITATION as CALCULATED. Tony Williams] Chris, To prevent penalties from being assessed on credit balances when returning deposits to customer accounts, you'll need to adjust your Penalties & Fines setup in PowerManager. Here's how to fix this issue: From the PowerManager main menu, go to Utility Billing > Processing > Penalties & Fines Click the Setup button to access the penalty calculation parameters In the Penalties & Fines Setup form, go to the Options/Included Amounts tab Look for the option Exclude accounts with credit balances and check this box This setting will prevent the system from calculating penalties on any account with an overall credit balance If you need more specific control, you can also: Check the Exclude accounts with zero balance option Adjust the Minimum penalty amount to ensure small penalties aren't assessed Click Save to apply these changes For accounts that already have penalties incorrectly assessed on credits, you can remove these by: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Select the customer account Go to the Fines tab Enter a negative amount equal to the incorrect penalty Save the adjustment Additionally, the ""Transfer Credits"" utility can help prevent this issue by properly allocating credits: From the PowerManager main menu, select File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Select Transfer Credits from the menu options This will help distribute credits appropriately across service types before penalty calculations Let me know if you need any clarification or have questions about implementing these changes.

Sreya Johny] Chris, We've identified the solution to prevent penalties from being assessed on accounts with credit balances. You'll need to run the Transfer Credits process before calculating penalties each time. When you return deposits or have credits on customer accounts, these credits need to be properly applied to any amounts owed before penalty calculations occur. The Transfer Credits process handles this by: Transferring credits from one service type to another Applying credits to outstanding balances Ensuring accounts with overall credit balances aren't penalized Steps to Run the Transfer Credits Process From the PowerManager main menu, go to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Click File > Select Transfer Credits from the menu options Enter the Payment Date and Posting Date. The ""Type of Payment"" will automatically be set to ""Transfer"" Click the CR Transfer button and select all customers When prompted ""Do you want to manually save each credit transfer payment?"": Select ""Yes"" if you want to review each customer's transfers before saving Select ""No"" to have the system automatically complete all transfers If you select YES, then the payment entry form will be displayed for each customer that has both credits and arrears. Click the “Save” button to post the transfer payment and continue searching for the next customer with both credit and arrears balances owed Click the “Cancel” button to not post the transfer payment. You will then be asked if you wish to continue searching for the next customer with credits and arrears balances owed Note: Always run the Transfer Credits process just prior to calculating penalty amounts. This sequence is critical to ensure proper credit application before penalties are assessed. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Teresa Akeson] I ran bills this morning, and then did the finalize. I have 54 pages of errors; it's basically all the accounts. I do not understand what has happened. I also need this process further explained.I printed bills prior to finalizing like I have been told to do. Does this issue affect the printed bills in any way?
Ben Aplin] Joe, could you please take a look at this one.

Joe Sheehan] Issue Teresa is experiencing errors during the bill finalization process in PowerManager Utility Billing. She reports having 54 pages of errors affecting essentially all accounts after running bills and attempting to finalize them. She's concerned about whether this issue impacts the printed bills and needs clarification on the finalization process. Analysis Based on the related tickets, this appears to be a common issue where the finalization process encounters errors related to either transactions not being marked as billed or meter readings not being marked as billed. The high volume of errors (54 pages) suggests a systematic issue rather than individual account problems. Proposed Solution The most likely cause is that the meter readings or transactions haven't been properly marked as billed in the system. This typically happens when bills are printed but the system doesn't register them as printed, or when there's an interruption in the finalization process (like in ticket #105775 where a power outage occurred). Steps to Complete 1. First, verify if the bills were actually printed correctly before attempting to finalize. Teresa mentioned she did print the bills before finalizing, which is the correct procedure. 2. Check the specific error messages in the 54 pages. Based on similar tickets, these are likely ""Transaction not marked as billed"" or ""Meter readings not marked as billed"" errors. 3. For transactions not marked as billed: Manually mark these transactions as billed in the system. 4. For meter readings not marked as billed: These can be manually marked as billed through the system or via a database query if there are too many to handle individually. 5. After addressing the errors, run the finalization process again to confirm the issues are resolved. 6. Regarding Teresa's question about whether this affects printed bills - assure her that if the bills were printed correctly before the finalization attempt, they should be accurate and can still be distributed to customers. The finalization errors don't invalidate already printed bills. Key People That May Be Able to Assist Based on the related tickets, these customer support agents have experience solving similar finalization errors: Susan Thomas Mitch Dahl Mitch Mullins Additional Information I would recommend scheduling a follow-up training session with Teresa to review the finalization process. The related document ""Webinar - Understanding the finalize bills process"" could be shared with her for additional guidance. This will help prevent similar issues in future billing cycles. Referenced Documents 105653: Finalizing bills (error) 94127: From Voicemail 107697: error on finalization of bills 105775: Finalize Errors 93866: Piggott - From voicemail

Joe Sheehan] I'll help you with information on how to mark transactions as billed in the PM - Utility Billing system. Let me search our knowledge base for the specific procedure.Based on my research, I can provide instructions for marking transactions as billed in the PowerManager Utility Billing system: How to Mark Transactions as Not Billed to Billed To mark transactions that are not currently marked as billed in the PowerManager Utility Billing system: Navigate to Utility Billing > Processing > Compute Charges For the specific customers with unbilled transactions: Select the customer(s) from the customer selection form Ensure the ""Billable"" option is checked for the relevant accounts Click ""Compute"" to calculate the charges After computing charges, verify the transactions in the customer history to confirm they are now properly marked as billed For commercial accounts specifically: Check that the ""Billable"" option is selected for commercial electric accounts This is a common issue that can prevent transactions from being marked as billed For multiple customers with the same issue: Add all affected customers to the Customer List Use batch processing to compute charges for all of them together This will mark all their transactions as billed at once For inactive customers with zero usage: These can also be marked as billed through the same process Select these customers and compute charges to update their status If you need further assistance with specific accounts or have additional questions about this process, please let me know.

Hayley  Fear] We have been experiencing issues with the system using the residents' deposits to pay their balance. We have previously been told to click the ""retain deposit"" option, and yet this issue keeps occurring. We have also noticed that with some accounts that have already paid their balance, the system will show in the history tab that they owe another bill days after the bill due date.

Susan Thomas] I reached out and spoke with Hayley, and it appears the issue stems from the deposits entered from the cash register being recorded as a ""payment"" type for new move‑in customers. She said ""When moving in new residents, we select the “retain deposit” option, but the system is still applying their deposit toward their balance when they are billed. We are not sure why this is happening. We have also noticed that on some accounts where the resident already paid their balance, the system will later show—under the History tab—that they owe another bill days after the original due date. This has caused additional confusion.
Susan Thomas] Hi Haylee,Could you please attach the customer history report starting from December 2025 for customer number 2158008 to this ticket?
Bonnie Ralston] I have gone through the customers that are having this issue and attached their customer history reports. There is a total of 6. Hayley Fear

Mitch Dahl] I think you're right. When they are entering the payment received for the service deposit into Cash Register, the payment is getting applied to the UB customer account. This means they are either selecting the UB Payments Cash Register item when entering these payments or they have a Cash Register item for Deposits with the Destination of Utility Billing. Don't use the UB Payments CR item to post payments received for service deposits and/or set the Destination for the Deposits CR item to Other. They will need to void the payments in UB-Adjustments that have been recorded for service deposits.

Susan Thomas] Hi Hayley, please follow the steps below to achieve the correct outcome. Issue Identified When payments for service deposits are being entered into Cash Register, they are being applied to the Utility Billing (UB) customer account.This means one of the following is occurring: The UB Payments Cash Register item is being selected when entering these deposit payments, or The Cash Register item used for deposits has its Destination set to Utility Billing. Because of this, the deposit payments are being sent into UB as regular customer payments instead of being recorded strictly as deposits. Required Actions 1. Stop Using the UB Payments CR Item for Service Deposits Do not select the UB Payments Cash Register item when entering service deposit payments. This item is intended only for posting regular customer payments into UB. 2. Update the Deposits Cash Register Item Go to the Cash Register setup for the item used to record service deposits. Change the Destination to Other, not Utility Billing.This will prevent deposit payments from being posted to the UB customer account. 3. Void Incorrect Payments in Utility Billing Payments that were incorrectly posted to UB as a result of this setup need to be corrected. Go to UB → Adjustments. Void the payments that were incorrectly recorded for service deposits. Re‑enter the payments correctly in Cash Register using the updated CR item.

Joe Sheehan] Mikey, See below for step-by-step instructions on how to handle this. Remember: if the paycheck hasn't been finalized yet there's no need to void first run PR Data File Maintenance on the employee. Joe Issue A new employee, Jesus Martinez, has a direct deposit issue where the payment did not go through. The customer is waiting on confirmation from the employee's bank, but needs instructions on how to void the direct deposit and issue a paper check today if necessary. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and similar tickets, there are clear steps to void a direct deposit payment and issue a paper check instead. Since the payroll was likely already processed but not finalized yet (as this is happening on the last day of the month), we can use the appropriate method to handle this situation. Steps to complete If the payroll has NOT been finalized yet: Go to File > Data File Maintenance > PR Data File Maintenance Select ""Un-print Non-updated Payroll Checks"" option under File Select Jesus Martinez from the employee list Once the direct deposit has been un-printed: Go to Payroll > Processing > Print Checks Select ""Print Checks"" as the Payment Method (instead of ACH) Enter the appropriate Check Date, Pay Period Start Date, and Pay Period Ending Date Click Next or Select Employees Select only Jesus Martinez from the employee list Select the Bank Account to use, enter the Starting Check# and select the Check Form Click Next Verify the information is correct and click Print to print the paper check Confirm the check printed correctly when prompted If the payroll HAS been finalized: Go to Payroll > Processing > Void Paychecks Select Jesus Martinez Void the direct deposit payment Process the payroll again for that employee using the same date Follow the Print Checks process to issue a paper check

Sachin Yadav] Morning Tim, Here is what our DEV found for the issue that occurred, From DEV - The first guess is that if somebody reverses a payment that is still in open transactions, it may use the username of the user who currently holds the open payment. No, the code looks like it registers the reverse payment on the user doing the reverse. And there is no action that icloud user would do to make a meter change out by the IC processes. The iCloud user is an actual user. You may check to see which AD user has the name of the iCloud user. could be somebody actually logged in as the AD user associated with that name. not likely but possible. They probably should have the AD user name set to something that does not exist It is also possible that if a user lost network connection and the IC process made a payment and registered itself as that user in sa_user_spid, then the actual user reconnected.... sa_user_spid has associated the spid with iClouduser and a lot of what they do could wind up being identified as iCloudUser rather than the actual user, but most likely they should have received a ""spid change error"" and be kicked out at some point.... and you did not mention any of that. Susan Thomas] While on a separate call for another ticket with Scott, I walked him through how to create an ACH for payroll.

Susan Thomas] To process ACH (direct deposit) payments in PowerManager Payroll, follow these steps: Initial Setup Go to Payroll > Maintenance > ACH Setup Complete the following setup tabs: Miscellaneous: Select your utility bank account number and check ""Utility prenotification sent"" once verified Company/Batch Header: Enter company name, identification number (usually federal tax ID), and originating DFI identification (bank routing number) File Header: Enter financial institution information for both originating and destination banks Report: Select your preferred ACH report format Processing ACH Payroll Go to Payroll > Processing > Print Checks Select ""ACH"" as the Payment Method (instead of ""Print Checks"") Enter the Check Date, Pay Period Start Date, and Pay Period Ending Date Click ""Next"" or ""Select Employees"" to choose which employees will receive direct deposit On the next screen, select your Bank Account and enter a Starting ACH Number On the ACH form (Step 3), enter: Transmission date: When you'll submit the ACH file to your bank Effective settlement date: When funds will be transferred ACH file name: Full path and filename for the ACH file Select appropriate report options Click ""Next"" to proceed to the final verification step Review the employee list and payment amounts, ensuring the ""OK to Print"" box is checked for each employee to be paid Click ""Print"" to generate direct deposit forms After confirming the forms printed correctly, the ACH file will be created Important Notes The ACH file must be uploaded to your bank's system before the effective settlement date Direct deposit forms can be provided to employees as payment confirmation Ensure all employee banking information is correctly entered in their employee records before processing You may need to create a prenotification file first to verify bank account information before sending actual payments Contact Customer Support if you need assistance with your specific ACH setup or encounter any issues during processing."
