Ashley Karrer] I have multiple customer accounts that are the same customer but two seperate accounts for the same service address. I would really like to clean up some of these and combine them together. I have tried multiple ways i could think of and have not succeeded. If there is a way we can do this could you let me know.
Ben Aplin] Hello - we'll review your ticket and update you shortly.

Joe Sheehan] Issue Ashley from the City of Ponca is trying to combine multiple customer accounts that belong to the same customer but have separate accounts for the same service address. She has attempted several methods without success and is requesting guidance on how to properly combine these accounts. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, there are two possible solutions depending on the specific situation: 1. If the accounts are for the same service location: You can merge the accounts using the UB Data File Maintenance tool. 2. If the accounts are for different service locations: Complete merging is not recommended as it can cause confusion in billing. However, you could perform a move-out on one account and then do a move-in selecting the existing customer for the other location. Steps to Complete (for same service location) 1. Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance 2. Select File > Merge Customers 3. Select the source customer account (the one you want to merge from) 4. Select the destination customer account (the one you want to keep) 5. Complete the merge process Important Considerations 1. Accounts can only be fully merged if they are at the same service location 2. If attempting to combine accounts from different locations, be aware that the customer will receive one bill and may not be able to distinguish which charges are for which location 3. For accounts at different locations that need to be billed together, you'll need to decide if multiple meters on one account will be billed with individual rates or combined under a single rate 4. If you're in the middle of a billing cycle, you may want to wait until bills are finalized before moving meters to different locations Customer Support Agents Who Have Solved Similar Issues 1. Susan Thomas 2. Mitch Dahl 3. Mitch Mullins 4. Tyler Silvernail Referenced Documents 102968: combining two accounts 96537: change name on account 90648: Move In's 102289: Merge accounts 296 90384: Preston - How to combine multiple accounts into one.

Joe Sheehan] Ashley, I understand you're looking to combine multiple customer accounts that belong to the same customer but have separate accounts for the same service address. There are two approaches depending on your specific situation: For accounts at the same service location: Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Select File > Merge Customers Select the source customer account (the one you want to merge from) Select the destination customer account (the one you want to keep) Complete the merge process Important considerations: Accounts can only be fully merged if they are at the same service location If you're in the middle of a billing cycle, you may want to wait until bills are finalized before merging accounts The merge process cannot be undone, so I recommend backing up your data first If the accounts are for different service locations, complete merging is not recommended as it can cause confusion in billing. In that case, you could perform a move-out on one account and then do a move-in selecting the existing customer for the other location. Let me know if you need any clarification or have questions about this process. Joe

JENNIFER PELLATZ] I have questions.1. I would like to know if in Power Manager there is a way for the system to automatically change rates on the electric meters. Normally this is something that I have to manually do. Being told by another user that the system automatically changes the rate just like changing the winter/summer rates.2. I need to speak about the Demand meter electric rates and the Tantalus system. We audit our demand meters each year. I would like to talk about changing the meters back and forth between reading the demand and not reading the demand.Maybe we can talk about these issues when you call about the AR penalty issue.
Ben Aplin] Hello - we are reviewing your ticket and will get back to you shortly.

Mitch Mullins] There is not a method for automatically changing rates. Rates will need to be manually edited to update their values. You can change a meter's read number max value from 2 to 1 to change a demand meter to a non-demand meter. Change the read number max value from 1 to 2 to change a non-demand meter to a demand meter. You may also need to change between demand and non-demand rates when changing the read number max value.

Sreya Johny] Hi Jennifer, Apologies for the delayed response! 1. There isn’t a way to automatically change rates. The rates will need to be manually edited to update their values. 2. You can change a meter from demand to non-demand by updating the Read Number Max value from 2 to 1 (under UB > Maintenance > Equipment > Meter > Edit the meter that needs to be changed). To change a meter from non-demand to demand, update the value from 1 to 2. You may also need to switch between demand and non-demand rates when changing the Read Number Max value. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya
JENNIFER PELLATZ] Jennifer Pellatz Deputy Clerk/Utility Billing Clerk City of Neligh PO Box 87 Neligh NE 68756

column1: 20994893,"Error: Run setup, enter missing liability lookup values, and refigure distribution","[2026-01-28 03:58
column2: Ben Aplin] # Payroll Error: ""Run setup, enter missing liability lookup values, and refigure distribution"" ## **Issue Summary**Users receive the error message ""Run setup, enter missing liability lookup values, and refigure distribution"" when processing payroll updates or finalizing payroll periods. ## **Symptoms**- Error appears in payroll processing status window- Payroll amounts cannot be finalized or posted to GL- Error specifically occurs under ""Deduction Errors"" or ""Employer Contribution Liability Errors"" sections- Employees have active earnings distributions but payroll processing fails ## **Root Cause**The system cannot find GL account assignments for liability deductions because: 1. **Employee earnings** are distributed to specific department-fund GL accounts (e.g., `02-02-5000`, `02-03-5000`)2. **System requires matching liability lookups** with corresponding department-fund patterns (e.g., `02-02-0000`, `02-03-0000`)3. **Liability lookup entries exist** in the setup tables but have **no GL account assigned**4. **During payroll processing**, the system cannot map deduction liabilities to actual GL accounts ## **Technical Details**- **Affected Tables**: `PRGLLiab` (Employee Deduction Liability), `PRGLECLiab` (Employer Contribution Liability)- **Key Fields**: `LookupAcct` (department-fund pattern), `PRGLAcctsKey` (GL account assignment)- **Process Flow**: Employee earnings GL accounts → Extract department-fund pattern → Match to liability lookup → Assign GL account ## **Resolution Steps** ### **Step 1: Identify Specific Missing Assignments**Run this query to find **only the employees and distributions** causing the current error:```sqlSELECT DISTINCT e.IdNr, a.Name as EmployeeName, gla.FullAcct as EarningGLAccount, liab.LookupAcct, liab.PREDKey as DeductionKey, ed.Description as DeductionDescription, 'Missing GL Account Assignment' as IssueFROM PREmpl e INNER JOIN SCAddress a ON a.""Key"" = e.SCAddressKey INNER JOIN PREmplXEarn exe ON exe.PREmplKey = e.PREmplKey INNER JOIN PREmplPctDist pct ON pct.PREmplXEarnKey = exe.PREmplXEarnKey INNER JOIN PRGLAccts gla ON gla.PRGLAcctsKey = pct.PRGLAcctsKey INNER JOIN PRGLLiab liab ON LEFT(liab.LookupAcct, 6) = LEFT(gla.FullAcct, 6) INNER JOIN PRED ed ON ed.PREDKey = liab.PREDKey LEFT JOIN PRGLAccts liabGL ON liabGL.PRGLAcctsKey = liab.PRGLAcctsKeyWHERE liabGL.GLAcctKey IS NULL;``` ### **Step 1: Focus Only on Error-Referenced Distributions****IMPORTANT**: Only update the specific **LookupAcct** values and **deduction types** shown in your query results. Do not assign GL accounts to other liability lookups that are not currently being used. ### **Step 2: Access Payroll Setup**1. Go to **Payroll** → **Setup**2. Click the **Liability Lookup** tab3. Select the specific deduction type identified in Step 1 (e.g., Social Security, Medicare) ### **Step 3: Assign GL Accounts for Identified Distributions Only**1. Locate **only the LookupAcct entries** that exactly match those from Step 12. For each entry showing in your error results with a blank **GL Acct#** field: - Click in the **GL Acct#** field - Select the appropriate liability GL account number - Save the assignment3. **Do not assign GL accounts** to other LookupAcct entries that weren't in your errors ### **Step 4: Repeat for Employer Contributions (If Applicable)**1. If the error also mentions ""Employer Contribution Liability Errors"": - Click the **Employer Liability Lookup** tab - Repeat the process for **only the specific distributions** identified in Step 1 ## **Prevention**- **Before adding new employees**: Ensure their specific department-fund combinations have corresponding liability lookup GL accounts assigned- **When creating new departments/funds**: Set up liability lookups only for combinations that will be actively used- **Regular maintenance**: Run the diagnostic query to identify missing assignments for active employee distributions only ## **Related Information**- **Error Types**: This same process applies to ""Employer Contribution Liability Errors"" and ""Employee Deduction Liability"" errors- **Account Patterns**: LookupAcct uses first two components (department-fund) of earnings GL accounts plus ""-0000"" for liabilities- **Targeted Approach**: Only assign GL accounts to liability lookups that are actively being used by current employee distributions ## **Keywords**payroll, liability, lookup, GL account, distribution, deduction, employer contribution, setup, missing ---"

cust] Missed Chat RequestFirst Name:ROXANNELast Name:MEYEREmail:hoopercityclerk@hooper.ne.govQuestion:please send instructions for estimating water meter reads to the prior month's read and calculating a bill

ROXANNE MEYER] i set up estimating water meter reads to pull previous month read over. it wants to average the last 3 months instead of just pulling last months read over for billing. i refreshed all and calculated all but i am missing a step to only pull last month's read over.

Susan Thomas] I reached out to Roxanne and walked her through the steps. She mentioned that the bills have already been sent out, but she will try this process next month. If she needs assistance, she will reach out. This issue can be resolved by changing the meter reading estimation settings to use only the previous month's reading instead of a 3-month average. This is done through the Estimated Reading Setup configuration. Steps to complete Access the Estimated Reading Setup in the Utility Billing EZ module Change the ""months of history to use"" setting from the current value (likely set to 3) to 1 Click the ""Refresh"" button to apply these changes Return to meter reading entry and select ""Estimate All Readings"" again The system should now pull just the previous month's readings instead of using a 3-month average Verify the estimated readings are correct before proceeding with billing"

Tony Williams] Julie, Instead of reprinting bills, Void the Bill run (and any additional Bill runs made for this) and start over. Print Bills again. Megan Bunch] printing bills and it keeps putting them in a different service date and making everything go into the balance forward column instead of current charges area. i did have to go back and reprint them so when it is printing in the second round it wont let me chang the dates.

Tony Williams] Megan, Root Cause Based on our documentation and related tickets, this appears to be an issue with how Impresa handles bill reprinting. When bills are reprinted, the system is using the current date instead of preserving the original service date. Once a notice has been created with any type of notice, creating another new bill run for the same account will print all balances as PREVIOUS BALANCES."" This explains why the charges are appearing in the balance forward column instead of current charges. Reprinting isn't the Proposed Solution Instead of creating a new bill run when reprinting bills, Void the Bill run (and any additional Bill runs made for this) and start over. Print Bills again. This will properly maintain the service dates and ensure charges appear in the correct column. Additional Information After a bill run is created, all new transactions are marked with a flag that shows it was included in this bill run and it also puts the due dates on each transaction in the database. This is important to remember, because if you re-read an account after the bill is printed, then the new charges MUST go through another bill run (or revised bill run) to mark new transactions with the correct due date, etc.

Tricia Kuszak] When I go to enter the new hours for payroll, the old hours show up. I am wondering if I am missing a step. Please let me know so that I can get the Payroll done tomorrow.

Ben Aplin] Hi Tricia - I've updated the severity of your ticket to an S1 since you have an issue blocking the completion of a critical business process that needs to be completed very shortly. We'll be in touch shortly. Please do select an S1 when creating tickets if it something that needs to be completed immediately.

Mitch Mullins] We would want to make sure the prior pay period has been updated. This can be verified by using the void paychecks application and making note of the Updateed colum. If false then the prior pay period needs to be updated. It's also possible that the prior pay period was updated and the option to carry forward posted hours from the prior pay period is checked. If the posting date didn't get changed then the carry forward hours would look the same as the prior pay period.

Susan Thomas] Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and similar tickets, this appears to be related to the ""Use prior posted hours"" setting in the Post Hours Setup form. When this option is checked, the system uses hours from the previous pay period as default values for the current pay period. Tricia needs to uncheck this option when starting a new payroll cycle. Steps to complete Go to Payroll/Processing menu and select ""Post Hours"" On the Post Hours Setup form, make sure the ""Use prior posted hours"" checkbox is UNCHECKED Enter the correct posting date for the current payroll period Click either ""Post"" (for daily entries) or ""Easy Post"" (for posting total hours to a single day) based on your preference Select the employees to process and enter the new hours without the old hours appearing as defaults Complete the remaining payroll process steps (Calculate, Edit, Print Checks, Update)

Susan Thomas] Tricia had six employees showing ""old hours"". We checked in Void Paychecks and all of them had “updated = false,” so we updated them. After updating the employees, she tried to post the hours for January 26, but the PR fields were grayed out and she couldn’t make any edits. We then tried updating the January 16 payroll, but we ran into multiple errors. We attempted to update each employee again, but the same errors kept appearing. We also went into Maintenance, looked up the missing records, and added the ones she recognized. Mitch Dahl] There is no place on the W-2 for Gross Wages. Box 1 is the wages subject to Federal Income Tax withholding, Box 3 is the wages subject to the Social Security withholding and Box 3 is the wages subject to the Medicare withholding. These wages will differ from the Gross wages by the amount of deductions that are subtracted from gross wages prior to the calculation of the Federal, FICA and Medicare amounts. You can check which deductions are being subtracted by editing the Federal, FICA & Medicare deductions, clicking the E/D List in the lower right-hand corner and reviewing the deductions listed in the lower left-hand corner (Deductions Used To Compute This Deduction). From what I can see, the Quarterly Tax Report is listing exactly the same thing as the W-2 (because, again, there is no place on the W-2 for Gross Wages).

Susan Thomas] Explanation of W-2 Box 1 vs. Gross Wages The discrepancy between gross wages and W-2 Box 1 wages is normal and expected. Box 1 on the W-2 form shows ""Wages, tips, other compensation"" that are subject to Federal Income Tax withholding, not the employee's total gross wages. Various pre-tax deductions reduce the amount that appears in Box 1. Proposed Solution The difference of $20,582 between the gross wages and Box 1 wages represents pre-tax deductions that reduce the employee's taxable income for Federal Income Tax purposes. These could include retirement plan contributions, health insurance premiums, HSA contributions, and other pre-tax benefits. Steps to verify the W-2 information Check the Federal Income Tax deduction setup by going to Payroll > Maintenance > Employee > select the employee > go to the Deductions tab > select the Federal deduction. Click the ""E/D List"" button in the lower right-hand corner to view ""Deductions Used To Compute This Deduction"" in the lower left-hand corner. The deductions listed here are subtracted from gross wages before calculating Federal Income Tax, which explains why Box 1 shows a lower amount than gross wages. Verify that the same setup applies to FICA (Box 3) and Medicare (Box 5) deductions, as these may have different pre-tax treatments. Confirm that the W-2 Box 1 amount matches the ""Federal wages"" amount on the Quarterly Tax Report for the year, which would validate that the system is reporting consistently. Information to share with employees Box 1 on the W-2 shows taxable wages subject to Federal Income Tax, not their gross wages. The difference represents pre-tax deductions that benefit employees by reducing their taxable income. This is standard tax reporting practice and is working as designed. The previous year may have shown different amounts if the employee had different pre-tax deductions.

Tony Williams] Possible AI solution Issue Dori Huck from the City of Bridgeport needs to import new budget numbers for the 2025-2026 budget year and wants to confirm if this should be done before closing out the current fiscal year. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and related tickets, Dori should import the new budget numbers BEFORE closing out the fiscal year. This is the correct process in PowerManager's General Ledger module. The system is designed to handle next year's budget while the current year is still active. Steps to complete Go to General Ledger > Budgeting > Export/Import Next Year Click the ""Import"" button to access the import functionality Select the prepared spreadsheet file containing the 2025-2026 budget figures Complete the import process following the on-screen prompts Verify the imported budget by going to General Ledger > Budgeting > Edit Amounts and clicking the ""Next"" button to view next year's budget Additional Information The documentation confirms that PowerManager is specifically designed to accommodate setting up next year's budget before closing the current fiscal year. When the fiscal year is eventually closed, the ""Next"" budget figures will automatically become the ""Current"" budget figures for the new fiscal year. Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Dahl - Has resolved multiple tickets related to budget imports and fiscal year closing Mitch Mullins - Has experience with budget report modifications and fiscal year processes Susan Thomas - Has assisted with fiscal year closing and budget issues Joe Sheehan - Has provided detailed guidance on budget setup options Referenced Documents 99927: [EXTERNAL] Current Budget Figures not showing up on Budget Worksheet report 93533: Preston - Help with GL budget category design changes. 92568: Closing fiscal year 107059: Budget 105688: GL Numbers not showing on Budget Category Report

Proposed Solution I can provide guidance on how to electronically file W-2 forms using PowerManager's W2 Electronic File feature. This process requires specific information from the Social Security Administration and follows a structured workflow. Steps to Complete W-2 Electronic Filing Navigate to Payroll > Processing > W2 Electronic File in PowerManager Enter the PIN number obtained from the Social Security Administration Complete all required contact information fields (Name, Phone, Email, Fax) Select your preferred method of problem notification Enter the Business EFile Number if filing with the Iowa Department of Revenue (not applicable for Nebraska) Specify the filename and complete path where the file should be created Indicate if this is a resubmission (if applicable) Set the date range for the W-2 data (typically the first and last day of the previous year) Click the ""Create File"" button to generate the electronic submission file Upload the generated file to the Social Security Administration's Business Services Online (BSO) portal Additional Information Based on related tickets, you may need to test your file using AccuWage before final submission. The system will create a formatted wage file that meets SSA requirements. For Nebraska filing, you may need to check if electronic filing is available through the Nebraska Department of Revenue or if paper filing is required.

column1: 20987766,"Refunded deposit on final bill when didn't need to","[2026-01-23 04:13
column2: KATHY GONZALES] Did final bill for owner, refunded the deposit and did not need to. A renter is moving in and need to keep owner deposit on account.

Tony Williams] Kathy, For the specific account in question, if the deposit has already been refunded, you will likely need to manually add the deposit back to the account using an adjustment. Proposed Solution Based on the related documents and similar issues with our other customers, this appears to be an issue with the final bill process in Impresa and how deposits are handled. The solution involves adjusting the deposit refund settings in the system configuration to prevent automatic refunds when processing final bills, and potentially using the ""leave credits in place"" option. Steps to complete Navigate to UB Administration > Configurations > Refund/Lock/Misc Tab Check the ""Full AR"" option for deposit refunds (this will apply deposits as credits to the account rather than generating refund checks) Ensure the ""leave credits in place"" checkbox is selected in UB Administration > Payment Information Save the configuration changes Close and reopen the Customer screen for changes to take effect Additional Information During the final bill process, there is a specific step where you select options for deposit and deposit interest refund. In the future, ensure the proper option is selected at this step to either keep the deposit on the account or refund it as needed. For owner/renter situations, you'll want to ensure deposits remain with the property rather than being refunded when processing final bills for renters."

Chris Holloway] Issues Mysterious payments are appearing in Impresa financial system that cannot be traced or found in Invoice Cloud (IC) records These unaccounted payments are specifically tagged as ""IC Credit"" or ""API Payment"" in the Impresa system but have no corresponding entries in the Invoice Cloud database The unexplained payment discrepancies have been consistently occurring since November 2025, with a minimum frequency of one payment appearing each day, creating ongoing reconciliation problems The financial inconsistencies are causing accounting concerns and audit trail issues Troubleshooting Steps Taken InvoiceCloud support team was contacted and they officially confirmed the specific payment of $2523.15 did not originate from their payment processing system The development team conducted a thorough database investigation and discovered several anomalies: The database table specifically designated for importing Invoice Cloud payments was completely empty, despite having a suspiciously high identity value According to system design documentation, no legitimate Impresa process or procedure should ever completely empty this transaction table The mysterious payment entries are marked as ""API Payment"" in the system, but critically, the source field is blank, providing no trail to the originating system Upon detailed examination, these payments lack all the standard identifying markers and metadata typically associated with genuine InvoiceCloud transactions Transaction timestamps show patterns that don't align with normal payment processing schedules Next Steps for Resolution Conduct a comprehensive investigation of all SQL agent processes and scheduled jobs that might be inadvertently deleting table contents or modifying transaction records Verify with the client if any additional or alternative payment processors are being utilized besides Invoice Cloud that might explain the source of these transactions Perform a detailed chronological review of all November and December transactions to identify patterns, frequency changes, or common characteristics among the problematic entries Investigate potential system architecture issues to determine if there could be unexpected interactions between test environments and production environments Consider implementing additional logging and

Chris Holloway] KB: Impresa - Meters Financial Location or Storage Location Meters have a Financial Location and a Physical/Storage LocationMost of the time, a meter will be financially connected to the same service location that the meter is physically connected to. You can look in a few places to check the meter connections. Financial Location Go to Utility Billing > Account Maintenance, select Service Location The Connections tab is where the financial connection is seen between a meter and a service location. If the meter is connected here, it is connected financially to the service location. Utility Billing > Account Maintenance, select MeterMeter Maintenance is another place to find this information. Navigate to Meter Maintenance, click on the Transactions sub-tab, select Financial Locations then click the Green curved arrow. This will reveal the service location or locations that a meter is financially connected. This is the first place that you should look if you are having problems with a meter. You may find that a meter is attached financially to multiple service locations when it should be attached to only one. You could also find the financial location and the current physical location is not the same. Physical/Storage Location Go to Utility Billing > Account Maintenance, select Meter The Storage Location is where the meter is physically located. You can find a meter's Storage Location by looking at the Meter Maintenance screen. This information is found in the Current Physical Location field. Navigate to the Service Location screen and click on the Storage tab. This will show you what meters are physically stored at this service location. Additional Troubleshooting: A meter can sometimes not be connected financially but will be connected physically (in storage at a location). Some of the problems with this is; - You cannot scrap a meter when it is in storage at a service location that is not the warehouse. - Meters that are in storage at a service location (not the warehouse) but not financially connected, may not show on reports properly. - The reverse, meters that are financially attached to a service location but not in storage at that same can also cause reporting and possibly other issues. When a meter is financially connected but not physically (in storage) at the location, you will need to correct this on the Meter Screen. To fix this, change the Current Physical Location of the meter where it is being stored to the Service Location where it is financially attached. If a meter is physically (in storage) at a location (not the warehouse) but not financially attached, the same procedure is taken to change the current physical location to the proper location (i.e. the warehouse or the location where it is financially attached). By changing the Current Physical Location to the proper location, this will remove it from the wrong location where it is stored. In Utility Billing > Reports, Meter Reports, there is a report to check these problems. In Utility Billing > Periodic, System Integrity, there is also a report to help.

Tony Williams] Likely fix for this VIA AI. Issue Jazmine from TriCo Regional Sewer Utility is experiencing an error when trying to verify apply in Impresa. The system is showing that not all meters for a group have been read. A similar message appears when trying to close the books. This is happening during their first attempt to bill in parallels for Hamilton County Regional Sewer Utility. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and knowledge base articles, this issue occurs when there are unread meters in the reading books that need to be closed. When even one meter is marked as unread, the system will not close the reading books for any meters in the selected range. This is a common issue that can be resolved by following specific steps. Steps to complete 1. First, verify if there are unread meters: Navigate to Utility Billing > Readings > Close Reading Books Check the ""Print read meters report"" box Click the Printer icon to generate both the Read meters report and Unread meters report Review the reports to determine how many meters are read vs. unread 2. Based on the number of unread meters, choose one of these approaches: If there are only a few unread meters: Navigate to each unread meter's Service Location > Connections tab Change the book number to a book number you do not normally use This removes the unread meters from the book you're trying to close If there are many unread meters or about half are unread: Try closing one book at a time until all books in the group are closed Or contact support for assistance with a script that can help under certain conditions If there are only a few read meters: Navigate to each read meter's Service Location > Connections tab Change all these meters to the same unused book number 3. After moving the problematic meters: Attempt to close the reading books again If successful, you'll see a ""Process Complete"" message You can then move the meters back to their original books if needed Proceed with applying the readings 4. Alternative check: It's also possible that the books have already been closed for these meters If the Read meters report shows no meters, this indicates books have been closed already In this case, you can proceed directly to applying charges Key people that may be able to assist Based on the referenced tickets, these customer support agents have successfully resolved similar issues: Chris Holloway Alex Mullins Tony Williams Referenced Documents 94663: Problems with Closing Reading Books 106721: UNREAD METER ERROR 91771: Unread meters when processing bills on 5/31/24 94672: Closing Reading Books 94688: unread meter still showing up

Therese Magill] Which report do I use to pay our Utility Taxes?

Tony Williams] AI Summary Solution Issue The Village of Decatur (NE) customer is asking which report to use to pay their Utility Taxes. Proposed Solution Based on reviewing the documentation and related tickets, the customer needs to run a tax report in the Utility Billing module. There are specific reports designed for this purpose that will help them identify and pay their utility taxes properly. Steps to complete Navigate to Utility Billing > Reports > Financial menu Select the ""Taxes"" report option Enter the date range for the tax period they need to report on (typically the first day through the last day of the month) Choose either a detailed or summary report format based on their needs Preview or print the report which will show all taxable transactions for the specified period Use this report to complete their utility tax payment forms Additional Information Based on the documentation, the tax report will consider either the printed invoice date and amounts (if using accrual accounting) or the payment date and allocation amounts (if using cash accounting). The report will provide the necessary information to complete state tax forms. If the customer specifically needs to see which customers have tax applied to their accounts, they could also run the ""Customer Listing By Rate"" report which can be modified to include tax information, as was done in ticket #103187.

Susan Thomas] Hi Theresa, Please review the Billing Summary Report located under Reports → Financial → Summary. This report displays sales tax based on billed transactions—that is, it reflects the tax amounts associated with invoices or charges issued to customers. It serves as a reliable source for understanding sales tax liability from a billing perspective. Feel free to contact me if you need any additional assistance. Susan

Chris Holloway] Issues The customer (Misty DeGroat, City Clerk/Chamberlain, City of Fulton) wants the ability to send batch emails to unpaid customers on a monthly basis through Impresa. DNS/SMTP relay records need to be set up before the batch email feature can be configured. Troubleshooting The initial contact was Jim Gibbons (City of Fulton IT), but it was determined that the DNS/email setup needed to go through their email manager, Computer Outlet North. SMTP relay DNS records (CNAME entries) were provided to the customer for both .com and .gov domains. Computer Outlet North (contact: Joe, 315-342-6503) confirmed they could set up the DNS records. As of March 5, 2026, David Stoutenger from Computer Outlet North replied, indicating the DNS records had been generated for both domains. Next Steps Follow up with Computer Outlet North (Joe, 315-342-6503) to confirm DNS records have been properly configured. You were going to speak with Beacon about this since the meter is the same as all the other meters you currently have with the same meter model. If they need to speak with us, you can set up a meeting between all three of us and we can help get this worked out. Please mention to Beacon about the lead 0s as well. You may be able to add the lead 0s by deleting any blank spaces after the meter number, then typing the 0s in the front of it. It is possible that Beacon is linking this meter to a different record on their end because of the missing lead 0s."
SHERRI WAGONER] On every one of my emailed bills. I get ""bad credentials"" on all of them.

Tony Williams] Dropping in AI suggestion privately to see if it helps. Issue The customer, Sherri Wagoner from Town of Haxtun, is experiencing an issue where emailed utility bills will not send. She's receiving ""bad credentials"" errors on all of her emailed bills. Root Cause Analysis Based on similar tickets, this appears to be an email authentication issue. The most common causes include: 1. Incorrect username or password in the email configuration 2. Missing OpenSSL files in the PowerManager application folder 3. Incorrect SMTP server settings (server name or port) 4. Firewall or antivirus software blocking the connection Proposed Solution The ""bad credentials"" error specifically points to an authentication problem. We need to verify the email settings and potentially update the authentication method or credentials. Steps to Complete Verify the OpenSSL files are correctly placed in the PowerManager application folder. These should be named exactly ""ssleay32.dll"" and ""libeay32.dll"". Check the SMTP server settings to ensure they're correct (server name and port number). Generate a new app password for the email account if using Gmail or another service that supports app passwords. Temporarily disable any firewall or antivirus software to test if they're blocking the connection. If the above steps don't resolve the issue, consider using a third-party SMTP service like SMTP2Go.com as an alternative solution. Customer Communication I'll call the customer to walk through these steps, focusing first on verifying the OpenSSL files and email credentials since these are the most common causes of this particular error. Customer Support Agents Who Have Solved Similar Issues Joe Sheehan - Resolved multiple email authentication issues including OpenSSL file placement Mitch Mullins - Experienced with email configuration problems and third-party SMTP services Tony Williams - Assisted with email delivery troubleshooting Susan Thomas - Helped with Gmail app password creation Referenced Documents 106865: Email statements 90598: Piggott - Help with UB email customer bill error. 94707: Spalding - Error when emailing customer bills 108666: Error when Posting Payments 102253: Emailing utility bills issue

Susan Thomas] I reached out to Sherry and connected remotely. She mentioned that her Gmail password was recently changed, and since then she has been receiving a password error when trying to send email bills. We generated a new Gmail app password and updated it in her billing system. After applying the new app password, Sherry was able to successfully send several test email bills. She plans to send additional bills, and if she encounters any further issues, she will update the ticket. For now, I will keep the ticket open pending her confirmation.
SHERRI WAGONER] All emails went out successfully!

Sreya Johny] Montana, Mitch reviewed this and mentioned that the final bills for those inactive accounts may not have been printed, which could be why bills continue to generate for them. He recommended printing the bills once for those inactive accounts to prevent them from generating again in the future. Please go to UB > Processing > Print Bills > Filter the list to inactive accounts by setting the Starting and Ending Status range to 2 > Select all > Print the bills or export them as a PDF. Those inactive accounts will still reflect the balance shown on the generated bills. After reviewing with your auditor, you may need to process a negative adjustment for those amounts charged using 11/24/2025 as the Adjustment date (the date when the charges were applied to the account), if appropriate. He also advised selecting All Customers whenever you run the Print Bills process. The system should then generate bills only for active accounts going forward. DIANE HALL] I cannot get into Utility billing/reports/click on customer – error message is: Could not compile program/variable1onCalc

Tony Williams] Dropping in AI suggestion to see if it's accurate. Issue Diane from Chester Municipal Electric is unable to access the customer base in Utility Billing. When attempting to access Reports and clicking on customer, she receives an error message: ""Could not compile program/variable1onCalc"". Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and documentation, this appears to be a compilation error related to the User Defined Reports module or a process that's still running in the background. The error ""Could not compile program/variable1onCalc"" suggests there's an issue with a calculation variable in one of the reports. This is similar to issues seen in tickets #104126 and #105810 where users encountered errors accessing reports after updates or when processes were still running. Steps to complete Ask Diane to close PowerManager completely if it's still open. Use Task Manager to end any PowerManager processes that might still be running in the background (PM process). Have all users log out of the system temporarily if possible. Restart the PowerManager application. If the issue persists, check if the User Defined Reports module is properly registered in the system. If needed, copy over the register number for the User Defined Reports as was done in ticket #105810. Alternative Solutions If the above steps don't resolve the issue, it could be related to: A corrupted report definition in the customer module A connectivity issue between Diane's workstation and the main PowerManager server User permissions that need to be updated for accessing customer reports In these cases, further troubleshooting may be required, potentially including reinstalling or updating the report components. Customer Support Agents Who Have Solved Similar Issues Susan Thomas - Resolved similar errors with PM processes and report access issues Mitch Mullins - Handled connection issues between workstations and the PowerManager main computer Mitch Dahl - Addressed User Defined Reports registration issues Joe Sheehan - Fixed User Defined Reports access problems Referenced Documents 105771: Manual Meter Reading 94879: Central City - Can't get into PowerManager 98459: Bill did not print for a few customers, How can i check if there were any others 104126: Invalid program path error 105810: User Defined Reports error post update

Tony Williams] Alicia, There is an answer to why you're seeing two water meters on the bill after a meter change out. The issue you're experiencing with the usage amounts is a known problem that can occur during meter change outs in Impresa. To fix the incorrect usage calculation where both meters are showing 2050 instead of splitting correctly (1870 for first meter and 180 for second), you'll need to reset everything back to before the meter change out and then redo the process: Set the top line of the old meter's edit readings tab to match the line below it (same reading, usage, and reading date) Set the top line of the new meter's edit readings tab to match the line below it (same reading, usage, and reading date) Remove the new meter from the service location and reattach the old meter (don't use the meter change out process for this step) Once everything is back to how it was before the change out, redo the meter change out process with the correct final reading This should correct the usage calculation so that each meter shows its proper usage amount rather than both showing the total.

Ben Aplin] There is no process. Would you like us to reach out to sales to engage for a quote to perform this?
Lori Rezny] We do not need to pay sales to do that. I feel like there should be a program written to accomplish this.
Ben Aplin] Apologies, you are correct. Sreya will update.

Sreya Johny] Hi Lori, You can mark the old vendor accounts as Temporary Vendor in the AP – Vendors screen. During the Year End Close process, those temporary vendor accounts can be deleted. This can only be done as part of the Year End process. To delete old utility billing accounts, go to UB > Processing > Year End Close > Select a Close thru date (Enter the date of the most recent transaction(s) to be consolidated. The default is December 31 three years prior to the current year) and check ""Delete inactive customers"" (When this option is selected, you are presented with a form containing a list of customers that may be deleted after the consolidation. You may then select which of the customers you wish to delete) > Click Year End and select those inactive accounts that needs to be deleted. This process will consolidate all individual Utility Billing transactions up to a point in time into a single beginning balance transaction for selected customer accounts. It also provides the option of deleting inactive accounts that end up with no transactions after the consolidation. Customers checked as written off will not be deleted. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Karolyn, I merged the new bank reconciliation ticket you created with this open one. If you're having difficult with where to start, I'd recommend starting reconciliation with the month prior to you switching to PowerManager. Start with the simplest bank account, like a CD or money market account. Your beginning balance should start with 0.00. You'll see many line items with the description ""Data conversion"", these are the transactions from your previous GL months. They should be checked. Remember that any outstanding AP invoices at the time of the switch should've be entered into PowerManager. When you enter the invoice, DEBIT the cash account so when you print the check, the offsetting transaction is a net zero. This will give you the outstanding check on the withdrawal side that you leave as uncleared in bank rec until it's been cleared. The bank statement reconciliation webinar in the Education Portal is very helpful for understanding how the application works. If you'd like, we can schedule some time to look at this together. Susan Thomas] Hi Tricia, Please follow the steps listed below. If you need any further assistance, please let us know. Susan Steps to complete Access Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts Click Edit to view customer information Navigate to the Meter tab to view meter information for each account Check the status field for each meter - a value of ""0"" indicates an active meter To generate a report of all meters and their statuses: Go to Utility Billing > Reports > User Defined Select 'Meter Listing' report Click OK to preview, then print or export as needed Alternatively, create a custom export: Go to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts Click Edit > Filter Select 'Status' from Fields column Enter range 0 to 0 for active customers only Click OK Right-click on any column to select desired columns Export and save the data For meters that should be inactive, you can update their status on the Meter tab or remove them from the Billing Meters section

Shaena Robertson] Kick Off Meeting notes 2/4 @ 12pm ET/11am CT Project Overview and Stakeholder Introductions: Shaena, Stephen, Danita, Tracy, Nathan, and other stakeholders introduced themselves and outlined three major projects for Woodville: migrating Impresa software to the cloud, a mass meter change-out to Neptune meters, and implementation of Neptune 360 AMI system. Participant Introductions: Shaena Robertson (Director of Professional Services at Enhance), Nathan Loan (technical lead), Danita Skinner (City Secretary), Tracy Johnston (Utility Billing and Accounts Payable), Stephen (City Administrator), Kuhn (Public Works Director), Mike Taverness (City Council), and Andrew Lawrence (IT) introduced themselves and described their roles in the upcoming projects. Project Scope: Shaena outlined three main projects: migrating Impresa software to the cloud, a mass meter change-out from Census to Neptune meters, and implementing Neptune 360 AMI for advanced metering infrastructure, confirming the city’s transition from Census to Neptune and the use of AMI for hourly readings. Stakeholder Roles: Shaena and Nathan will serve as main contacts and project managers from Enhance, while Stephen’s team will oversee the meter project, and Danita and Tracy will coordinate utility billing and accounts payable aspects. Project Sequencing and Timelines: The group, led by Shaena, Stephen, and Danita, confirmed that the Impresa cloud migration must occur before the mass meter change-out, with a target go-live date for the cloud project at the beginning of March, and discussed coordination with third-party vendors. Project Dependencies: Danita and Stephen clarified that the mass meter change-out cannot begin until the Impresa upgrade to the cloud is complete, and the third-party vendor (Core and Main) is waiting for this migration before starting their work. Timeline Coordination: The team agreed on a tentative timeline aiming for a cloud migration go-live at the beginning of March, with Shaena responsible for creating and sharing a detailed project timeline and updating all stakeholders via the hosted ticket system. Vendor Coordination: Shaena requested contact information for third-party vendors and discussed the need for a pre-construction meeting with Core and Main, while also offering to provide standard data file layouts to facilitate the meter change-out process. Kickoff and Pre-Construction Meetings: Danita and Stephen noted that Core and Main wants a pre-construction meeting, and Shaena and Nathan clarified their involvement would focus on data requirements and integration rather than construction logistics. Impresa Cloud Migration Process: Shaena and Nathan detailed the technical steps for migrating Impresa to the cloud, including server setup, database backup, version upgrade, user training, and user acceptance testing, with Andrew supporting IT implementation. Technical Migration Steps: Shaena explained that Enhance will set up two cloud servers (one for SQL/database, one for the client), back up the on-premise database, upgrade from 32-bit to 64-bit (version 4.28.2.0), and use Parallels for remote access. User Training and Support: Up to six hours of remote training will be provided in two sessions for four to five users, focusing on new features and changes, with additional hours available if needed at no extra cost. User Acceptance Testing (UAT): After migration, users will perform UAT using a provided checklist to verify all functions, with a typical testing period of two weeks to a month, and any issues will be addressed before go-live. Go-Live and Support Procedures: Shaena described the go-live process, including shutting down Invoice Cloud, final database backup, and switching users to the cloud environment, with Enhance providing support during and after the transition. Active Directory and User Management: Enhance will manage user accounts and security via Active Directory on the cloud server, requiring users to contact Enhance for password resets and user changes. Integration and Data Handling: Nathan and Shaena discussed integration points, including Invoice Cloud, meter reading file transfers, receipt printing, and user licensing, ensuring all technical requirements and limitations are addressed for the cloud environment. Invoice Cloud Integration: The team confirmed Invoice Cloud integration will continue, with no changes to payment processing or batch imports, and discussed the possibility of using a credit card swiper or point-of-sale integration, pending confirmation from Invoice Cloud. Meter Reading File Transfers: Current meter reading files are manually transferred via FTP (FileZilla), and Nathan explained that this process will remain largely unchanged, with minor adjustments for the cloud environment. Receipt Printing Requirements: Receipt printers (thermal) will require the Parallels client for proper operation in the cloud, and users must use the downloaded client rather than the web client for receipt printing. User Licensing and Access: The contract allows for five concurrent Impresa users; each workstation (e.g., window and desk) must have a unique username, and generic logins (e.g., 'drive through') are permitted for shared workstations. Financial System Transition: Danita and Shaena confirmed that the city will continue using the IUS (Enhance) financial module locally until transitioning to Tyler Technologies ERP 10 in October, with manual data transfer required during the interim. Manual Data Transfer: After the Impresa cloud migration, utility billing will no longer automatically update the local financial system; Danita will print daily reports and manually load coding information until the new ERP system is live. Future ERP Integration: The city has contracted with Tyler Technologies for ERP 10, targeting an October 1st go-live, and will consider integration needs between Impresa and the new ERP system. Follow-up tasks: Mass Meter Change Out Project Contacts: Provide contact information for the Core and Main team and any other relevant third-party contacts involved in the mass meter change out project to Enhance for documentation and coordination purposes. (Danita, Stephen) Kickoff Meeting Participation: Include Enhance in the kickoff and pre-construction meetings for the mass meter change out project to discuss data file requirements and project coordination. Shaena Robertson] Kick Off Meeting notes 2/4 @ 12pm ET/11am CT Project Overview and Stakeholder Introductions: Shaena, Stephen, Danita, Tracy, Nathan, and other stakeholders introduced themselves and outlined three major projects for Woodville: migrating Impresa software to the cloud, a mass meter change-out to Neptune meters, and implementation of Neptune 360 AMI system. Participant Introductions: Shaena Robertson (Director of Professional Services at Enhance), Nathan Loan (technical lead), Danita Skinner (City Secretary), Tracy Johnston (Utility Billing and Accounts Payable), Stephen (City Administrator), Kuhn (Public Works Director), Mike Taverness (City Council), and Andrew Lawrence (IT) introduced themselves and described their roles in the upcoming projects. Project Scope: Shaena outlined three main projects: migrating Impresa software to the cloud, a mass meter change-out from Census to Neptune meters, and implementing Neptune 360 AMI for advanced metering infrastructure, confirming the city’s transition from Census to Neptune and the use of AMI for hourly readings. Stakeholder Roles: Shaena and Nathan will serve as main contacts and project managers from Enhance, while Stephen’s team will oversee the meter project, and Danita and Tracy will coordinate utility billing and accounts payable aspects. Project Sequencing and Timelines: The group, led by Shaena, Stephen, and Danita, confirmed that the Impresa cloud migration must occur before the mass meter change-out, with a target go-live date for the cloud project at the beginning of March, and discussed coordination with third-party vendors. Project Dependencies: Danita and Stephen clarified that the mass meter change-out cannot begin until the Impresa upgrade to the cloud is complete, and the third-party vendor (Core and Main) is waiting for this migration before starting their work. Timeline Coordination: The team agreed on a tentative timeline aiming for a cloud migration go-live at the beginning of March, with Shaena responsible for creating and sharing a detailed project timeline and updating all stakeholders via the hosted ticket system. Vendor Coordination: Shaena requested contact information for third-party vendors and discussed the need for a pre-construction meeting with Core and Main, while also offering to provide standard data file layouts to facilitate the meter change-out process. Kickoff and Pre-Construction Meetings: Danita and Stephen noted that Core and Main wants a pre-construction meeting, and Shaena and Nathan clarified their involvement would focus on data requirements and integration rather than construction logistics. Impresa Cloud Migration Process: Shaena and Nathan detailed the technical steps for migrating Impresa to the cloud, including server setup, database backup, version upgrade, user training, and user acceptance testing, with Andrew supporting IT implementation. Technical Migration Steps: Shaena explained that Enhance will set up two cloud servers (one for SQL/database, one for the client), back up the on-premise database, upgrade from 32-bit to 64-bit (version 4.28.2.0), and use Parallels for remote access. User Training and Support: Up to six hours of remote training will be provided in two sessions for four to five users, focusing on new features and changes, with additional hours available if needed at no extra cost. User Acceptance Testing (UAT): After migration, users will perform UAT using a provided checklist to verify all functions, with a typical testing period of two weeks to a month, and any issues will be addressed before go-live. Go-Live and Support Procedures: Shaena described the go-live process, including shutting down Invoice Cloud, final database backup, and switching users to the cloud environment, with Enhance providing support during and after the transition. Active Directory and User Management: Enhance will manage user accounts and security via Active Directory on the cloud server, requiring users to contact Enhance for password resets and user changes. Integration and Data Handling: Nathan and Shaena discussed integration points, including Invoice Cloud, meter reading file transfers, receipt printing, and user licensing, ensuring all technical requirements and limitations are addressed for the cloud environment. Invoice Cloud Integration: The team confirmed Invoice Cloud integration will continue, with no changes to payment processing or batch imports, and discussed the possibility of using a credit card swiper or point-of-sale integration, pending confirmation from Invoice Cloud. Meter Reading File Transfers: Current meter reading files are manually transferred via FTP (FileZilla), and Nathan explained that this process will remain largely unchanged, with minor adjustments for the cloud environment. Receipt Printing Requirements: Receipt printers (thermal) will require the Parallels client for proper operation in the cloud, and users must use the downloaded client rather than the web client for receipt printing. User Licensing and Access: The contract allows for five concurrent Impresa users; each workstation (e.g., window and desk) must have a unique username, and generic logins (e.g., 'drive through') are permitted for shared workstations. Financial System Transition: Danita and Shaena confirmed that the city will continue using the IUS (Enhance) financial module locally until transitioning to Tyler Technologies ERP 10 in October, with manual data transfer required during the interim. Manual Data Transfer: After the Impresa cloud migration, utility billing will no longer automatically update the local financial system; Danita will print daily reports and manually load coding information until the new ERP system is live. Future ERP Integration: The city has contracted with Tyler Technologies for ERP 10, targeting an October 1st go-live, and will consider integration needs between Impresa and the new ERP system. Follow-up tasks: Mass Meter Change Out Project Contacts: Provide contact information for the Core and Main team and any other relevant third-party contacts involved in the mass meter change out project to Enhance for documentation and coordination purposes. (Danita, Stephen) Kickoff Meeting Participation: Include Enhance in the kickoff and pre-construction meetings for the mass meter change out project to discuss data file requirements and project coordination. (Danita) Neptune 360 Software Contact Clarification: Obtain and confirm the appropriate contact person for the Neptune 360 software side (separate from Core and Main if applicable) and communicate this information to Enhance. (Danita) Usernames for Cloud Migration: Submit a list of first and last names for all users (including any generic logins such as ""drive through"" or ""clerk window"") who will require access to Impresa in the cloud environment via ticket 96071. (Danita) Ticket Access and User Updates: Provide names and email addresses for Danita and Stephen to be added to the hosted ticket (96071) and request removal of Mandy (former city administrator) from the ticket. (Danita)

Upon The City of Woodville acceptance promote changes to live production database Assumptions: All project activities to be performed remotely. The City of Woodville will provide the appropriate remote access to its network, facilities, and systems as may be required to perform activities from one of inHANCE’s locations. inHANCE shall abide by all rules and directions of The City of Woodville when accessing The City’s network, facilities or systems. Upon deployment of the custom modification into Impresa Test environment, The City of Woodville will have ten (10) business days for acceptance testing, during which time, issues reported within the 10 days will be resolved at no charge provided they are part of the original scope of work. At that time the custom modification will be promoted to the Live environment on agreement from The City of Woodville. All changes or issues reported after 10 days will be considered billable, unless a prior arrangement or extension to the acceptance period is made. If a project delay is encountered due to external factors outside of inHANCE’s control, and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) needs to extend beyond the 10 day period for remediation, inHANCE and The City of Woodville will analyze the results of this external factor on the project timeline to determine if there is a need for a change order to reflect an impact on the project cost or schedule. The City of Woodville resources will be available as required for UAT. It is expected that the resources will have experience in the day-to-day operations and will work closely with inHANCE to resolve issues as required for quick and consistent response to any issues. column1: 20982567,"Bill Cards printing WA incorrectly on bills","[2026-01-21 03:23
column2: Michelle Roach] Bill cards are printing 2 WA codes for WA and 2 for SE. Customers will not like this.

Ben Aplin] If the rates are set up so that there are two components on the rate, then they will print on the default bill print that way. Having said that, you can change rate setup. nder rate maintenance: when you edit the rate, there is the ""add to"" number column, you could add the base charge to the usage charge (assuming these are the components) and then it will only print the usage charge. Please note: you can find the below if you hit F1.

Sreya Johny] Since the ""UTILITY BILL"" value is hard-coded in the batch header record creation process, a formal programming/development change is required to allow users to enter a custom Company Entry Description in the ACH File Setup form under the Company/Batch Header page. In the interim, Brandi can manually edit the ACH file using a text editor such as Notepad, replacing ""UTILITY BILL"" with ""PAYMENT "" (with 3 trailing spaces) in both 520 records. It is critical that the replacement text occupies the exact same number of characters as the original text to preserve the fixed-width NACHA file format — characters must be overwritten, not deleted and retyped in a way that shifts column positions. Steps to Complete As an immediate workaround, open the generated ACH file in Notepad (or another plain text editor). Locate both 520 batch header records (lines 2 and 59 in the file). In each record, find the text ""UTILITY BILL"" in the Company Entry Description field (columns 54-63). Overwrite ""UTILITY BILL"" with ""PAYMENT "" — ensuring exactly 3 trailing spaces are included to maintain the correct 10-character field width. Do NOT simply delete the old text, as this will shift all subsequent column positions and corrupt the file format. Save the file and submit it to the bank. For a permanent fix, the development team needs to implement a programming change that adds a configurable ""Company Entry Description"" field to the ACH File Setup form under the Company/Batch Header page, so users can customize this value without manual file editing. Once the programming change is deployed, navigate to the ACH File Setup form, go to the Company/Batch Header tab, and update the Company Entry Description field to ""PAYMENT "" (with 3 trailing spaces).

column1: 20981215,"Meter Reading Interface Project: File Format: Reading Number for KWH-Received (Solar) and Water (newly introduced during todays' call)","[2026-01-20 07:38
column2: Ben Aplin] Tesco needs to know the reading number to use for (a) KHW-Rec and (b) Water. Context: Email sent to Springfield October 1, 2025: Meter Reading: 1. ‘Meter Key’ is Meter Number (Badge Number) correct? Meter Key is the value of the “Key” field for that meter in the PowerManager meter table. This is a unique value for each meter. It basically identifies the record of the meter in the table. 2. What is ‘Reading Number’? Reading Number is used for meters that have multiple readings, such as demand meters where there is a kwh reading and a kw reading. Reading #1 is the kwh reading and reading #2 is the kw reading. 3. We have multiple registers (KWH-Del, KHW-Rec and KW) are these going to be on separate lines? How do we differentiate between them, is that what ‘Reading Number’ is for? Yes, that is basically the purpose of the Reading Number.

Michelle Nelson] This is what our IT said:Microsoft's security defaults are pretty strict. They phased out an old Authentication method for the type of emailing that is used by PowerManager. We can allow this to work, only if we add what's called ""Entra ID P1"" to the Microsoft Tenant first. This is an add-on license that costs $72/yr. The Microsoft Tenant is your company account for everyone set up under the Village.After that license is purchased and applied, it will allow us to set up policies that will replace the security defaults setting that is currently in place.These policies are basically turned off automatically once I turn off the security defaults option. We then have to manually create them so that your accounts stay protected.Microsoft is retiring this older, less secure way that apps sign in to send email. Your billing software uses that old method. We can safely keep it working for now by adding a tightly controlled exception, but Microsoft has already said this method will be removed completely by 2027. At that point, this software won't be able to send email through Microsoft 365 unless the vendor updates it or we move you to a different solution.

Susan Thomas] Proposed Solution There are two possible approaches depending on whether the Entra ID P1 license will be purchased. Option 1: Proceed With Entra ID P1 License If the Entra ID P1 license is obtained, an app password can be created for Microsoft 365 to support PowerManager’s email integration. Steps to Complete Determine if the Entra ID P1 license ($72/year) will be added, as recommended by the IT department. If the license is in place: Work with the IT department to: Disable Security Defaults. Implement the necessary security policies. Create an app password specifically for PowerManager. Update the email settings in PowerManager using: Server name: smtp.office365.com Username: the new @ansleyne.com email address Password: the newly created app password From email: the @ansleyne.com address Port: 587 (or as recommended by IT) Test email billing to confirm that the configuration is working. Option 2: Do Not Purchase Entra ID P1 License If the license will not be purchased at this time: Continue using the existing email configuration that is currently functioning with the older email address. This serves as a temporary solution until a future migration plan is established.

Colleen Thompson] I submitted a ticket to Invoice Cloud as one of customers was attempting to pay a final bill. Below is how they responded to the request. Additional account numbers that have had the same issue are: 120751, 120753, or 120755 120812. In InvoiceCloud, customer accounts and their associated invoices are created and updated entirely based on the data included in the invoice file your team sends to us. The system does not independently create, modify, or infer accounts or bills outside of what is provided in that file. If a customer is unable to view their final bill online, it typically indicates one of the following: The account number was not included in the most recent invoice file. The final bill for that account was not present in the file. The account number in the file differs from what the customer is using to search (e.g., Account Number vs. Display Account Number). Since all account visibility and bill retrieval are driven by the invoice file content, I recommend confirming that: Account 120812 is included in the latest invoice file. The final bill for this account is present and associated with the correct account number. The account number provided in the file matches what the customer is using to search in the portal. Once the correct account number and invoice data are included in the invoice file, the customer should be able to view their final bill online without issue.

Tantalus Dashboard Integration Problem: - Meters being changed out and moved to inactive accounts in Power Manager are not updating as inactive on the Tantalus dashboard - Potential system integration issue between PowerManager and Tantalus AMI system 2. New Service Location Setup: - Customer needs guidance on adding a new home's address to PowerManager - Requires step-by-step instructions for creating a new service location - Needs help with entering parcel number, street, city, state, zip, and setting up billing parameters Recommended next steps: - Investigate the Tantalus-PowerManager integration - Provide detailed walkthrough for new service location setup - Confirm all necessary information is available for account creation Message History Looking at our Tantalus dashboard, it looks like the meters we are changing out and moving to the inactive account on Power Manager are not updating as inactive on Tantalus. Hi there! I'm PowMan, your PowerManager Operator. I'm here to help you with any questions you have about using the PowerManager applications for your daily tasks. What can I help you with today? Whether it's finding an account, processing a payment, or generating a report, just ask! Let me look into this Tantalus dashboard and meter status issue for you. Based on my search, I don't have specific information about Tantalus integration with PowerManager and how meter status updates between the two systems. This appears to be an integration issue between PowerManager and your Tantalus AMI system. To help you better, I need to understand more about your process. When you move meters to the inactive account in PowerManager, what steps are you following? Are you using the meter change-out process or another method? have a new home that was just built in in our village. I do not know how to add the address into my power manager. So they can get bills for their utilities Let me find information about adding new addresses in PowerManager. I need to search for more specific information about adding new addresses. I need more specific information about creating new service locations in PowerManager. Based on what I've found, I can guide you through adding a new service location for the newly built home in your village. From the information I found in the knowledge base, here's how to create a new service location in PowerManager: 1. Go to **Utility Billing** > **Account Maintenance** > **Service Location** 2. On the General tab, click on the green plus at the top in the **Navigation** pane 3. Enter the parcel number in the **Parcel number** field 4. In the **Building** box, enter the building number 5. In the **Street** field, enter the street name or select it from the dropdown - If the street doesn't exist, click the golden wrench next to the **Street** field - In the **Code Maintenance** dialog, click the gold plus and enter the street name - Click **OK** 6. Enter the **City**, **State**, and **Zip** 7. Click **Save** Then you'll need to set up the billing parameters: 1. Click on the **Connections** tab 2. Activate the appropriate **Group** for this location 3. Set the book/route and sequence for this location 4. Set the **Prorate status** to **New** 5. Enter the **Start date** for service 6. Check the appropriate **Income Centers** this location needs to be charged for Is this a completely new service location, or are you trying to set up service for a new customer at an existing location? Also, do you have all the necessary information like parcel number, address details, and billing parameters ready?

column1: 20978568,"Incorrect Payment Method for UB Payment - Correction Process","[2026-01-19 06:49
column2: Ben Aplin] Payment Method Correction in Utility Billing A user needed guidance on correcting an accidentally entered wrong payment method for a UB (Utility Billing) payment. The resolution involves a step-by-step process to reverse the incorrect payment and re-enter it with the correct payment method. Key steps include navigating to Account Maintenance, using the ""Reverse"" button, selecting the specific payment, and then re-entering the payment with the correct method. Message History I accidently entered the wrong payment method for a costumer for UB Payment. How do I fix it? Hi there! I'm PowMan, your PowerManager Operator. I'm here to help you with any questions you have about using the PowerManager applications for your daily tasks. What can I help you with today? Whether it's finding an account, processing a payment, or generating a report, just ask! Let me look that up for you. Based on the information I found, to fix an incorrect payment method for a UB payment, you'll need to reverse the payment and then re-enter it correctly. Here's how: 1. Navigate to Utility Billing > Account Maintenance > Customer 2. Click the ""Reverse"" button on the ribbon at the top 3. In the Reverse Customer Transactions screen, select the date range when the payment was made 4. Click the green flip button to populate the list of payments 5. Check the box next to the payment that needs correction 6. Enter any necessary information (adjustment code if applicable) 7. Click to complete the reversal 8. Once reversed, you can re-enter the payment with the correct payment method Would you like me to walk you through re-entering the payment after the reversal is complete?

cust] Payment Method Correction in Utility Billing A user needed guidance on correcting an accidentally entered wrong payment method for a UB (Utility Billing) payment. The resolution involves a step-by-step process to reverse the incorrect payment and re-enter it with the correct payment method. Key steps include navigating to Account Maintenance, using the ""Reverse"" button, selecting the specific payment, and then re-entering the payment with the correct method. Message History I accidently entered the wrong payment method for a costumer for UB Payment. How do I fix it? Hi there! I'm PowMan, your PowerManager Operator. I'm here to help you with any questions you have about using the PowerManager applications for your daily tasks. What can I help you with today? Whether it's finding an account, processing a payment, or generating a report, just ask! Let me look that up for you. Based on the information I found, to fix an incorrect payment method for a UB payment, you'll need to reverse the payment and then re-enter it correctly. Here's how: 1. Navigate to Utility Billing > Account Maintenance > Customer 2. Click the ""Reverse"" button on the ribbon at the top 3. In the Reverse Customer Transactions screen, select the date range when the payment was made 4. Click the green flip button to populate the list of payments 5. Check the box next to the payment that needs correction 6. Enter any necessary information (adjustment code if applicable) 7. Click to complete the reversal 8. Once reversed, you can re-enter the payment with the correct payment method Would you like me to walk you through re-entering the payment after the reversal is complete?"

column2: Tony Williams] Issue Lara Terrones from Castle Pines Metro. Dist. is reporting that several rate codes she updated in Impresa for rate increases effective 1/1/26 have disappeared. The affected rate codes are: 15 SERVICE/Water Usage B4, 30 - SERVICE/Cap Improvement Fee, 55 SERVICE2/Cap Improvement Fee 2, 80 SERVICE3/Cap Improvement Fee 3, and 90 HYDRANT/HYDRANT USAGE. Cause Based on the related tickets, this issue appears to be similar to ticket #100233 where rate codes were not showing up because they were not marked as active or the user was viewing a screen that excluded inactive rate codes. It's also possible that the rate codes were created with an incorrect effective date or there was an issue during the rate code update process. Proposed Solution The customer needs to check the active status of the rate codes and ensure they're viewing all rate codes (including inactive ones). If the rate codes are truly missing, they may need to recreate them using the proper effective date. Steps to Complete Open Utility Billing in Impresa Navigate to UB Administration Click on Rate Code Make sure ""Show Inactive"" is checked if it's an option on the screen Search for the specific rate codes by name or code number If the rate codes are found but not showing in the main interface, check if they are marked as active If the rate codes are not found, recreate them by copying an existing similar rate code and modifying the information as needed When creating or modifying rate codes, ensure the correct effective date (1/1/26) is entered Save the changes and verify the rate codes now appear correctly Additional Information According to KB Article ""Impresa - Create and Modify Rate Codes,"" rate code information can be modified as needed. Changes can be made to take effect immediately or on a specified effective date. The customer should verify that the correct effective date was entered when updating the rates. If multiple versions of the same rate code exist with the same effective date, the system will use the most recently entered conditions. This could potentially cause confusion if rate codes were entered multiple times.

Chris Holloway] Hi Lara, Please check the rate code screen and click the include inactives button at the top. This is the first thing to try. please let us know if the rate codes come back at that point. If not, we will need to connect to your server to take a look.

Lara Terrones] Chris, I just got it to work. It only worked when I clicked the button under water usage codes for some reason. You can close this ticket."
