Ben Aplin] Issue The customer, Jen Kasl from Village of Dorchester, is concerned about the process of printing W-2s and 1099s for 2025 and is unsure if she should update the tax tables for 2026 before printing the 2025 tax forms. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and documentation, the customer can safely update the 2026 tax tables before printing the 2025 W-2s. This is a common concern at the beginning of a new year, and the only potential issue would be if an employee exceeded the FICA maximum earnings threshold in 2025, which is unlikely for this small municipality. Steps to complete Reassure the customer that she can update the 2026 tax tables even if she hasn't printed the 2025 W-2s yet. To print W-2s for 2025, navigate to the Payroll module > W2's function. In the W2s Form, select the appropriate report grid (form design), and enter the date range for 2025 (01/01/2025 to 12/31/2025). Use the Preview button to review the W-2s before printing them. Once verified, use the Print button to print the W-2s. After W-2s are printed, the customer can proceed with updating the 2026 tax tables if she hasn't already done so by going to PR > Maintenance > E/D > clicking the Fed update button and then Update. For state tax tables, she will need to manually enter the Nebraska state tax information. Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Dahl Mitch Mullins Joe Sheehan Ben Aplin Referenced Documents 105629: Federal Tax Deduction 87298: Pender - Payroll Tax Questions 87260: Preston - Install PM 2023 main version and updates. 97859: [EXTERNAL] Tammy had not run her 2024 W-2s before she updated NE State Withholding Rates for 2025 108531: 2026 Tax Charts

Sreya Johny] Hi Jen, Please follow the steps below for processing W-2 forms: Steps for W-2 Forms In PowerManager, navigate to Payroll > Processing > W2s Select ""W-2 Substitute Employee (2025)"" for the Report Name Set the Date Range (typically first and last day of the year) Preview the forms to verify the information is correct For electronic filing, go to W2 Electronic File option Enter your PIN Number from the Social Security Administration Complete the contact information fields Specify the filename and location for the electronic file Click ""Create File"" to generate the electronic submission file After W-2s are printed, you can proceed with updating the 2026 tax tables if she hasn't already done so by going to PR > Maintenance > E/D > clicking the Fed update button and then Update. For state tax tables, you will need to manually enter the Nebraska state tax information from the email you received. If you encounter any errors, please send a screenshot and we will get back to you. Sreya

Sreya Johny] Two customers who moved in during October and November are having issues with their water readings not showing on the account or being computed. Only sewer and sanitation charges are appearing in Customer History for both accounts. Additionally, the Manual Entry screen doesn’t show any information for these two customers, so we’re unable to manually enter the previous month’s reading and complete the billing cycle. Sreya Johny] Self note: Edit the water meter record and check the status in the Readings tab and make sure it's not set to Final. Enter water readings manually and only compute water charges when backdating and complete the billing process. Take a backup if this didn't work.

Sreya Johny] Spoke with Jeanne and connected remotely. The water meter status on both accounts was set to Final in the Readings tab. We changed it to Active, manually entered the readings, computed water charges only, printed the bills, and finalized them. On one account, there were duplicate charges, so we uncomputed those charges, entered the January readings, then recomputed, printed, and finalized the bill. Jeanne is now able to proceed with the February readings.

Tony Williams] Issue The customer is encountering an error message when trying to input a new meter and transaction information after changing out a meter. The specific error message is: ""CANNOT INSERT THE VALUE NULL INTO COLUMN 'FUND_ID', TABLE 'OASIS.DBO.SA_COA_LINK: COLUMN DOES NOT ALLOW NULLS. INSERT FAILS. THE STATEMENT HAS BEEN TERMINATED."" Cause Based on similar tickets, this error occurs during the meter change out process when there is missing information in the meter record. Specifically, the system is trying to create a record in the SA_COA_LINK table but cannot because the FUND_ID field is null, which is not allowed. This typically happens when the Asset Distribution field is not properly populated during meter setup or when there are issues with the meter change out process. Proposed Solution The solution involves running specific SQL scripts to fix the missing information in the meter record. This will allow the meter change out process to complete successfully. Based on similar tickets, we need to run scripts that fix missing meter information and then properly complete the meter change out process. Steps to Complete 1. Contact the customer to schedule a time to connect to their server to run the necessary SQL scripts. 2. Run the ""FixMissingIssNums"" script which will correct the missing information in the meter record. This script typically needs to be modified to target the specific meter(s) having issues. 3. Run the ""FixMeterIssueInfo"" script to ensure all meter issue information is properly populated. 4. After running the scripts, verify that the meter change out process can be completed without errors. 5. If needed, guide the customer through the proper meter change out process as outlined in the KB article ""Impresa - METER CHANGE OUTS"": Go to the Service Location screen that needs a meter change out Click on the Connections tab Click the Change Out button in the Process pane Set the Change out/Reading date and enter the Final reading Select the replacement meter and enter the initial reading Complete the process Key People Who Have Solved Similar Issues Based on the referenced tickets, the following customer support agents have resolved similar issues: Tony Williams Chris Holloway Alex Mullins Additional Notes The customer may need to ensure that the Asset Distribution field is properly populated for all new meters to prevent this issue from recurring. According to the KB documentation, if they don't know which asset distribution to set, they should contact their finance department. Mitch Mullins] I would think if an ACH failed we'd be using adjustments to void the payment. The payment amount can be different than the most recently amount billed if the customer is setup as a budget customer, or perhaps they had a previous balance owed or a credit balance owed prior to the most recent billing. At any rate, if the customer payment didn't clear then that payment will need to be voided, whatever the amount may be.

Sreya Johny] Hi Theresa, When an ACH payment fails, you should use the ""Void Payment"" function in the Adjustments module to properly void the payment. The discrepancy between the billed amount and payment amount is normal in certain situations, such as when the customer is on a budget billing plan, has a previous balance, or has a credit balance. The void payment process will reverse the exact payment amount that failed, regardless of the most recent bill amount. Steps to complete Navigate to the Adjustments module in PowerManager Enter the original payment date as the Posting Date Select the customer whose ACH payment failed In the Payment Selection Grid, locate and click on the returned ACH payment (it will be highlighted) Click the Void button only once at the bottom right Click Save once the selected payment appears in the Adjustments section at the bottom. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Lara Terrones] We would like to set up the batch email process under BI in Impresa. Can someone please help us with this?
Lara Terrones] We would like to set up the batch email process under BI in Impresa. Can someone please help us with this?

Chris Holloway] REVIEW ALL SCRIPTS BEFORE RUNNING ANY OF THEM ON THEIR LIVE DATA!! Issue Castle Pines Metro. Dist. would like to set up the batch email process under BI in Impresa. This allows them to send mass emails to customers through the system. Proposed Solution Based on the related documents and similar tickets, we need to configure the batch email functionality in Impresa by setting up the SMTP server connection and ensuring proper permissions are in place. This will enable the customer to send batch emails to their customers through the BI module. Prerequisites The batch email process uses the Client machine and no longer uses the SQL server to send emails in version 4.25.0.0 and newer. The SMTP server needs to be set to allow the workstation/s to send emails. (They must be white listed.) Steps to complete Check the sa_company table in sql for the smtp_username and smtp_password field. if they are not there, run this script: IF NOT EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM syscolumns C WHERE id = OBJECT_ID('dbo.sa_company ') AND name = 'smtp_username' ) ALTER TABLE dbo.sa_company ADD smtp_username VARCHAR(255) NULL GO IF NOT EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM syscolumns C WHERE id = OBJECT_ID('dbo.sa_company ') AND name = 'smtp_password' ) ALTER TABLE dbo.sa_company ADD smtp_password VARCHAR(255) NULL GO EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; GO RECONFIGURE; GO EXEC sp_configure 'Ole Automation Procedures', '1' GO RECONFIGURE GO Run the following SQL commands on the database server to enable Ole Automation Procedures: EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; GO RECONFIGURE; GO EXEC sp_configure 'Ole Automation Procedures', '1' GO RECONFIGURE GO Update the SMTP server credentials in the database: UPDATE sa_company SET smtp_username = '[email_username]', smtp_password = '[email_password]' Ensure the SMTP server name is correctly configured in the Batch Email screen If using version 4.25.0.0 or later, ensure the workstations have permission to send emails through the SMTP server (as batch emails are now sent from workstations, not the SQL server) Guide the customer through the batch email process: Go to Utility Billing > Batch Entries > Batch Email Select criteria such as Billing Cycle, Billing Status, Class, etc. Choose accounts by book number or specific accounts as needed Create contact information and subject line Select target accounts and verify information Click Send to distribute the emails Key people that may be able to assist Chris Holloway - Support Analyst who has resolved multiple batch email setup issues Sachin Yadav - Support agent who has worked on batch email configuration Tony Williams - Support agent who has knowledge of batch email functionality and version requirements The customer's IT staff - Will need to provide SMTP server details and possibly grant permissions Additional Notes Based on the related tickets, common issues with batch email setup include: SMTP server configuration problems Missing Ole Automation Procedures permissions Workstation permission issues in newer versions of Impresa Missing or incorrect credentials for the email account We no longer use cdosys_mail_errorlog_id table for tracking problems with batch emails. It is still used for the email billing process. If the customer continues to experience issues after initial setup, we may need to check the error logs with: SELECT TOP 50 * FROM dbo.sa_cdosys_mail_errorlog ORDER BY cdosys_mail_errorlog_id DESC Referenced Documents 36710: KB: Impresa - How to send a Batch Email 33895: KB: impresa - How to Create a Service Location 33098: Impresa: Batch E-Mail 88902: KB: Impresa 4.23.0.0 Release Notes 34955: KB: Impresa: Payment Extensions 91470: Batch Email 93438: D0# 56904 - Mass Emails or Phone Calls 87442: Sending an email for shut off notices 101055: Batch Email Issue 106036: Batch email set up

column1: 20974367,"What is the difference between Deposit function vs Post Amounts function in the cash register module?","[2026-01-15 09:58
column2: Ben Aplin] Closed bank reg training ticket, but Kristina needs to understand the difference asked above.

cust] Dear Kristina, The resolution for this ticket has been provided. If the issue has not been resolved please update the ticket as soon as possible by responding in this ticket. Susan Thomas] Hi Kristina, Below are the differences between the Deposit function and the Post Amounts function in the Cash Register module. Please let us know if you need any further assistance. Susan Post Amounts Function The Post Amounts function in the Cash Register module is used to define how batch payments will be distributed to their destination modules. Specifically: It allows you to define how payments related to Utility Billing and/or Accounts Receivable will be posted to their respective modules You can specify batch numbers, types of payment, and GL accounts for each destination For Utility Billing payments, you can indicate whether to include non-finalized charges For Accounts Receivable payments, you can specify the cash account to be used The function essentially moves the payment data from the Cash Register module to the appropriate destination modules (UB, AR, or GL) Deposit Function The Deposit function (Bank Deposit) is used to create a bank deposit record that groups payments for physical deposit at a bank. Key features include: It allows you to add cash register batches, utility billing payments, accounts receivable payments, and general ledger entries to a bank deposit record You can assign a deposit number and date to track when funds were physically deposited at the bank The function helps with bank statement reconciliation by recording when funds were deposited It's typically used after payments have been posted through the Post Amounts function Key Differences Timing in workflow: Post Amounts is used first to distribute payments to their destination modules, while Deposit is used later to record the physical bank deposit Purpose: Post Amounts focuses on the accounting distribution of funds, while Deposit focuses on tracking the physical movement of funds to the bank Module integration: Post Amounts connects Cash Register to UB/AR/GL modules, while Deposit connects to the Bank Reconciliation module Required information: Post Amounts requires batch numbers and GL accounts, while Deposit requires deposit numbers and dates In summary, Post Amounts handles the accounting side of payments (where the money goes in your system), while Deposit handles the banking side (tracking when money physically goes to the bank).

Kristina Stoki] I still don't understand how I don't end up with duplicate entries in the Bank Reconciliation module if I am supposed to use both functions. When you post something to GL it automatically shows up in Bank Reconciliation module and so then does the Create Deposit function so I'm still confused on what the workflow is supposed to be. Kristina Stoki City Clerk/Treasurer City of Ashland Ashland, NE 68003 (402)944-3387 (402)944-3386 fax

Susan Thomas] Understanding the Difference Between Deposit and Post Amounts Functions Clarification of Workflow These two functions serve different purposes in the payment processing workflow and work together without creating duplicates: 1. The Post Amounts function distributes your payment data from Cash Register to the appropriate modules (UB, AR, or GL). This step handles the accounting side - telling your system which accounts should be credited/debited. When you post amounts, the transactions are recorded in your GL and will appear in Bank Reconciliation as ""unreconciled"" items. 2. The Deposit function (Bank Deposit) is used after posting amounts and groups those already-posted payments into a physical bank deposit record. This function doesn't create new GL entries; rather, it creates a deposit record that helps you match your bank statement deposits during reconciliation. Correct Workflow to Avoid Duplicates To avoid duplicates, follow this sequence: Process payments in Cash Register Balance your cash drawer Use Post Amounts to distribute the payments to their proper modules (UB/AR/GL) Use the Deposit function to group these posted payments into a bank deposit record that matches your physical bank deposit When reconciling your bank statement, you'll match the deposit record to your bank statement deposit The system is designed so that using both functions does not create duplicate entries. The Deposit function is simply grouping already-posted transactions into a deposit record for reconciliation purposes.

Kristina Stoki] So I just leave the entries created in GL which show up in Bank Rec as uncleared items in Bank Rec and only clear the bank rec entries created through the Create Deposit function? Or does the Create Deposit function get rid of the GL entries created through the Post Amounts function in the Bank Rec module while leaving them in the GL? Still confused. Kristina Stoki City Clerk/Treasurer City of Ashland Ashland, NE 68003 (402)944-3387 (402)944-3386 fax
Ben Aplin] Going to review tomorrow morning and hopefully we can bring clarity and closure to this issue.

Susan Thomas] Understanding Post Amounts vs Create Deposit Functions Post Amounts Function The Post Amounts function in Cash Register distributes your payment transactions to their destination modules (UB, AR, or GL). When you use this function: It creates the accounting entries in your General Ledger These entries automatically appear in Bank Rec as uncleared items This step handles the accounting side of your transactions Create Deposit Function The Create Deposit function in the Bank Deposit module groups already-posted payments into a physical bank deposit record. When you use this function: It does NOT create new GL entries It creates a deposit record that helps match your bank statement deposits during reconciliation This step handles the banking/reconciliation side of your transactions How They Work Together Without Creating Duplicates When you use both functions in sequence, you're not creating duplicate entries. Here's why: The Post Amounts function creates the initial GL entries that appear in Bank Rec as uncleared items. The Create Deposit function simply groups these already-posted transactions into a deposit record, making it easier to match with your bank statement during reconciliation. Correct Workflow To properly process payments without duplicates: Process payments in Cash Register Balance your cash drawer Use Post Amounts to distribute payments to their proper modules Use Create Deposit to group these posted payments into a bank deposit record When reconciling your bank statement, match the deposit record to your bank statement deposit The GL entries created by Post Amounts will remain in your system, and the Create Deposit function simply provides a way to organize these entries for reconciliation purposes.

Kristina Stoki] I will give this a try. This makes a lot more sense with the explanation provided. Kristina Stoki City Clerk/Treasurer City of Ashland Ashland, NE 68003 (402)944-3387 (402)944-3386 fax"

column1: 20974070,"DO #6749 City of Wilber - CHANGE READ DATE FOR WATER METER","[2026-01-15 08:20
column2: Melissa Beeman] We are entering meters this week. This month we estimate water usage. I tried doing that and most are working but all seem to have the wrong read date for just the water. Without manually changing each meter date I was hoping we can change all to the same read date.
Ben Aplin] Good Morning Melissa - someone will be in touch shortly. Do you bill on Friday?

Mitch Dahl] This would need to be done with an update SQL statement. I usually like to review the data before developing the statement, but it would be something like this: update UBMeterRead set ReadDate = 'MM/DD/YYYY' where (ReadDate = 'MM/DD/YYYY') and (PriorDate = 'MM/DD/YYYY') and (TypeOfRead = 0) and (Billed = False) ReadDate in the ""set"" clause is the reading date she wants. ReadDate in the ""where"" clause is the ""wrong read date"". PriorDate in the ""where"" clause is the value of the PriorDate field for those wrongly dated readings. TypeOfRead in the 'where"" clause - 0 = Estimated Billed in the ""where"" clause - only want to deal with readings that haven't been billed yet.
Ben Aplin] email to Sreya asking together DO in asap.
cust] Ticket has been synced with TFS' work item CHANGE READ DATE FOR WATER METER with state ""New"".

column1: 20974006,"NEED HELP WITH PENALTIES AND LATE NOTICES","[2026-01-15 08:01
column2: Tristan Plessel] We need to have our late notices sent out by tomorrow or Tuesday at the latest- we are following the instructions we have and cannot get them to work. We are short staffed and need help walking through the process.

Joe Sheehan] I'll help you find information about Penalties and Late Notices in the Utility Billing system. Let me search the knowledge base for this information.# Penalties and Late Notices Process in PM Utility Billing Step 1: Calculate Penalties Navigate to UB > Processing > Penalties & Fines Enter the Bill Due Date (the date bills were due) Enter the Posting Date (date penalties will be applied) Click Calculate button In the customer selection form, typically select all customers Click OK to calculate penalty amounts Step 2: Review Calculated Penalties The system displays calculated penalties in the grid area You can edit penalty amounts if needed You can delete rows using ctrl-click or shift-click for multiple selections Click the Reports button to print a penalty report (do this before finalizing) Step 3: Finalize Penalties When satisfied with the penalties, click the Finalize button In the Finalize form, check ""Print Statements"" if you want updated statements for penalized customers Click OK to complete the finalization process Step 4: Generate Late Notices/Disconnect Notices Navigate to UB > Processing > Reminders (for payment reminders) Enter statement date range (typically first and last days of the month) Choose between ""open invoices"" or ""activity"" type statements OR Use Disconnect Letter Printing process for disconnect notices Note: There is an option ""Only include customers with penalties"" that can be checked to only include customers who have penalties applied Important Notes: Penalties can be percentage-based (configured in Penalties & Fines Setup Form) You can set different percentages for ""Current"" and ""Arrears"" balances If you forget to print penalty reports, you can access them later under Reports > Financial > Penalties Manual penalties can be added through UB > Processing > Adjustments > [Select Customer] > Fines tab Customers with active payment arrangements can be excluded from penalties if that option is enabled in the setup This process helps manage late payments and ensures customers are properly notified of penalties and potential disconnection for non-payment.

column2: DEB MCGUIRE] Utility Billing:I have been writing off customer account balances after 4 years of non-payment. I need to know how to delete those customers off of our customer maintenance page. I do the year end process every year and those accounts don't come off the maintenance page.
Ben Aplin] Hello - we'll review and get back to you shortly on this.

Mitch Dahl] My guess is that the write off transactions are dated more recently than the Close Thru Date use for the Year End Close process. Those write-off transactions would not get deleted in the Year End Close process and therefore, the customers can't be deleted. A Customer History report would show if this is the case or not.

Sreya Johny] Hi Deb, It could be because the write off transactions are dated more recently than the Close Thru Date use for the Year End Close process. Those write-off transactions would not get deleted in the Year End Close process and therefore, the customers can't be deleted. A Customer History report would show if this is the case or not. Please let us know if you have any questions.
DEB MCGUIRE] I wrote some off on 1/5/2020 that are still on the customer maintenance page. Are you saying that those customers can never be deleted? Deb

Sreya Johny] Hi Deb, Customer accounts won’t be deleted during the Year End Close if they have any remaining balances or transactions dated after the Close Thru Date. To ensure inactive accounts are properly removed, make sure all balances (including budget balances) are cleared and all transactions fall within the Close Thru Date. You can review this using the Customer History report.

Amy Wegener] Looking for w-2 information. This is my first time running w-2's through PM and would like some help on starting the process and what forms I need for the w-2? Would really like to be able to run them tomorrow if someone can help me today?

Susan Thomas] Hi Amy, Please follow the steps and let us know if you have any further questions. Steps to complete Navigate to Payroll > Processing > W2s in PowerManager Select ""W-2 Substitute Employee (2026)"" for the Report Name Set the Date Range (typically 01/01/2025 to 12/31/2025 for the full tax year) Select the desired sort order for the forms (typically by employee ID or name) Use the ""Preview"" button to verify the information is correct before printing Use the ""Print"" button to print the W-2 forms on plain paper (no special forms needed) For the W-3 form (employer summary), select the W-3 form design from the Report Grid If electronic filing is needed, use the ""W2 Electronic File"" option in Payroll > Processing Additional Information PowerManager provides ""substitute"" forms that can be printed on plain paper - you don't need to order pre-printed forms. The system will print all boxes, text, and other form elements automatically. These substitute forms are acceptable for distribution to employees and for filing purposes. You can use the ""Design"" button to customize the form layout if needed, but the default templates should work for most situations. Before printing all forms, it's recommended to preview them to ensure all information appears correctly. Susan

Tony Williams] AI Suggested Solution Issue The customer is trying to run a Work Order Report by employee (specifically employee #2458 Eric Fountain) but is getting no results even though there should be data. Additionally, they mentioned that the list of employees shown in the Job & Labor Selection includes both active and inactive employees, and they would prefer to see only active employees. Analysis Based on the ticket information, it appears there may be a configuration issue with the Work Order Reports functionality. The customer is trying to filter work orders by a specific employee but getting no results. This could be due to how employee information is linked to work orders in the system, or potentially a setup issue where the employee selection criteria isn't properly configured. Proposed Solution After reviewing the documentation and related tickets, I recommend checking the WO Reports configuration in the Inventory module. The issue appears to be with how employee data is being filtered or linked to work order records. Steps to complete 1. Navigate to the Inventory module and select Reports > WO Reports 2. When selecting the report type, ensure you're using a report that properly includes employee data. The ""WO Reports"" section in the documentation indicates there are various report formats available. 3. When setting up the report parameters, check if there are additional filters that need to be set besides just selecting the employee. For example, you might need to: Ensure the correct date range is selected (or confirm ""no date range"" works as expected) Verify that the Job Types selection includes the types of jobs this employee works on Check if there are any other required filters that might be limiting results 4. For the issue with active/inactive employees displaying in the selection list, this appears to be a system limitation in the current version. This may require a feature enhancement request to filter the employee selection list to show only active employees. Additional Recommendations If the above steps don't resolve the issue, consider these alternatives: 1. Try running a more general Work Order report without the employee filter, then export to Excel where you can filter for the specific employee.

Joe Sheehan] Sales tax Billing Register reportPenalty's are calculated on each service and rounded independently Banker's Rounding Doing calculations based on totals

Joe Sheehan] Hi Jody, I spoke with Mitch Mullins on this topic. He said that the system is calculating the totals correctly. How it works is that tax is applied to each service and rounded on the service level using Banker Rounding. Rather than calculating the total and then applying the tax and rounding. Also, any penalties being calculated are calculated independently and rounded. A good way to look at it is the Billing Register Report found in UB > Reports > Financial > Register. You could always print this out for the customer in question to help them understand how the bill is broken down. I'm going to close this ticket for now, but if you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reply. Joe

Maddy Schmitz] Voided out a paycheck now it shows negative amounts on my reports. need to finish payroll today.

Susan Thomas] How to Fix Negative Contributions After a Voided Paycheck Hi Maddy, Here are the steps to correct the negative contribution amounts: ✔ If the void was done by mistake and needs to be reversed Go to Payroll > Void Paychecks Select the employee’s ID Enter today’s date as the Posting Date Check “Post to GL” if you want the correction to post to the General Ledger In the grid, select the voided check that caused the negative amounts Click Void again — this “voids the void” and removes the negative entries Run a Journal Report to confirm the negative amounts are gone ✔ If the void was correct, but the negative amounts are hurting reporting Add a positive contribution in the current payroll cycle to offset the negative amount. This brings the employee’s contribution balance back to zero. Please let us know how it went, or if there’s anything else you need from us. Susan

Joni Elston] We have an water account that has been active since 2015 and they are now adding sewer. Sewer start date is 1/13/26. If we add the sewer rate code now, will the system prorate the sewer charges for three days on the billing cycle we are in now?12/15/25 to 1/15/2026, bills due 2/15/26.Colleen also has an open ticket 107357 regarding sewer if you can help us with both of these at the same time.
Ben Aplin] Good Morning - we're reviewing and will get back to you shortly.

Tony Williams] Joni, For this ticket, Yes, the system will prorate the sewer charges when you add the sewer rate code with the correct start date. You'll need to make sure the proration settings are configured correctly on the rate code and that you enter the proper start date for the sewer service. Steps to complete 1. Go to the customer's Service Location screen and click on the Connections tab. 2. For the sewer service you're adding, set the ""Prorate status"" to ""New"". 3. Enter ""1/13/2026"" as the ""Start date"" for the sewer service. 4. Make sure the appropriate sewer Income Center is checked. 5. Verify that the sewer rate code has ""Prorate"" set to either ""New"" or ""Both"" (which covers both new and final bills). 6. Also verify the ""Prorate days"" setting on the rate code is set to the correct number of days for your billing cycle (likely 30 days). 7. Check the ""Prorate charge"" checkbox on the rate code to ensure charges will be prorated. 8. When you run billing, the system should automatically calculate the prorated amount for the 3 days of sewer service. With some other customers having Prorate issues, we've found that the proration days were set to 60 instead of 30, which was causing incorrect calculations. Make sure your proration days are set correctly for your billing cycle. For 107357, Lisa and/or Chris will be reaching out.

Chris Holloway] Hi Joni, If the meter is being added to the same group as the water meter is, and you set it to a prorate status of new, both the water and the sewer will be prorated. You will have to check the rate code to be sure that it is set to be able to be prorated as well.

Joni Elston] Tony, On our sewer accounts, we have never put in a start date in for sewer, only a start date for water, then we add a rate code for Midlothian sewer. See attached screen shots. This customer is not as our normal set up because they already have water established since 2015. The actual rate code Meven is checked to prorate but there is not any place to put a start date. Mitch Mullins] I'm not aware of the need to de-authorize a meter for a customer move out/move in. Typically the move out/move in is done in the typical fashion and the new customer should show up in the Tantalus dashboard after a new file from PowerManager has been imported by Tantalus. Meters should be automatically sent with a 0 for the authorized value when they are changed out. (0=de-authorized, 1=authroized) This code is sent for the old removed meter until the customer bill is finalized. Meters are selected for Tantalus files based on the meter manufacturer. In order to add a meter to a de-authorized file we'll need a special meter manufacturer code and defined a file with 0 for the authorization value. Here's an example:

Susan Thomas] I spoke with our developers for clarification. They confirmed that you do not need to manually de‑authorize a meter during a customer move‑out/move‑in. Once the move‑out/move‑in is completed in PowerManager, the new customer will appear in the Tantalus dashboard after the next file import—no extra steps are required. If a meter is physically replaced, the system automatically sends it with a 0 (de‑authorized) status until the final bill is completed. This is handled automatically. In the rare case that a meter needs to be manually added to a de‑authorization file, it would require a special meter manufacturer code and a file set with an authorization value of 0.

The server response was: 550 5.7.1 Service unavailable, Client host [69.27.84.16] blocked using Spamhaus. To request removal from this list see AS(1450) [CH3PEP

Chris Holloway] Hi Nate, Here is the suggestion from AI about the error and it suggested to check for the IP address to see if there were any reported issues. I did check and the site returned ""69.27.84.16 has no issues""here are the results from AI when I fed it the results: ""no issues"" doesn't align with current checks. I queried a multi-RBL tool, and it shows IP 69.27.84.16 is actively listed on several Spamhaus blocklists as of now: Spamhaus Block List (SBL): Listed (return code: 4) – Typically indicates IPs involved in spam or abuse. Exploits Block List (XBL): Listed (return code: 5) – Often due to detected exploits, open proxies, malware, or compromised devices on the network. Policy Block List (PBL): Listed (return code: 6) – This flags end-user or dynamic IPs not intended for direct email sending (common for residential/broadband connections). ZEN (Combined List): Listed (return code: 2) – Aggregates the above; many email providers query this. Composite Blocking List (CSS): Not listed. This explains why your SQL Server emails are still being blocked—recipients (or their providers) are rejecting based on these listings. Why the Discrepancy? You might have checked a different IP or misread the results. Double-check directly at by entering 69.27.84.16. If you recently requested delisting, changes can take 24-48 hours (or longer) to propagate across DNS and email systems. Retest sending after that window. Cache issues: Some email servers cache blocklist results for hours/days. Next Steps to Resolve Confirm and Delist: Visit search the IP, and follow the per-list instructions. For PBL: Often self-removable if your ISP policy allows—click the removal link and verify you're using a static IP or mail server setup. For SBL/XBL: These require fixing root causes (e.g., scan for malware on your network/server using tools like Malwarebytes or ESET). Then, contact your ISP (Point Broadband) to request delisting on your behalf—use abuse@point-broadband.com or call +1-903-873-3122. Provide evidence of fixes. Avoid repeated requests without fixes, as it can extend listings. Check Other Blocklists: Run a full scan at (enter the IP) or to see if it's on non-Spamhaus lists like Barracuda, SORBS, or UCEPROTECT. Workarounds While Fixing: Switch your SQL Server Database Mail to relay through a reputable SMTP service (e.g., SendGrid, Amazon SES, or Microsoft 365) to bypass your IP's reputation issues entirely. Update the SMTP server in SSMS > Management > Database Mail > Accounts. Test email delivery using tools like mail-tester.com or GlockApps to identify blocks. If you share a screenshot of your Spamhaus check or the exact current error message, I can refine this further. In the meantime, addressing the listings should clear the blocks.

Sreya Johny] Hi Mikey, Please follow the steps below to manually enter the budget amounts: Go to General Ledger > Budgeting > Edit Amounts Click the ""Next"" button (it will be grayed out when active) - this takes you to next year's budget amounts In the left side of the form, scroll through the account list Enter the total annual budget amount for each account in the Next Budget column The system will automatically divide each total equally across the 12 accounting periods Use the Budget Detail section on the right side to modify individual monthly amounts if you don't want equal distribution Click Close to save all budget amounts Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

column1: 20969263,"PowerManager Utility Payments - Search and Navigation Issues","[2026-01-13 10:15
column2: Ben Aplin] Customer Experiencing Search and Navigation Problems in PowerManager The customer reported two main issues with the PowerManager Utility Payments system: 1. Unable to search for customers by account numbers 2. Unable to navigate to the next page after finding a customer Troubleshooting steps suggested: - Uncheck ""Retain Last Search"" option in the system settings - Click save button in the standard menu bar - Verify search and navigation functionality after making these changes Message History Yes I need help with power Manager Utility Payments Hi there! I'm PowMan, your PowerManager Operator. I'm here to help you with any questions you have about using the PowerManager applications for your daily tasks. What can I help you with today? Whether it's finding an account, processing a payment, or generating a report, just ask! I'd be happy to help you with PowerManager utility payments! Could you tell me more about what you're trying to do? For example, are you trying to process a customer payment, set up automatic payments, or perhaps view payment history? It is not allowing to search costumer by account numbers. Then when I do find the costumer it is not allowing me to go to next page. Let me look into that for you! I found some information that might help with your customer search issue. Based on what you're describing, it sounds like the ""Retain Last Search"" feature might be causing problems. Try these steps: 1. Click on the Gear/COG wheel on the customer screen 2. Scroll down and uncheck ""Retain Last Search"" 3. Click the save button at the top of the screen in the standard menu bar This should fix the issue where you can't properly search for customers or move to the next page. The system might be stuck on a previous search result. Is there anything specific you're trying to do after finding the customer account?

Chris Holloway] Hi Ashtyn, We took a look at your system and found that your computer is still on Windows 10. The workstation that is able to print the bills correctly is on Windows 11. We have noticed a problem with drivers from Windows 10 since 11 came out. We attempted to adjust the printer settings, and created a Bills profile, this allowed the paper size to remain the same, but the alignment is off. Normally we are able to adjust the crystal report for the bill form to correct this, but doing that in Parallels will change it for everyone who is able to print. Your IT will probably have to update your drivers for the printer on your workstation, if there is an option for that. Or update your computer to Windows 11. Joe Sheehan] I'll need to search our knowledge base for information about the Tantalus PowerManager integration. Let me do that for you.# Tantalus PowerManager Integration Overview Introduction The Tantalus PowerManager integration provides automated meter reading capabilities by connecting PowerManager's utility billing system with Tantalus' advanced metering infrastructure (AMI). This integration streamlines the meter reading process, improves data accuracy, and enhances operational efficiency. Key Components 1. System Architecture PowerManager: Handles utility billing, customer accounts, and meter data management Tantalus AMI System: Collects meter readings wirelessly through network infrastructure Integration Layer: Facilitates data exchange between both systems 2. File Exchange Process PowerManager exports meter information files to Tantalus Tantalus collects readings and returns consumption data Files are typically exchanged via SFTP using WinSCP Automated file transfers are configured using Windows Task Scheduler 3. Meter Configuration Meters must be properly configured in PowerManager with: Correct meter manufacturer assignment Radio ID/Serial Number Proper truncation values and multipliers UOM (Unit of Measure) settings 4. Meter Types Support Electric Meters: Tantalus, Itron (various ERT types), Sentinel Water Meters: Neptune (various models including T0, T1, T2, M10) Each meter type requires specific configuration parameters Implementation Process 1. Pre-Implementation Requirements VPN connection to Tantalus server Meter inventory with correct identifiers PowerManager Value Support plan 2. Setup Steps Install and configure WinSCP for secure file transfer Create meter manufacturer definitions in PowerManager Configure Tantalus export file definitions Set up automated file transfer schedule Test file imports/exports 3. Testing and Validation Compare manual readings with AMI readings Verify reading formats and multipliers Confirm proper meter status updates Operational Considerations 1. Meter Change-Outs Changed meters are automatically deauthorized (Authorized=0) Requires proper meter manufacturer assignment 2. Troubleshooting Common Issues UOM errors (""UOM is undefined"") Incorrect truncation values Meter authorization problems VPN connectivity issues 3. Dashboard Management Monitor meter reading status Review error reports in ""done"" folder Verify utility name matches between systems Benefits Eliminates manual meter reading Improves billing accuracy Provides near real-time consumption data Reduces operational costs Enhances customer service capabilities For specific implementation assistance, please contact your PowerManager support representative.

Monica Ferguson] Good morning, How do I do a meter changeout reversal? Also, I am new to Leitchfield Utilities, and I need to be set up on the online hub. Monica Ferguson City of Leitchfield/Leitchfield Utilities “An Equal Opportunity Provider and Employer” Email: monica.ferguson@leitchfield.org 515 S. Main St. P.o. Box 398 Leitchfield, Ky. 42754 Phone: 270-259-4034 Fax: 270-259-5858

Tony Williams] Monica, I've set you up for the Customer Hub and you will be getting an email to create your password for the account. To reverse a meter changeout, you need to reset everything back to the way it was before the meter change out occurred. This process will ensure that historical data and reporting remain accurate. Steps to complete a meter changeout reversal Set the top line of the old meter's ""Edit Readings"" tab to be the same as the line below it, including the reading, the usage, and the reading date. Set the top line of the new meter's ""Edit Readings"" tab to be the same as the line below it, including the reading, the usage, and the reading date. Remove the new meter from the service location and reattach the old meter to the service location (do not use the meter change out process for this step). Once everything is reset to how it was before the meter changeout, you can perform the meter changeout process again with the correct final reading if needed. - Tony

Sachin Yadav] Hi Monica, You can change the readings and usage and reading dates on both meters in edit readings back to the previous reading. Then remove the meters from the location and put the old one back on. This only works if they have not applied charges, though. Please let me know if you need our assistance on this. Sachin
Monica Ferguson] Another question, will the customer be charged twice for the usage?
Monica Ferguson] CLOSE TICKET"

Susan Thomas] Marlene was running the UB Payments report for December and noticed that the sewer charge is showing as credited.

Mitch Mullins] We sometimes see negative payments when a previous payment was over-allocated to a type of service. The negative payment is applying the credit balance owed for one type of service to other service types with amounts that are owed.

Susan Thomas] Hi Marlene, Sometimes you may see a negative payment on the account. This happens when too much of a previous payment was applied to one type of service. The system then moves the extra amount to other services that still have a balance. So, the negative payment is just the system shifting the overpaid amount to where it’s actually needed. You don’t need to do anything — the system has already corrected it. Susan

Mitch Mullins] It's possible the employee's estimated annual income is lower then the federal married income tax withholding threshhold. I'm assuming the deduction itself is setup with the correct table values, list of earnings, and list of pre-tax deductions. We'll also need to know the employee's earnings, pay frequency, and W4 information. If the employee ""2020 or later w-4"" option is checked the standard deduction amount is multiplied by 3. This is the same as the employee claiming 3 federal exemptions.

Sreya Johny] Hi Robyn, Apologies for the delayed response! This could be because the employee's estimated annual income is lower than the federal married income tax withholding threshold. Other possibilities include incomplete W-4 information, incorrect deduction setup, or issues with the ""Compute"" checkbox settings. Steps to complete Verify the employee's W-4 information is correctly entered on the W-2/W-4 tab page of the Employee Edit form, including checking if ""2020 or later W-4"" option is selected if applicable. Check if Step 4(c) amount from the W-4 is entered in the federal withholding's extra column on the Deductions page. Verify the employee's earnings, pay frequency, and filing status are correctly entered. Confirm the federal withholding deduction is properly set up with the correct table values. Check that the ""Compute"" checkbox is selected for the federal withholding deduction on the employee's Deductions page. If the employee's estimated annual income is below the federal tax withholding threshold for their filing status, this would explain why no tax is being withheld (this is functioning as designed). If necessary, manually enter an amount in the ""Extra"" field for the federal withholding deduction to force a specific withholding amount. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Sreya Johny] Root Cause Analysis Based on the information provided and similar cases, this issue appears to be related to how PowerManager determines billing service dates. As Mitch Mullins explained in the ticket conversation, ""Customer bill service from date is determined by the oldest prior reading date, electric or water, for each customer. The customer bill service to date is determined by the most recent current reading date, either electric or water."" The issue may be related to either the Tantalus or Neptune 360 integration, as there's a known issue with Neptune 360 and electric meter readings sometimes containing incorrect digit counts. Proposed Solution To resolve the issue with mismatched billing days between electric and water services, we need to examine the meter reading data and potentially void and re-bill the affected customers. This approach has successfully resolved similar issues in other cases where billing days or readings were incorrect. Steps to Complete Review the affected customer accounts to verify the discrepancy between electric and water billing days Check the meter reading dates for both services to identify where the mismatch is occurring Verify if there are any blank dates in the meter reading history that could be causing the system to use incorrect prior readings Check if any readings have incorrect status (Final vs. Normal) or if any are marked as ""ignore for calculations"" For affected accounts, use the Void/Re-bill feature in Utility Billing Adjustments to correct the bills: Void the incorrect bills Enter the correct meter reading information with proper dates Use the ""Post readings and calculate charges"" option when re-billing Consider sending corrected bills to affected customers with an explanation of the issue Document the process for future reference to prevent similar issues
Sreya Johny] Going to call Jackie.

Sreya Johny] Spoke with Jackie and connected remotely. We reviewed the affected accounts and found that the October electric readings on those accounts were misdated as 9/30. We corrected the read dates on all affected accounts. Jackie will let us know during the next billing cycle if the report still doesn’t show the correct number of days."

Ben Aplin] Would want to look at their pay frequency and W4. Pay frequency determines the total to be run through the withholdings amount - e.g. if paid 24x per year, then annual income is estimated based on current income x 24 (this would be for semi-monthly frequency). This amount is then if fed through a threshold that if the employee is paid below that, no income tax will be withheld.

Ben Aplin] If Federal Married, it takes 3x the exemption, so if employee makes less than the following, they would not be charged tax: 19300+(3*4300)=32,200 However, on the W4, employee can select the amount per period they would like to have with held."

column2: JEAN KLASSEN] I have two board members that chose to put all their money to Federal income tax. Now when i am printing W-2's they are not printing one for them, I need help on the setup for them.Jean Klassen

Ben Aplin] Hi Jean - when you say they chose to put all their money to Federal Income tax, do you mean that they were not paid, and that their entire compensation was directed to W2s? Also, can you attach the screens and parameters entered for the process of attempting go print these W2s? Thank yuo.

JEAN KLASSEN] Hello, I have enclosed the Council Member’s employee setup (She gets paid quarterly), the department I use for payroll processing and the selected W-2 form. City Treasurer Jean Klassen 223 S Chestnut St Kimball, NE 69145

Sreya Johny] Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and related tickets, this appears to be an issue with how the board members' compensation was set up. When employees direct all compensation to taxes, they still need proper deduction setup in the system to ensure W-2s are generated correctly. The system likely isn't recognizing these employees as having reportable wages since they didn't receive physical paychecks. Steps to complete Verify that the board members have Federal & State withholding deductions properly added to their employee records on the Deductions tab, even if all compensation goes to taxes. Check that the board members have appropriate earnings recorded in the system. Even if all earnings went to taxes, they need to be properly recorded as earnings first. When running the W-2s process, ensure you're using the correct date range that encompasses when these board members were paid (since they're paid quarterly). If using a custom W-2 form design, verify it's properly configured to display all employee types. Consider using the standard ""W-2 Substitute Employee (2024)"" format provided in the system update rather than a modified version. If the above steps don't resolve the issue, you may need to review the employee setup to ensure they're classified correctly for tax reporting purposes. Additional Information Based on ticket #100660, even when employees are exempt from certain taxes, they should still have the appropriate deduction entries added to their profile with the ""Compute"" checkbox unchecked and zero in the ""Total"" column. This ensures proper W-2 reporting. The board members may need similar setup to ensure their W-2s generate correctly, even though all compensation is directed to Federal income tax. Joe Sheehan] Issue The Village of Greenport has reported that a customer (account #236) has received two separate bills for the same account. The customer should only receive one bill, and Tina Zilnicki, the Utility Biller, is seeking an explanation for this double billing issue to provide to the customer. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, this appears to be a case where the customer is being double billed due to either duplicate rates being assigned to the account or an issue with how charges were computed. This can happen when there are multiple rates assigned to the same meter, when bills aren't properly voided before rebilling, or when charges are computed multiple times for the same billing period. Steps to Complete First, request images of both bills from Tina to confirm the exact nature of the double billing (which our agent Ben Aplin has already done). Once images are received, examine the customer's account in PowerManager to identify if there are duplicate rates assigned to the same meter or if the customer has been billed twice for the same period. If duplicate rates are found, make one of them inactive to prevent future double billing (as was done in ticket #105191). For the current double billing, use the Adjustments application to void one of the bills. Make sure to set the correct posting date when voiding. If needed, use the Re-Bill feature in Utility Billing Adjustments to issue a corrected bill with proper meter readings and charges. Be sure to check the ""Post readings and calculate charges"" option if issuing a corrected bill. Print and finalize the new bill, even if the customer doesn't need a physical copy. Consider issuing a credit adjustment if the customer has already paid the incorrect amount. Key People Who Have Solved Similar Issues Mitch Dahl Susan Thomas Joe Sheehan Sreya Johny Additional Notes Based on similar tickets, the most common causes of double billing are: 1) duplicate rates assigned to the same meter, 2) improper voiding/rebilling procedures, or 3) computing charges twice for the same billing period. Once we receive the bill images, we can determine which specific issue is occurring and provide a targeted solution to prevent this from happening again in the future. Referenced Documents 101737: Budget Billing Customer payment display doubled 105191: Customer charges 104818: Adjusting a water meter reading after the bill has been finalized 105855: Meter change out billing 106969: ACH HAS WRONG DATE ON WHEN FINALIZED

Ben Aplin] Good Morning, we've received your voicemail and will be reaching out to shortly to review.

Sreya Johny] Need to review with Mitch Understanding the Issue This discrepancy is expected and correct. In 2020, the IRS introduced a new W-4 form with significant changes to how federal withholding is calculated. The system correctly maintains separate deduction types for pre-2020 W-4 forms and 2020+ W-4 forms, as they use different calculation methods. Proposed Solution Explain to Becky that the difference between pre-2020 and post-2020 federal tax withholding calculations is expected and correct. The 2020 W-4 form redesign eliminated withholding allowances and introduced a different calculation method, which is why the system maintains separate deduction types. Steps to Complete Explain that the difference between pre-2020 and post-2020 federal tax withholding calculations is expected and correct due to IRS changes Clarify that employees who completed W-4 forms before 2020 use the older calculation method (based on allowances) Explain that employees who completed W-4 forms in 2020 or later use the newer calculation method (using Steps 1-4 on the new W-4 form) Advise that both calculation methods are valid and should remain in the system to properly handle employees with different W-4 forms Refer her to the W-2/W-4 page in the Employee Edit Form where the ""2020 or later W-4"" checkbox indicates which calculation method should be used for each employee Additional Information The 2020 W-4 form introduced the ""Step 2"" checkbox which affects withholding calculations. For employees who check this box on their W-4, the corresponding box should be checked in the system, and a different federal withholding deduction should be assigned to them. This is documented in the related tickets where similar issues were addressed.

Sreya Johny] Hi Becky, The difference between pre-2020 and post-2020 federal tax withholding calculations is expected and correct due to IRS changes. In 2020, the IRS introduced a new W-4 form with significant changes to how federal withholding is calculated. The system correctly maintains separate deduction types for pre-2020 W-4 forms and 2020+ W-4 forms, as they use different calculation methods. Both calculation methods are valid and should remain in the system to properly handle employees with different W-4 forms. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Sachin Yadav] Issue Castle Pines Metro. Dist. would like to set up the batch email process under BI in Impresa. This allows them to send mass emails to customers through the system. Proposed Solution Based on the related documents and similar tickets, we need to configure the batch email functionality in Impresa by setting up the SMTP server connection and ensuring proper permissions are in place. This will enable the customer to send batch emails to their customers through the BI module. Prerequisites The batch email process uses the Client machine and no longer uses the SQL server to send emails in version 4.25.0.0 and newer. The SMTP server needs to be set to allow the workstation/s to send emails. (They must be white listed.) Steps to complete Check the sa_company table in sql for the smtp_username and smtp_password field. if they are not there, run this script: IF NOT EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM syscolumns C WHERE id = OBJECT_ID('dbo.sa_company ') AND name = 'smtp_username' ) ALTER TABLE dbo.sa_company ADD smtp_username VARCHAR(255) NULL GO IF NOT EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM syscolumns C WHERE id = OBJECT_ID('dbo.sa_company ') AND name = 'smtp_password' ) ALTER TABLE dbo.sa_company ADD smtp_password VARCHAR(255) NULL GO EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; GO RECONFIGURE; GO EXEC sp_configure 'Ole Automation Procedures', '1' GO RECONFIGURE GO Run the following SQL commands on the database server to enable Ole Automation Procedures: EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; GO RECONFIGURE; GO EXEC sp_configure 'Ole Automation Procedures', '1' GO RECONFIGURE GO Update the SMTP server credentials in the database: UPDATE sa_company SET smtp_username = '[email_username]', smtp_password = '[email_password]' Ensure the SMTP server name is correctly configured in the Batch Email screen If using version 4.25.0.0 or later, ensure the workstations have permission to send emails through the SMTP server (as batch emails are now sent from workstations, not the SQL server) Guide the customer through the batch email process: Go to Utility Billing > Batch Entries > Batch Email Select criteria such as Billing Cycle, Billing Status, Class, etc. Choose accounts by book number or specific accounts as needed Create contact information and subject line Select target accounts and verify information Click Send to distribute the emails Key people that may be able to assist Chris Holloway - Support Analyst who has resolved multiple batch email setup issues Sachin Yadav - Support agent who has worked on batch email configuration Tony Williams - Support agent who has knowledge of batch email functionality and version requirements The customer's IT staff - Will need to provide SMTP server details and possibly grant permissions Additional Notes Based on the related tickets, common issues with batch email setup include: SMTP server configuration problems Missing Ole Automation Procedures permissions Workstation permission issues in newer versions of Impresa Missing or incorrect credentials for the email account We no longer use cdosys_mail_errorlog_id table for tracking problems with batch emails. It is still used for the email billing process. If the customer continues to experience issues after initial setup, we may need to check the error logs with: SELECT TOP 50 * FROM dbo.sa_cdosys_mail_errorlog ORDER BY cdosys_mail_errorlog_id DESC Referenced Documents 36710: KB: Impresa - How to send a Batch Email 33895: KB: impresa - How to Create a Service Location 33098: Impresa: Batch E-Mail 88902: KB: Impresa 4.23.0.0 Release Notes 34955: KB: Impresa: Payment Extensions 91470: Batch Email 93438: D0# 56904 - Mass Emails or Phone Calls 87442: Sending an email for shut off notices 101055: Batch Email Issue 106036: Batch email set up

Bonnie Ralston] I need to upload the ACH file today for the auto-pay customers. When I attempt to create the ACH file, I get a pop-up that says ""I/O error 123."" I can't get the file created, and I am running out of time to do this.
Ben Aplin] Good Morning Bonnie - I've sent an invite for tomorrow morning to review this.

Joe Sheehan] Issue Bonnie is experiencing an ""I/O error 123"" when attempting to create an ACH file for auto-pay customers in PowerManager. This error is preventing her from generating the necessary ACH file for processing customer payments. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and related tickets, this appears to be a file path issue. The error ""I/O error 123"" typically indicates that the system cannot access the specified file path when trying to create the ACH file. Similar issues were resolved in other tickets by correcting the ACH file path in the configuration settings. Steps to complete Connect remotely with Bonnie to review the current ACH file setup Open the ACH File form in PowerManager Click the ""Setup"" button to access the ACH Setup form Verify the ACH file name field has a valid and accessible path (this is likely the source of the I/O error) Correct the path if needed, ensuring it points to a location where Bonnie has write permissions Save the configuration changes Return to the ACH File form and attempt to create the ACH file again Guide Bonnie through selecting the appropriate customers and completing the ACH file creation process Key people that may be able to assist Susan Thomas (resolved similar ACH path issues in tickets #97191 and #107911) Mitch Mullins (experienced with ACH file creation in ticket #92688) Joe Sheehan (familiar with ACH file path enhancement requests in ticket #103514) Ben Aplin (already scheduled a call with the customer) Referenced Documents 98376: Payroll won't update because of deduction 92688: Wymore - On-site and remote setup and training. 103514: Popup window with button that takes you to Config screen. 97191: AP ACH posting error 107911: ACH Utility Billing

column1: 20960012,"How to enter CD's into the system","[2026-01-08 06:45
column2: Krystal Rutti] Hello, we have a few CD's that we have added from the cemetery, and I am needed to add those into powermanager. Just looking for a little direction to go about getting that done.
Ben Aplin] Good Morning - an analyst is reviewing your question and will get back to you shortly.

Ben Aplin] Issue The customer, Krystal Rutti from City of Wymore, needs guidance on how to enter cemetery CDs into the PowerManager system. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation provided, I can see that the customer needs to add Cemetery CDs as inventory stock items in the PowerManager system. This can be done through the Inventory module's Stock Number Maintenance feature. Steps to complete Access PowerManager and select ""Inventory"" from the main menu Go to ""Maintenance"" and select ""Stock Number Maintenance"" Click the ""New"" button to add new stock items Enter a number and description for each CD from the cemetery Consider using a consistent naming convention for the CDs (e.g., ""CD CEMETERY [NAME/DESCRIPTION]"") Enter any additional information such as order points if applicable Click the ""Close"" button to save your changes Additional Information When entering stock items, it's important to use a consistent naming convention so similar items will be grouped together. For example, all CDs should start with ""CD"" followed by their specific description. This will make it easier to locate and manage these items in the future. If you need to track vendors for these CDs, you can also set up vendor information through the ""Vendor Maintenance"" option in the Inventory module. Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Dahl Susan Thomas Referenced Documents 89418: CR Setting Change 103828: [EXTERNAL] Village of Amherst 106636: Deposit off- payment entered incorrectly 90128: Mis-dated Deposit 100544: correct GL enter
Joe Sheehan] Krystal, We're reviewing this and will get back to you soon. Joe
Ben Aplin] Hi Crystal - can you provide us a touch more detail. You have cash deposit that you'd like to enter into the system...onto an existing account, other?
Krystal Rutti] Hello, they are just new accounts and I want to make sure that I don’t miss a step while entering them into the system.

Joe Sheehan] Krystal, Please see the steps below for the proposed solution. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation provided, the cemetery CDs should be entered as inventory stock items in the PowerManager system using the Stock Number Maintenance feature in the Inventory module. Steps to complete Access PowerManager and select ""Inventory"" from the main menu Go to ""Maintenance"" and select ""Stock Number Maintenance"" Click the ""New"" button to add new stock items Enter a number and description for each cemetery CD Consider using a consistent naming convention for the CDs (e.g., ""CD CEMETERY [NAME/DESCRIPTION]"") Enter any additional information such as order point and/or low stock point if applicable Click the ""Close"" button to save your changes and return to the stock selection form Additional Information When entering stock items, it's important to use a consistent naming convention so similar items will be grouped together. For example, all CDs should start with ""CD"" followed by their specific description. This will make it easier to locate and manage these items in the future. If you need to track vendors for these CDs, you can also set up vendor information through the ""Vendor Maintenance"" option in the Inventory module by selecting it from the Inventory > Maintenance menu. Let us know if this answers your question. Joe
Krystal Rutti] We do not use the Inventory module. But I am also wondering why we would enter the CD’s which are a cash asset into the inventory. That is just very confusing to me.
Joe Sheehan] Krystal, Sorry, that was wrong information. Let me see if I can get the correct steps for adding a CD into the system. Joe
Tony Williams] Update the customer on the status of this issue please.

Susan Thomas] I'll help you create a response with instructions on how to enter new CD accounts into the PowerManager Banking system. Let me search the knowledge base for the proper procedure.# Based on the information gathered, I'll create a response for the customer explaining how to properly enter CDs into the PowerManager Banking system. Dear Krystal, How to Enter New CD Accounts in PowerManager Banking Access the General Ledger Module Open PowerManager and select the General Ledger module Set Up the Account Number Select ""Account Number Maintenance"" from the General Ledger/Maintenance menu Click the ""Add"" button to create a new account Enter the full account number following your chart of accounts structure Mark the account as ""active"" In the ""Uniform Account Information"" section, provide descriptions for each part of the account Select the appropriate account type (typically an Asset account for CDs) Click ""OK"" to save the new account Enter Beginning Balances Select ""General Journal Entry"" from the General Ledger/Processing menu Click the ""New"" button to create a new work file Enter a description such as ""CD Beginning Balances"" Enter the account number, date, transaction description, and amount for each CD The entry should debit the new CD account and credit the funding source account Click ""Save"" to save the transactions to the work file Optional: Set Up Banking Information If you need to track additional details about the CD such as maturity date, interest rate, etc., you may want to make notes in the account description or maintain a separate tracking system If you need any clarification or have additional questions about this process, please let me know.

REGINA WEBER] I need to move a paid invoice to a different vendor. Regina Weber Accounts Payable Clerk City of Kimball rweber@kimballne.org

Ben Aplin] Issue Regina Weber from the City of Kimball needs to move a paid invoice to a different vendor. The invoice has already been paid, but it needs to be reassigned to another vendor. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, this is a common issue that can be resolved using the Table Utilities program to change the vendor association for the paid invoice. This method allows you to reassign the invoice without having to void and recreate the payment, which is especially useful when the payment was correct but just associated with the wrong vendor account. Steps to complete Use the Table Utilities program to access the AP invoice records Locate the specific invoice that needs to be moved by searching for the invoice number or vendor information Change the APInvoice.VendorId field to point to the correct vendor Also update the SCBankTrans.AddressKey field to match the desired vendor's name/address record Save the changes to complete the reassignment Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Mullins Mitch Dahl Susan Thomas Sreya Johny Additional Notes This solution has been successfully implemented for the City of Kimball before, as seen in ticket #91505 where Mitch Mullins helped Regina Weber with a similar issue. The method is particularly useful when dealing with the same physical vendor that has different vendor accounts set up for different purposes, as noted in ticket #89789. Referenced Documents 97989: Changing Check information 91505: Kimball - Re-assign an AP invoice and check from one AP vendor to another. 89789: Payable Problem 104636: Refund Work File 106987: ach question

Sreya Johny] Need to review with Mitch: Issue The City of Gibbon created two new funds last month, and now their bank reconciliation is off by the total amount of dollars that went through those funds. The bank reconciliation shows an out-of-balance amount of $13,130.39. The customer has provided screenshots showing this discrepancy and has been requesting urgent assistance for several days. Based on the related tickets and documentation, this issue is likely caused by the new funds not being properly added to the Bank Reconciliation Setup. When new funds are created with their own GL cash accounts, these accounts need to be added to the Bank Reconciliation Setup to ensure transactions in those accounts are included in the bank reconciliation process. Steps to complete Access the Bank Reconciliation Setup by going to Banking > Bank Reconciliation Setup In the Bank Account Setup grid, locate the bank account that's showing the discrepancy Add the GL cash accounts for the two newly created funds to this bank account using the Navigator Control's ""Insert new record"" button Select the bank account in the Name column, then select the appropriate GL account in the Cash Account column for each new fund Click Close to save the changes Re-run the Bank Statement Reconciliation process to verify that the GL account is now in balance Technical Explanation In a fund-based accounting system, multiple general ledger accounts often make up a single bank account. When new funds are created, their associated GL cash accounts need to be manually added to the Bank Reconciliation Setup. Without this step, transactions posted to those new fund accounts won't appear in the Bank Statement Reconciliation module, causing the out-of-balance condition we're seeing here. Similar Resolved Tickets This issue is very similar to ticket #107653 where Mitch Dahl resolved an issue for the Village of Exeter by adding a missing GL account for a new fund to the Bank Reconciliation Setup. Other similar tickets include #104158, #104233, and #91285, all involving bank reconciliation issues related to new or missing fund accounts. Referenced Documents 107653: General Ledger/Bank Rec 104158: Bank Reconciliation 104233: Gibbon - Issue with bank statement reconciliation 93738: Yutan - Help with bank statement reconciling. 91285: Bank Statement Out Of Balance

Mitch Dahl] I think that the AI explanation/resolution is pretty spot on in this case. My guess is that they didn't add the GL cash accounts for the new funds to the Bank Reconciliation Setup. Doing so should resolve the issue.

Sreya Johny] The GL accounts for the new funds have already been added to the Bank Reconciliation setup. Susan mentioned that when she initially set up the funds, she remembered to include them in the bank statement reconciliation. However, when the account numbers were created, the Accounts Payable setup was not completed at that time. After discovering that the bank reconciliation was out of balance following the posting of AP entries, she added the Accounts Payable setup (0101) for funds 24 and 25. Transactions for other AP accounts, such as 22-0101, are appearing in the Transaction grid, but transactions for 24-0101 and 25-0101 are not showing.

Sreya Johny] Reached out to Susan with Mitch D and Ben. We found that incorrect Payable Account was entered for the 24 and 25 accounts in AP > Lookup Accounts, which caused the amounts to post to the wrong AP account. Using Table Utilities, we retrieved the correct account key by filtering FullAcct in the GLACCT table, then updated the AcctKey in the GLTrans table by filtering on the incorrect AcctKey. After making the correction, we reviewed the Bank Statement Reconciliation and confirmed that it is now in balance."

Chris Holloway] Hi Tina, The most likely cause of this is that the include inactives check box was not checked when the export was done from impresa. Without access to that export file we won't be able to confirm that this was the cause, but it is the most likely cause. Since these books were already applied, we use the Estimate process to pull the meters into a reading file in reading maintenance. If you are going to use that reading file, you may need to uncheck all the estimates boxes before you apply. The reason this works is because your other meters in books 50-52 have been applied, which puts a check in the meter read box for those meters. The inactive meters are in the same set of books, but they do not have a check in the meter read box. If you do not apply readings to these meters, you will have problems closing books 50-52 next billing period. Please open a new support ticket if you run into problems the next time you bill these accounts."

Mitch Dahl] This looks like when the charges for December readings/bills were computed, the posting date was not changed from the 11/24/2025 date that was used for the November readings/bills. This can be seen on the screen shot that shows the Adjustments (2 sets of 11/24 charges). After identifying the UBTransactions records that are associated with the December readings/bills, an update query can be ran to change the PostingDate field value as follows: update UBTransactions set PostingDate = '12/23/2025' where ""Key"" between X and Y X is the value of the Key field in the UBTransactions table of the first record that needs to be changed and Y is the value of the Key field of the last record that needs to be changed. The records will have a TransTypeCode value of 1 and the When field will contain a date on or about 12/23/2025. I would expect them to be consecutive records.
Ben Aplin] Hello Michelle - we believe the issue has been identified. Susan will provide next steps first thing tomorrow.

column2: VIKKI CARLSON] Mitch Don’t know if you are still around, but we need help with deleting a payroll check. Vikki Carlson Laurel City Clerk/Treasurer City of Laurel 302 E 2nd St Laurel, NE 68745 Office 402-256-3112 Fax – 402-256-9120

Sreya Johny] Hi Vikki, To void a payroll check: 1. Go to the Payroll module 2. Select ""Void Paychecks"" from the Processing menu 3. In the Void Paychecks form: Enter the employee's ID number in the Id# field (or use the ellipse button to select from the Employee Selection Form) Enter the original date in the Posting Date field Check the ""Post to GL"" box if you want the voiding transactions posted to the General Ledger module In the Paychecks Grid, click in the Selected column next to the paycheck you want to void Click the Void button to proceed with voiding the selected paycheck When the Updated column in the list of checks grid indicates ""False,"" no voiding transactions will be posted to GL because the original transactions have not yet been posted to GL. Susan -If a customer is marked as a location owner in your system, then that customer will not be used as part of the rate‑comparison process when you’re trying to figure out what rates to assign to a new customer at the same location. -Usually, when adding a new customer at a location, the system might look at other customers at that same location to help decide the correct pricing or rates. -But location owners are excluded from this comparison. 📌 Example Location: 123 Main StreetCustomer A → Location OwnerCustomer B → Regular customer (has assigned rates)You add Customer C at the same location. When the system tries to decide what rates Customer C should get, it: ✔️ Will look at Customer B’s rates❌ Will NOT look at Customer A’s rates, because Customer A is marked as a location owner. Being a location owner = they’re ignored for rate‑matching purposes. It prevents their rates from influencing or setting the baseline for new customers.

For some reason instead of taking 160.62 from Dvorak for Child Support it doubled it. How do I fix this?
