Sreya Johny] Hi Shelly, This can be resolved by creating a negative deduction for the employee in the next payroll cycle. This will effectively refund the extra amount that was incorrectly withheld from the employee's paycheck. Since the AP voucher has already been voided and re-entered correctly, we just need to ensure the employee receives the over-withheld amount in their next paycheck. Steps to complete Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Employee > Edit Select the affected employee (Dvorak) Navigate to the Deductions tab Find the Child Support garnishment deduction line Enter -160.62 (negative value) in the Total column Uncheck the 'Compute' checkbox to ensure the system uses the exact amount entered Save the changes When processing the next payroll, this negative deduction will add $160.62 to the employee's net pay, effectively refunding the over-withheld amount After the payroll is processed with this correction, the deduction will return to normal for future pay periods If you need to process this correction immediately rather than waiting for the next regular payroll cycle, you can run a payroll again just for this employee with the negative deduction and complete the steps in the payroll cycle and print a check or create an ACH file. Please let us know if this helps. Sreya
Joe Sheehan] On a call with Shelly. We took care of this one as well. Turns out the deduction had a default amount and it was in the extra field as well causing the 2x amount being taken out."

Sachin Yadav] Sorry, Karen, we don't have access to make you an Admin in your Network Domain; this has to be done by your IT group. I am attaching a Knowledge Base Article, you can refer to this to Mike on how to add a user to the Windows Active Directory Group. KB: Impresa - Adding a New User in Windows Active Directory These instructions will cover the basics of adding a new user to the Windows Active Directory as it relates to Impresa. Normally, your System Administrator or Network Administrator will add/change/remove people in the Active Directory. This is in no means a comprehensive guide, and you should always check with IT staff before making any changes to the Windows Active Directory. The idea of this document is to show you how to copy an existing user who has the access you would like the new user to have. As users come and go this method simplifies getting new users the access to Impresa they need. Open “Windows Administrative Tools” and select “Active Directory Users and Computers” In “Active Directory Users and Computers” select the “Users” folder then select user you want to copy, right click then click “copy”. Copying a user will allow the new user to mirror all settings of the old user, in this case we are focusing on the ICIS user groups the user is in (this will also copy the users access to the network and is why it is important to verify with IT staff before making the change). Be sure to copy a user with the access you would like the new user to have. Note: Impresa users are given access based on the Windows 'Active Directory' Group for network access to your domain. Once they are added to one of the ICIS_xxx groups on your domain, Impresa will authenticate with their windows login, give them the Impresa access of the group they are in and create their new username in the system automatically when they launch Impresa. Enter in the new user information. First/Last name, logon name, and password. Take note of the screenshot below, depending on your policies you will need to determine if the user can set their own password by checking “User must change password at next logon”. If they cannot change their password by checking “User cannot change password”. If you want the password to expire or not by checking “Password never expires”. An option is to leave “Account disabled” unchecked so I can delete the old user manually. Click “next” and you are done. The new user will need to logon to their workstation with the username and password you provided.

Ben Aplin] AI Suggestion - definitely validate before actioning it. Issue Eric Scott is encountering an ""Arithmetic overflow error converting numeric to data type numeric"" when attempting to close reading book 500. This error is preventing him from closing the book, which is necessary to apply reads and run billing. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and knowledge base articles, this error likely occurs because there are one or more meters in book 500 with extremely high readings that the system is interpreting as invalid values (possibly appearing as rolled-over meters). The solution is to identify these problematic meters and correct their readings before closing the book. Steps to complete Navigate to Utility Billing > Readings > Close Reading Books. Check the ""Print read meters report"" box and click the Printer icon to generate both the Read meters and Unread meters reports. Review these reports to identify any meters with unusually high readings (likely showing readings that appear to be rolled over with values close to 9999999). For each problematic meter identified, take a screenshot of the current reading for reference. Enter temporary corrected readings for these meters (reasonable values that won't trigger the arithmetic overflow). Try closing book 500 again. If successful, you can proceed with applying reads and running billing. After billing is complete, you may need to adjust the readings back to their correct values if necessary. If the above steps don't resolve the issue, an alternative approach would be to temporarily move the problematic meters to a different book number (one not normally used), close book 500, and then move the meters back after processing. Joe Sheehan] Issue Tracey from the Village of Morrill is experiencing issues with inactive meters showing up on meter reading and finalize bill reports after switching to Tantalus. She's also having problems with meter associations when changing out old meters, and is unsure about the correct water meter values for different Neptune models. Root Cause Based on related tickets, this is a common issue when integrating with Tantalus. Inactive meters continue to appear in reports because the meter manufacturer value is still present in the inactive meter records, causing PowerManager to include them in the Tantalus export files. Proposed Solution To prevent inactive meters from appearing in your meter reading and finalize bill reports, you'll need to remove the meter manufacturer value from those inactive meter records. This will prevent those meters from being included in the Tantalus files and receiving readings. Steps to Complete Go to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Equipment > Meter Select the inactive meter you want to modify Click the Edit button to display the Meter Edit form Remove the meter manufacturer value in the ""Manuf"" field Save your changes Repeat for all inactive meters that should not be included in the Tantalus files Additional Information Regarding your question about water meter values, the Neptune meter models (Neptune 10, Neptune T3, and Neptune M1) refer to different configurations in PowerManager: The numbers/letters after the manufacturer name indicate different settings for handling readings: T0, T1, T3 = Truncating 0, 1, or 3 digits M1, M10 = Multiplier set to 1 or 10 The correct setting depends on how your specific meters report readings and how you want them to appear in PowerManager For Old Meter Associations When changing out meters, check your meter change-out setup options to ensure information you're entering on new meters isn't being overwritten by values from old meters. Go to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Equipment > Meter Change Outs, then click the ""Setup"" button to review these settings. Sreya Johny] Issue Sandra from Cozad Board of Public Works is experiencing issues with large power bills. After attempting to fix the bills by re-entering readings from 11/18 and computing, printing, and finalizing them, she's now seeing doubled bills for November and some doubled bills for December. She also reports that reads from last month are missing from some accounts. Sandra needs assistance to correct these billing issues as customers are already inquiring about their bills. Proposed Solution Based on the ticket information and related documents, this appears to be an issue with meter readings not being properly marked as billed or finalized, causing incorrect prior readings to persist in subsequent billing cycles. The solution involves identifying the problematic accounts, correcting the readings, and properly marking them as billed. Steps to complete Schedule a remote session with Sandra to view the problematic bills directly. Identify which accounts have missing or incorrect readings from the previous month. For accounts with doubled bills, check if there are unbilled readings causing the prior reading to remain unchanged (similar to ticket #106156). Edit the problematic meter readings to reflect the correct values and mark them as billed. For finalized bills that need correction, use the Re-Bill feature in Utility Billing Adjustments (as described in KB article #88016): Use the Void button on the Bills tab Enter corrected meter readings Check the ""Post readings and calculate charges"" option Save the changes For any accounts requiring adjustments due to overbilling, create an adjustment with negative values for both the Amount and Quantity fields. Compute charges for the current billing period with the corrected prior readings. Print test bills to verify the corrections before finalizing. Finalize the corrected bills once everything looks accurate.

Ben Aplin] Hood Morning Tristan -

Mitch Mullins] Employee maintenance earnings/deductions,/employer/time off amounts become read-only after a paycheck has been printed but not yet updated. This prevents any changes to paycheck information after the check was printed. After the pay period has been updated time off values may be edited. It would be helpful to have a specific employee and to know which time off isn't calculating as desired. We'd need to know the employee's seniority and last accrued date. It would also be helpful to see how the time off item is defined in PR-Maintenance-Setup.

Sreya Johny] Hi Tristan, The time off values become read-only after a paycheck has been printed but not yet updated (finalized). This is a security feature to prevent changes to paycheck information after checks are printed. The time off values will become editable again after the pay period has been updated/finalized. Please complete the pay period update process (if your last pay period has not been finalized yet) and then try editing the time off values again by going to Payroll > Maintenance > Employee and clicking on the ""Time Off Edit"" button. In the Time Off Edit screen, you can select the appropriate time off item from the dropdown, and then adjust the ""Start"" values for the affected employees to correct their beginning balances. For a more detailed analysis, could you please let us know which employee(s) are affected, their seniority, last accrued date, and a screenshot of the Time Off Setup screen if possible to know how the time off item is defined in PR-Maintenance-Setup. Sreya

Would you please just call me to get this resolved? Tristan Keyes Account Clerk II City of O’Neill

Mitch Dahl] To my knowledge, PM doesn't have a way to carry over a certain number of hours from the previous year. There is a Maximum value for each accrual step though. That maximum value could be set to whatever value needed to achieve only carrying over 40 hours.

Sreya Johny] Hi Tristan, Mitch reviewed this and confirmed that PowerManager doesn’t have a way to carry over a specific number of hours from the previous year. However, each accrual step does have a Maximum value, which can be set to whatever value needed to achieve only carrying over 40 hours.

Chris Holloway] Issue The City of North Miami Beach is experiencing duplicate payment entries affecting 2,431 accounts. Payments originally made between December 1 and December 17 were reposted when a data push was completed after resolving an API communication issue between InHance and InvoiceCloud. This has resulted in credit balances on the affected accounts. Root Cause Analysis Based on the ticket conversation and related documents, the issue appears to have occurred due to a sequence of events: 1) A test environment upgrade was completed on December 17, 2) An API communication issue between InHance and InvoiceCloud was detected on December 22, 3) A failed data push occurred, and 4) When communication was restored on December 24, another data push was completed that inadvertently reposted payments that were already in the system, creating duplicates. Proposed Solution The duplicate payments need to be removed from the system through a developer-assisted process. Since there are 2,431 affected accounts, a manual reversal process would be time-consuming and error-prone. The Steps to Complete 1. Continue with the current approach of pulling the customer's data from iRecoverit for our developers to analyze. 2. Have our development team identify all duplicate payments by comparing transaction dates, amounts, and reference numbers. 3. Create a script to generate reversing entries for all identified duplicate payments. 4. Test the script in a controlled environment to ensure it only affects the duplicate payments. 5. Schedule a time with the customer to run the script in production. 6. After running the script, provide the customer with a report of all reversed transactions for their records. 7. Implement preventative measures to avoid similar issues in the future, such as enhanced validation during data pushes to detect and prevent duplicate transactions. Additional Considerations From ticket #96925, we've learned that Invoice Cloud cannot change the unique identifier for payments, which means duplicate payments cannot be re-sent to Impresa. The solution must involve processing these as corrections within the system. Key People Who May Be Able to Assist Based on the referenced tickets, the following customer support agents have experience with similar issues: 1. Chris Holloway - Has extensive experience with InvoiceCloud integration issues and payment processing (referenced in tickets #96925, #99466, #98586, #95348) 2. Ben Aplin - Has previously assisted with payment issues (referenced in this ticket) 3. Jimmy Biondo - Has experience with transfer issues between systems (referenced in ticket #99466) 4. Alex Mullins - Has experience with balancing payments between InvoiceCloud and InHance (referenced in ticket #92893) Next Steps I recommend we continue with the current approach of having our developers analyze the data from iRecoverit. Once they have assessed the situation, we can provide a more detailed timeline for the correction process. I'll keep you updated on our progress and reach out if we need any additional information to complete this task. DANA KLABENES] 1) I have a new employee with Head of Household marked on the W-4. In Power Manager, Employee 2 tab, I can only select marital status of single or married. Which does Head of Household fall under? 2)I am getting an error message that the wrong federal deduction code is used. I have code 93 and 166 that both say Federal Head of Household. Can you help?

Ben Aplin] Whether you pick married or single, it has nothing to do with the error you're receiving on the employee 2 tab. You want to be on the W2/W4 tab to fill out the employees W4 information. Under deductions, you'd have to add the head of household deduction for a given employee.

Ben Aplin] Susan, can you take a look at Dana's question please.
Ben Aplin] Hi Dana, we'll review again shortly.

Susan Thomas] Hi Dana, Please go through each step below to validate if you have set it up correctly. If you need any further assistance, please feel free to reach out to us. Susan Steps to complete Go to PR-Maintenance-Earnings/Deductions If you don't already have a Federal Head of Household deduction set up, create one by selecting ""Federal Income Tax Type Deduction"" and setting it up for Head of Household Use the ""Fed Update"" button to fill in the current federal tax table information - this will ensure the proper tax rates are applied Go to the employee record (PR-Maintenance-Employee) On the W2/W4 tab, check the ""2020 or later W-4"" option if the employee completed a 2020 or newer W-4 form Select ""Head of Household"" under filing status (section 1c) On the Deductions tab, ensure the employee has only ONE federal withholding deduction assigned - it should be the Federal Head of Household deduction If another federal withholding deduction (like Married or Single) is already assigned, replace it with the Head of Household deduction rather than adding a second federal withholding Save the employee record Important Note The marital status field on the Employee 2 tab does NOT determine how income tax withholdings are calculated. This is determined by the specific deduction assigned to the employee. Also, note that an employee can only have one federal withholding type assigned at a time - having multiple federal withholding deductions will cause errors.

Steps to complete 1. Go to PR-Maintenance-Employee and select the employee in question 2. If there is another federal withholding deduction (like Single or Married) already assigned, replace it with deduction #167 rather than adding a second federal withholding 6. Ensure deduction #167 (Federal Head of Household for 2020 or later) is properly assigned 7. Click the Save button - the warning message should no longer appear Important Note The marital status field on the Employee 2 tab does NOT determine how income tax withholdings are calculated. This is determined by the specific deduction assigned to the employee. Also, an employee can only have one federal withholding type assigned at a time - having multiple federal withholding deductions will cause errors.

Mia Altum] If someone accidentally post dated a payment plan, would that affect that throw off the balance between Impreza vs Transfer to G/L? Mia Altum Accounting Technician Main Office: (512)303-3943 Direct: (512)581-3113 Accounting Group: Ext 275
Ben Aplin] Hello - we're reviewing your question and will respond shortly.

Tony Williams] Mia, Payment plans in Impresa are designed to remove a portion or all of a balance from regular A/R to Payment Plan A/R. This process allows customers to pay off balances in predetermined increments without being penalized or considered for disconnection for amounts in the Payment Plan A/R. So, to answer your question: Yes, post-dating a payment plan can affect the balance between Impresa and the GL transfer. When a payment plan is created with a future date, the system may not properly transfer the payment plan amounts to GL until that date is reached, causing a temporary discrepancy between the systems. This is because transactions are typically posted to GL based on their effective dates. Steps to Resolve 1. Check the payment plan setup to ensure the correct dates are being used. 2. Verify the payment plan process settings in UB Administration > System Code Maintenance > Payment Plans tab to ensure they align with your GL transfer requirements. 3. If the payment plan was accidentally post-dated, you have three options: a. Edit the payment plan with the correct date b. Allow the system to process the payment plan on the post-dated date c. Remove the current payment plan and create a new one with the correct date 4. After correcting the date, run the ""Transfer to GL"" process to ensure the balances are properly synchronized. Prevention Tips To prevent this issue in the future, always verify the payment plan dates before finalizing them. The system uses these dates to determine when to transfer balances to GL, so accuracy is important for maintaining balance consistency between Impresa and GL. - Tony

Lori Rezny] New to the power manager system for W-2 & 1099 forms. Need help setting up the design for both and help filing electronically. I have the BSO User ID and password.
Ben Aplin] Hello - we're reviewing your issue and an analyst will be in touch shortly.

Ben Aplin] Mitch, does the below look correct? Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and related tickets, I can provide guidance on how to set up, design, and electronically file W-2 and 1099 forms in PowerManager. This process involves using the built-in design tools and electronic filing features in the system. Steps for W-2 Forms In PowerManager, navigate to Payroll > Processing > W2s Select ""W-2 Substitute Employee (2025)"" for the Report Name Set the Date Range (typically first and last day of the year) Use the ""Design"" button to customize the form layout if needed Preview the forms to verify the information is correct For electronic filing, go to W2 Electronic File option Enter your PIN Number from the Social Security Administration Complete the contact information fields Specify the filename and location for the electronic file Click ""Create File"" to generate the electronic submission file Steps for 1099 Forms Navigate to Accounts Payable > Reports > 1099 Reports And Form Select ""Substitute Form 1099"" for the Report Design Name Enter the Reported Year Select the Type of 1099 (1099-MISC, 1099-NEC, etc.) Set minimum 1099 amounts if needed Preview the forms to verify information is correct For electronic filing, click the ""E-File"" button Complete the required information for electronic submission Additional Notes PowerManager provides ""substitute"" forms that can be printed on plain paper - you don't need to order pre-printed forms. The system will print all boxes, text, and other form elements automatically. You'll still need to print copies for your employees/vendors, even when filing electronically.

Sreya Johny] Hi Lori, Please follow the steps below for processing W-2 and 1099 forms: Steps for W-2 Forms In PowerManager, navigate to Payroll > Processing > W2s Select ""W-2 Substitute Employee (2025)"" for the Report Name Set the Date Range (typically first and last day of the year) Use the ""Design"" button to customize the form layout if needed (Please preview the form and let us know if any changes to the design are needed) Preview the forms to verify the information is correct For electronic filing, go to W2 Electronic File option Enter your PIN Number from the Social Security Administration Complete the contact information fields Specify the filename and location for the electronic file Click ""Create File"" to generate the electronic submission file Steps for 1099 Forms Navigate to Accounts Payable > Reports > 1099 Reports And Form Select ""Substitute Form 1099"" for the Report Design Name Enter the Reported Year Select the Type of 1099 (1099-MISC, 1099-NEC, etc.) Set minimum 1099 amounts if needed Preview the forms to verify information is correct For electronic filing, click the ""E-File"" button Complete the required information for electronic submission PowerManager provides ""substitute"" forms that can be printed on plain paper - you don't need to order pre-printed forms. column2: DORI HUCK] I have 3 payroll questions that need to be addressed today for our payroll. These are high priority questions. Thankyou Dori Doretta “Dori” Huck City Clerk / Treasurer City of Bridgeport PO Box 280 Bridgeport NE 69336 Office: 308.262.1623 Fax: 308.262.1599 Email: dhuck@cityofbport.com
Ben Aplin] Hello, can you please tell us what the questions are.
DORI HUCK] Hello this is High priority..

DORI HUCK] Is there any updates to the federal fica state charts? How do we set up no tax on OT? A employee wants to do a flat amount for retirement and not a percentage / set up? On the 2025 W2’s how is the no tax on Overtime figured?

Mitch Mullins] The 12/30/2025 PowerManager update includes updated 2026 federal income tax table information. Use Payroll's Federal Update application to update the federal income tax tables when the time is right. (PR-Maintenance-Earnings/Deductions) Click the ""Fed Update"" button. Click the ""Update"" button. There is no setup required for ""No tax on overtime"" An employee may have the desired flat rate amount entered in the retirement deduction total. Be sure to un-check ""Compute"" so payroll knows to accept the manually entered total and not calculate the total itself. The IRS does not require 2025 W2 no tax on overtime amounts. We weren't planning on providing this information on the 2025 W2.

DORI HUCK] I am still waiting as this is a HIGH PRIORITY AS PAYROLL IS DUE FRIDAY. Please Call Dori Is there any updates to the federal fica state charts? How do we set up no tax on OT? A employee wants to do a flat amount for retirement and not a percentage / set up? On the 2025 W2’s how is the no tax on Overtime figured?

Sreya Johny] Hi Dori, The 12/30/2025 PowerManager update includes updated 2026 federal income tax table information. To check if your PowerManager is updated to 2025, please check the Release date in the About section and it should display ""09/23/2025"". Use Payroll's Federal Update application to update the federal income tax tables when the time is right (PR-Maintenance-Earnings/Deductions). Click the ""Fed Update"" button. Click the ""Update"" button. There is no setup required for ""No tax on overtime"". An employee may have the desired flat rate amount entered in the retirement deduction total. Be sure to un-check ""Compute"" so payroll knows to accept the manually entered total and not calculate the total itself. The IRS does not require 2025 W2 no tax on overtime amounts. We weren't planning on providing this information on the 2025 W2. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Sreya Johny] Hi Dori, As previously mentioned, your PowerManager was updated on 12/30/2025 with the 2026 federal income tax tables.

Sreya Johny] Hi Dori, Mitch reviewed this and confirmed that running the first payroll before updating the State Withholding Rates will not have a significant impact this year, as the changes to the federal and state withholding tables are minor, and there’s no employer contribution involved. Typically, tax tables don’t change enough from year to year to make a difference if you process a single payroll with the old tables. At year-end, any overpayment will be refunded, and any underpayment will need to be paid, so the normal year-end reconciliation applies here. Please let us know if you have any questions."
KRISTIN CHILDERS] I have to process payroll today and need to set up a Roth (post-tax) deduction, but I don't know how.

Ben Aplin] set up a new deduction: click new: choose deduction type: and fill in deduction details (example is flat, you will need to determine your type as it may not be) Since this is a post-tax deduction, not additional configuration required beyond defining the deduction Once the configuration is completed, you will need to assign the deduction to each employee and set the amount to be withheld.
KRISTIN CHILDERS] I need someone to call me and help me with this please. It is still coming out as pre-tax. Kristin Childers, CMC Clerk
KRISTIN CHILDERS] I still have something set up wrong because it is still being calculated as pre-tax instead of post-tax. I need someone to call me ASAP because payroll needs to be completed today!

Sreya Johny] Kristin, Could you please also check the Federal and State tax deductions under Payroll > Maintenance > Earnings/Deductions? Edit each tax deduction and review the E/D List in the bottom-right corner. In the “Deductions Used to Compute This Deduction” section (bottom left), please make sure the Roth deduction is not listed. If the Roth deduction is listed there, select it and move it to the right (Available Deductions) so that it is treated as post-tax. Please let us know if this helps.

column2: TERA EDEN] I am not sure who to email about this problem, Power Manager or Invoice Cloud, so I am emailing both simultaneously. I feel like it is more of a Power Manager issue, but need clarification. I was told long ago that if you changed the UBICdailyexport file would not be sent to Invoice Cloud if I changed the “included charges” field to either ""Printed on a bill"" or ""Finalized”. Last month I changed it to “Finalized” because just because a bill prints doesn’t mean it’s actually mailed out right away. Once I print bills, I go through them to make sure they are correct and nothing was missed during the “Compute Charges/Pre-Bill Report” part of bill processing. There are times where something has been missed, or computed incorrectly that would have to be recomputed then reprinted. What happened after I chose for the file to include what had been finalized is that a file was still created and sent to all the paperless customers that had their account balance previous to computing the new bills. This causes nothing but confusion for our customers, and nothing but frustration for us. We then get phone calls and emails asking why their “new” bill says they owe $0 if their balance from the previous bill had been paid, or whatever balance they had before the new bill was computed. I do not want to simply have the file send upon printing just because this is the easiest way. What do I have to do to get the file sent to Invoice Cloud when I want it to be sent? Please help! Tera Eden Utility Billing Clerk City of Syracuse

Sreya Johny] To review with Mitch: Tera is having issues with the UBICdailyexport file being sent to Invoice Cloud too early. Even when the Included Charges field is set to Finalized, files are still being created and sent to paperless customers showing previous account balances. She doesn’t want to rely on sending the file only when bills are printed just because it’s the easiest option. She’d like to know what can be done to control when the file is actually sent to Invoice Cloud.

Sreya Johny] Hi Tera, Apologies for the delayed response! To control when the file is sent to Invoice Cloud, you’ll need to turn off the automatic sending and run it only when you choose to. This can be done by removing the scheduled task in your Task Scheduler so the file does not send automatically. Create a desktop shortcut for the file and double-click the shortcut only when you are ready to send the file. This allows you to fully review and finalize bills first, then send the file at the exact time you want. Choose ""All"" for Included charges drop down. Please let us know if you need help with this and we will give you a call. Sreya

Sreya Johny] Steps to Complete Verify the customer has two meters properly set up in the system - one for delivered electricity (D) and one for received electricity (R) from solar production. Check that the R meter has been assigned the correct rate (similar to ""rate 79"" mentioned in ticket #94446) so readings can be entered properly. Ensure the billing-meters configuration is set to subtract the R meter's usage (solar production) from the D meter's usage (electricity delivered). Review the December meter readings to confirm they were entered correctly for both meters. If needed, use the Re-Bill feature in PowerManager Utility Billing Adjustments to void the incorrect bill and issue a corrected bill with proper readings. When using Re-Bill, make sure to check the ""Post readings and calculate charges"" option to save corrected meter readings and calculate charges properly.

column1: 20946648,"Criteria used on the Verify Apply Calculations Summary Report","[2025-12-31 08:49
column2: Tracy Schuld] What criteria is used to determine the ""Low"" or ""High"" callout on the Verify Apply Calculations Summary Report? Is it compared to the same time last year, the last read or what??

Tony Williams] Tracy, The High/Low flags on the Verify Apply Calculations Summary Report are based on percentage thresholds set in the meter models. By default, the system compares the current reading to the previous period's reading. However, there is a configuration option that can change this behavior to compare against the same period from the previous year instead. Detailed Explanation The High/Low settings are configured in two places: 1. In UB Administration > System Information > Meter Model Info - This is where the percentage thresholds are set for each meter model. For example, if a meter model has settings of 100/20, this means readings will be flagged as ""High"" if they are 100% higher than the comparison reading, or ""Low"" if they are 20% lower than the comparison reading. 2. In UB Administration > Configurations > Readings - There is a checkbox option labeled ""Last year's usage for high/low flag."" When this box is unchecked (default), the system compares the current reading to the previous billing period's reading. When checked, the system will instead compare to the same period from last year. Steps to Verify Current Settings 1. Go to UB Administration > System Information > Meter Model Info to check the current high/low percentage thresholds for your meter models. 2. Go to UB Administration > Configurations > Readings to check if the ""Last year's usage for high/low flag"" option is enabled or disabled. 3. The Verify Apply Calculations Summary Report will use these settings to determine which readings to flag as ""High"" or ""Low"".

Tracy Schuld] I went in and checked the ""last year's usage for high/low flag"". The percentage thresholds are set at 200/50. Account #30040 has cons of 1000cf for Nov/Dec 2025. It is flagged on the Verify apply report as low. The cons for Nov/Dec 2024 was 1000cf. Based on your explanation of the percentage thresholds, it should flag it as high at 2000cf and low at 500cf, not when it has the same exact cons as last year. Am I missing something?

Sachin Yadav] Some screenshots from Tracy's Configurations, System Information Tab. The Usage is flagging almost every account as below the Low Limit, while we found that the winter-time usage was less last year as well, we need to verify why this is being flagged as ""below limit.""

Garrett Treece] It appears that when customer accounts are being written off as bad debt, Impresa is debiting Accounts Receivable (Account #510.0000.102161) and crediting Bad Debt Expense (Account #510.0325.770130). As shown in the attached example, this is the reverse of the correct entry. The proper treatment should be to debit Bad Debt Expense and credit Accounts Receivable.Can you make this change or show me how to do it?

Tony Williams] Issue The customer, Garrett Treece from City of Hurst, has identified an accounting issue in Impresa where customer accounts being written off as bad debt are incorrectly posting debits and credits. Currently, the system is debiting Accounts Receivable and crediting Bad Debt Expense, but the correct accounting treatment should be the reverse: debit Bad Debt Expense and credit Accounts Receivable. Proposed Solution This appears to be a configuration issue in how the write-off process is set up in Impresa. The solution involves adjusting the account mapping in the system to ensure proper accounting treatment for bad debt write-offs. Since this relates to account mapping rather than a software bug, we can guide the customer on how to modify these settings. Steps to complete Access the System Administration module in Impresa Navigate to the GL Account Mapping section (likely under Financial Configuration) Locate the Bad Debt/Write-Off configuration settings Modify the account mapping to ensure that when write-offs occur, the system debits Bad Debt Expense (Account #510.0325.770130) and credits Accounts Receivable (Account #510.0000.102161) Test the configuration by processing a small sample write-off to verify the accounting entries are now correct If necessary, consult with your accounting department to ensure the new configuration meets your accounting standards Additional Information The issue appears to be specific to the account mapping configuration rather than a system-wide software issue. While we don't have documentation specific to write-off account mapping in the provided knowledge base articles, the general process for modifying GL account mappings in Impresa follows standard procedures. If you encounter difficulties accessing or modifying these settings, we can schedule a remote session to assist with the configuration changes. column1: 20945074,"Impresa KBA: Are you ready to close the books?","[2025-12-30 07:54
column2: Chris Holloway] Are you ready to close the reading books? Let’s check. Print the read meters report along with the unread meters report. Navigate to Utility Billing > Readings > Close Reading Books. Check the Print read meters report. Click the Printer icon. This prints both the Read meters report and the Unread meters report. (with the box unchecked, clicking the Printer Icon will only print the unread meters report). If the unread meter report is blank, you are ready to close the reading books. Go to step 11. If the unread meter report has meters on it, the close reading books process will fail. Go to step 6. Check the Read meters report. If there are meters on this report, then you have mixed read and unread meters. Determine how many meters you have that are read and unread. If you have only a few unread meters, then you can manually take care of these. Go to step 7. If you have half and half read and unread meters, call support and get help. Support has a script that can help with this if the conditions are right. If there are a few read meters you can manually take care of these. Go to step 9. Navigate to each of the unread meters’ Service Locations > connections tab. Change the book to a book number you do not normally use. This is to remove the unread meters from the book that you are trying to close, allowing you to close that book. Once all the books are changed for these meters, go to step 11. Navigate to each of the read meters’ Service Locations > connections tab. Be sure to change all these to the same book number. This is to remove the read meters to a new book that you can close. Close the reading books. Be sure to select the correct set of reading books to close. Click Save Changes to close the books. If the message “Process Complete” comes up, the book have been closed and you are ready to move on to applying the readings. If any meters come up in the list on the screen, repeat steps 7. Through 10. Depending on the situation. NONE OF THE BOOKS WERE CLOSED FOR ANY OF THE METERS IN THE BOOKS SELECTED. Notes: If closing more than one book at a time, it is possible for the unread meters to be in a single book in the range of book numbers you are closing. You can close all the other books separately and deal with the one book having the problem separately. If a meter connected to a group is in a prorate status of New or Calculated, the system ignores the meter read flag for that meter. This means that no matter what state the meter read flag is in, the system will not show it in the Read or Unread reports. If a meter connected to a group is in a prorate status of Calculated, when you close the book that it is in, the system will change the prorate status to none. If a meter connected to a group is in a prorate status of New, the system will change the prorate status to Calculated when the readings are applied in reading maintenance. You can check to see if you have closed the reading books by navigating to Utility Billing > Reports > Administration Reports. Selecting System Process Report and clicking next. Then select Close Reading Book, and enter a Process date begin and end from the drop down menus. Click Finish to view the report. The only time close reading books shows up on this report is if it was successful."

Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and documentation, this appears to be an issue with deduction settings during a special payout situation. When processing vacation time payouts, you need to properly handle deductions that should be excluded. The solution is to edit each employee's deductions to temporarily disable specific deductions for this special payroll run. Steps to complete Go to Payroll > Processing > Edit to select each employee receiving a vacation payout On the Deductions tab, for each deduction you want to exclude (Aflac and IRA), uncheck the ""Compute"" checkbox and enter ""0"" in the Total column Ensure that federal and state tax deductions still have the ""Compute"" checkbox selected After making these changes, click ""Calculate"" to recalculate the payroll without those deductions Process the payroll as normal After finalizing this special payroll run, remember to re-enable the deductions for future regular payrolls by checking the ""Compute"" box again for those deductions Alternative Solution If the above solution doesn't work, you can try entering negative amounts for the deductions you want to exclude. Go to the Deductions tab for each employee, enter the amount that would normally be deducted as a negative figure in the Total column and uncheck the Compute checkbox. This will effectively cancel out those deductions for this payroll run only.

Tony Williams] Andria, Below is a proposed solution to the issue you mentioned. If this doesn't address your issue, please reply back and someone will reach out to you as soon as possible. Note that we are running with shorter staff due to the Holidays through the rest of this week. Proposed Solution Based on the related documents and similar tickets, this appears to be an issue with multi-meter accounts where one meter is experiencing a calculation error. Since the books have likely not been closed yet (as this is a recent billing issue), we can use the reapply process to correct the charges for the problematic meter. Steps to complete First, check if the meters are properly connected both financially and physically to the correct service location. According to KB: Impresa - Meters Financial Location or Storage Location, this is the first place to look when having problems with a meter. Navigate to Utility Billing > Account Maintenance > select Meter, and check the Transactions sub-tab, then Financial Locations to verify the meter is correctly connected financially. Check the Current Physical Location field to ensure the meter is physically stored at the correct service location. Once connections are verified, create a new reading file in Reading Maintenance for the problematic meter with the correct reading and read date. Click ""Verify Reapply"" to see what changes will be made (select the detailed/Summary Calculations detailed report option to see what is changing). After verifying the changes look correct, click ""Reapply"" to reapply the most recently applied readings. This will overwrite the reading on the meter's edit reading screen and will remove the charge on the customer's ledger, replacing it with a new charge. When prompted with a message popup, click ""Yes"" to continue. If needed, revise the bill for this specific account. Additional Information If the books have been closed since the charges were applied, you will need to calculate what the charges should have been for each income center and make adjustments to correct the charges. In this case, refer to the KB: Make Corrections to Readings and Charges - REAPPLY document for the adjustment process. Also, it's important to note that while there is a setting to allow estimates on multi-meters, the system will not automatically do a correction from estimates on multi-meters. This may be relevant if any estimated readings are involved in this situation.

Tony Williams] Issue Southwest Suburban Sewer District is experiencing an issue with their GP/Impresa Integration after a server VSIT update. The integration appears to be broken, preventing them from completing their end-of-year processes, which has put everything on halt. This is a high-priority issue as it's impacting critical year-end operations. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, this issue is likely related to the Windows service that controls the integration between Impresa and GP. When servers receive updates or are rebooted, sometimes the integration service doesn't restart properly. The issue may also be related to authentication problems with the service that handles the communication between Impresa and GP. Steps to complete Check if the iONE Integration Windows service is running on the server. If not, restart it and set it to automatically start. Verify that the user credentials used for the integration service have not expired or been disabled. Check for time synchronization issues between the server and workstations (a difference of more than 5 minutes can cause authentication failures). If necessary, repair the integration by running the Winthrop DC Integration Toolkit. Test the integration by attempting to re-export to GL after the service has been restarted. Technical Details From the related tickets, we can see that similar issues have been resolved by: Restarting the service that controls the integration Ensuring the service is set to automatically start after server reboots Checking for time synchronization issues between servers and workstations Verifying that user accounts used by the integration are not disabled Reinstalling or repairing the integration toolkit when necessary Key people who have solved similar issues Alex Mullins - Has resolved multiple integration issues between Impresa and GP in tickets #88665, #92266, and #93612

Joe Sheehan] Just taking some notes before the call. It seems like this is happening because the Estimation setup parameters. I need to search the knowledge base for information about how estimated readings are calculated. Let me do that for you.# How Estimated Readings Are Calculated When a meter reading is marked as estimated, the system calculates the reading based on historical usage patterns. Here's how the estimation process works: Estimation Setup Parameters The system uses parameters defined in Utility Billing > UB Administration > System Information > Groups/Incomes > Estimates tab: Number of previous readings to use (normal and minimum required) Whether to consider seasonal usage patterns Whether to use default usage values when reading requirements aren't met Whether to consider readings from the same period last year Estimation Calculation Process The system analyzes historical usage patterns based on the configured parameters It calculates an estimated reading that reflects typical usage for that account This estimated reading is used to apply usage charges for the current billing period Corrections from Estimates When a non-estimated (actual) reading follows one or more estimated readings: The system takes the previous non-estimated reading and the current non-estimated reading and finds the difference This difference is divided by the number of estimated readings plus 1 to determine the average usage per period The system then recalculates charges for each estimated period: If the estimated reading was too high, it credits back the excess usage and charges If the estimated reading was too low, it adds the additional usage and charges The total adjustments are applied to the current billing period to correct for any discrepancies between estimated and actual usage. Important Notes Multi-meters can be marked as estimated, but the system will not perform corrections from estimates on these meters When correcting past readings, all consecutive readings since the incorrect one must be marked as estimated Reapplying over a previously estimated reading should be avoided; use adjustments instead

Based on the knowledge base information and similar tickets, I'll provide you with information on updating sewer rates in the PowerManager Utility Billing system. Process for Updating Sewer Rates Rate Maintenance Overview The process for updating sewer rates in PowerManager Utility Billing involves working with rate components and can be simplified through the use of shared components. Here's the step-by-step process: Access Rate Maintenance: Navigate to Maintenance > Rate > Rates in the PowerManager Utility Billing module Locate the Sewer Rate: Find the specific sewer rate you need to update in the rates list Select the rate to view its components Update Rate Components: Edit the existing components (such as base charges or usage charges) Update the amounts according to your new rate structure For flat rate charges, update the base charge component For usage-based charges, update the per unit charge components Consider Using Shared Components: If the same component (like a facility charge) is used across multiple rates, consider using shared components This allows you to make a change once that will apply to all rates using that component Component Consolidation (Optional): If you have duplicate components serving the same purpose, you can use the ""Consolidate components"" feature Access this via File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance > File > Consolidate components Verify Meter Assignments: For usage-based sewer rates, ensure the appropriate water meter is assigned to the sewer rate Check the Billing-Meters tab and add the water meter to the ""Meters Used By Billing Information"" section for the sewer rate Test the Rate Changes: After updating rates, test by computing charges for a sample customer Verify the billing statistics report to ensure charges are calculating correctly Special Considerations For winter averaging (basing sewer charges on winter water usage), you'll need to set up specific components that reference historical usage from previous months When updating rates, be mindful of effective dates to ensure proper billing transitions If you need to prorate charges during rate changes, this can be configured in the rate components Remember to document your changes and communicate rate updates to customers according to your utility's policies.

Chris Peterson] Customer # 102842 moved out on 12/29/2025, I computed charges as of that date and now it appears they have two bills. One for 12 days equaling $88.80 and one for 28 days equaling $117.27.Should I compute charges after they move out?
Ben Aplin] Hi Chris - I just wanted to let you know we've received your ticket and will get back to you soon on this.

Sreya Johny] Hi Chris, This issue occurs when a customer is moved out but the charges are computed incorrectly. When processing a customer move-out, the system needs to be configured properly to ensure only one final bill is generated. Steps to complete Check the ""Make inactive immediately"" option when processing the move-out if you don't want the customer to receive another bill. This will delete any current, non-finalized, non-adjusted charges. If you want the customer to receive one final bill, leave the ""Make inactive immediately"" option unchecked. The customer will be made inactive when they are finalized through Utility Billing. If the bills have already been generated but not finalized, you can void the incorrect bill through the Utility Billing Adjustments application. Use the Re-Bill feature in the Utility Billing Adjustments application to enter the corrected meter readings and compute charges. Make sure to check the ""Post readings and calculate charges"" option. If the bills have already been finalized, you'll need to create an adjustment to correct the charges. The duplicate bills likely occurred because the system was set to compute charges again after the move-out was processed. In general, you should not compute charges after a customer has moved out unless you're specifically using the Re-Bill feature to correct an error. The move-out process should handle the final billing appropriately based on your selection of the ""Make inactive immediately"" option. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya"

column2: SHEILA MARZOLF] With the new Federal guideline regarding OBBBA on Overtime Wages we are trying to figure out how to - put the OT Salary in Box 14?

Tony Williams] Sheila, Based on our documentation and helping other customers with this issue, here is a proposed solution. If this doesn't address your issue, let us know and someone can reach out as soon as possible. Proposed Solution To report overtime earnings in Box 14 of the W-2 form, you'll need to modify the settings for the overtime earning code in your PowerManager Payroll system. This can be done by configuring the W-2 reporting options for the overtime earning. Steps to complete Go to Payroll > Setup > Earnings/Deductions Maintenance Find and select your Overtime earning code Edit the earning and navigate to the W-2 reporting options Check the option to ""Print in Box 14"" for the overtime earning Enter an appropriate label for the Box 14 entry (such as ""OT WAGES"" or ""OBBBA OT"") Save your changes Test this by previewing a W-2 for an employee with overtime to ensure it appears correctly in Box 14 Additional Information Box 14 on the W-2 form is designated for ""Other"" information that employers want to report to employees. This box is commonly used for reporting items that don't have a specific box elsewhere on the W-2 form. Based on the documentation, the W-2/W-4 page in the Employee Edit Form allows for configuration of what information appears on W-2 forms, and the earnings/deductions setup controls what values get reported in specific boxes.

Tony Williams] Referenced Documents 104240: Utility Billing 89061: General Ledger Entry Issue 95358: General Ledger 104445: Utility Billing 107434: How to split a payment into two different accounts

Tony Williams] Melissa, Based on our documentation and helping other customers with this issue, here is a proposed solution. If this doesn't address your issue, let us know and someone can reach out as soon as possible. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and documentation, there are two main approaches to correct this issue. The recommended method is to void the incorrect payment and re-enter it on the correct account using the Enter Payments function in the system. Steps to complete Access the Utility Billing module and navigate to the Enter Payments function. Locate the account where the payment was incorrectly applied. Void the payment that was applied to the wrong account. This will remove the payment from that account. After voiding the payment, use Enter Payments again to enter the payment on the correct customer account. Verify that the payment has been properly applied to the correct account by checking the account history or balance. Alternative Method (if applicable) If the void and re-enter method is not possible due to timing or system constraints, you could also use a Transfer payment type to move funds between accounts."

Brenda Corman] Somehow My ACH File didn't save that I need to pay my employees. I needed two separate ACH files and it says they got deleted. Please Help ASAP.

Ben Aplin] Issue The customer, Brenda Corman from City of Superior (NE), is reporting that their ACH file for employee payments didn't save properly. They needed two separate ACH files, but the system indicates they were deleted. This is a high-priority issue as it affects employee payroll payments. Proposed Solution Based on similar tickets, the solution is to use the PR Data File Maintenance option to ""Un-print Non-Updated Payroll Checks"" and then recreate the ACH file. This will allow the customer to regenerate the ACH files that were not saved properly. Steps to complete Connect remotely with the customer to assist them through this process. Navigate to UB Datafile Maintenance > PR Datafile Maintenance > Unprint Non-Updated Payroll Checks. After un-printing the payroll checks, go to PR > Processing > Print Checks. Follow the steps to create a new ACH file, ensuring to specify the correct file name and path. Verify the ACH file was created successfully before submitting it to the bank. If the payroll has already been finalized/updated, additional steps may be required using Table Utilities to set Updated and PostedToGL to False for the relevant PREmplPay records. Additional Notes If the ACH file has already been sent to the bank and processed, creating a new file won't help. In that case, the customer would need to work with their bank for a solution. Also, verify if there are any issues with employee email addresses or setup that might have caused the original file not to save properly. column2: Christy Sartori] VERY URGENT!!!Please have Alex Mullins call us regarding a billing issue unable to close reading book to bill customers! This book was to be billed on 12/22/25 and is now 7 days late in billing it out.This is the message we are getting - ""Sorry, the following unread meters have caused the close out of the meter books to be unsuccessful""We have to have this resolved today because there is another holiday and close of the month.
Tony Williams] Christy, Alex left inHance earlier in the year. Someone else from our team will reach out. - Tony

Chris Holloway] Hi Maegan, You were able to apply the charges and move on with billing. Here are some tips on how to deal with this in the future and determine what to do next depending on the situation. Are you ready to close the reading books? Let’s check. Print the read meters report along with the unread meters report. Navigate to Utility Billing > Readings > Close Reading Books. Check the Print read meters report. Click the Printer icon. This prints both the Read meters report and the Unread meters report. (with the box unchecked, clicking the Printer Icon will only print the unread meters report). If the unread meter report is blank, you are ready to close the reading books. Go to step 11. If the unread meter report has meters on it, the close reading books process will fail. Go to step 6. Check the Read meters report. If there are meters on this report, then you have mixed read and unread meters. Determine how many meters you have that are read and unread. If you have only a few unread meters, then you can manually take care of these. Go to step 7. If you have half and half read and unread meters, call support and get help. Support has a script that can help with this if the conditions are right. If there are a few read meters you can manually take care of these. Go to step 9. Navigate to each of the unread meters’ Service Locations > connections tab. Change the book to a book number you do not normally use. This is to remove the unread meters from the book that you are trying to close, allowing you to close that book. Once all the books are changed for these meters, go to step 11. Navigate to each of the read meters’ Service Locations > connections tab. Be sure to change all these to the same book number. This is to remove the read meters to a new book that you can close. Close the reading books. Be sure to select the correct set of reading books to close. Click Save Changes to close the books. If the message “Process Complete” comes up, the book have been closed and you are ready to move on to applying the readings. If any meters come up in the list on the screen, repeat steps 7. Through 10. Depending on the situation. NONE OF THE BOOKS WERE CLOSED FOR ANY OF THE METERS IN THE BOOKS SELECTED. Notes: If closing more than one book at a time, it is possible for the unread meters to be in a single book in the range of book numbers you are closing. You can close all the other books separately and deal with the one book having the problem separately. If a meter connected to a group is in a prorate status of New or Calculated, the system ignores the meter read flag for that meter. This means that no matter what state the meter read flag is in, the system will not show it in the Read or Unread reports. If a meter connected to a group is in a prorate status of Calculated, when you close the book that it is in, the system will change the prorate status to none. If a meter connected to a group is in a prorate status of New, the system will change the prorate status to Calculated when the readings are applied in reading maintenance. You can check to see if you have closed the reading books by navigating to Utility Billing > Reports > Administration Reports. Selecting System Process Report and clicking next. Then select Close Reading Book, and enter a Process date begin and end from the drop down menus. Click Finish to view the report. The only time close reading books shows up on this report is if it was successful."

Carolyn Black] Drafts were not withdrawn on the 22nd and now the bill run done for the next cycle. Need to do ACH for December, but it pulls up both December and January. How do I pull ach for just December?

Tony Williams] Issue The customer, Carolyn Black from East Central SUD, is experiencing an issue with ACH withdrawals. The drafts were not withdrawn on the 22nd of December, and now the bill run for January has been completed. The customer needs to process ACH withdrawals for December only, but when attempting to do so, the system is pulling up both December and January bills. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and related tickets, this appears to be an issue with the ACH file generation process in Impresa. The customer needs to use the ACH File Edit function to manually select and process only the December invoices, excluding the January ones. Steps to complete Navigate to the ACH File process in Accounts Receivable module Enter the appropriate Transmission Date and Effective Settlement Date for the December drafts Specify the ACH file name and location where you want the file saved Click the ""ACH"" button to select customers and generate the initial file After the file is created, click the ""Edit ACH"" button to access the ACH File Amounts Edit form In the File Amounts Grid, identify and keep only the December invoices by modifying the amounts (set January invoice amounts to zero) Click ""Save"" to re-create the ACH file with only the December invoice amounts Process this file with your bank as usual for the December withdrawals After processing December's ACH, you can create a separate ACH file later for January's invoices Additional Information The ACH File Edit function allows you to manually adjust which invoices are included in the ACH process by changing their amounts. By zeroing out the January invoices, you can effectively exclude them from the current ACH file while keeping the December invoices at their correct amounts. This approach allows you to process December's ACH withdrawals independently from January's. KERRI MILLER] I have an employee who has changed his federal from Married to Head of Household. I though I set everything up.. but when I close ..it tells me that incorrect federal deduction. So I guess I need help setting this new withholding field up.
Ben Aplin] Hello - can you please attach the exact error message you are receiving?
KERRI MILLER] Please see attached screen shot Kerri L Miller Deputy City Clerk City of Tecumseh

Ben Aplin] Issue The customer is trying to change an employee's federal withholding status from Married to Head of Household but is receiving an error message stating ""incorrect federal deduction"" when attempting to save the changes. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, the issue is likely that both the Married withholding deduction and the Head of Household deduction are assigned to the employee simultaneously. The system requires that only one federal withholding type be assigned to an employee. You need to properly set up the Head of Household deduction and replace (not add to) the existing Married deduction. Steps to complete 1. Go to PR-Maintenance-Earnings/Deductions 2. If you don't already have a Federal Head of Household deduction set up, create one by selecting ""Federal Income Tax Type Deduction"" and setting it up for Head of Household 3. Use the ""Fed Update"" button to fill in the current tax table information 4. Go to the employee record (PR-Maintenance-Employee) 5. On the Deductions tab, locate the current Federal W/H - Married deduction 6. Instead of adding a new deduction, change this existing deduction to the Federal W/H - Head of Household deduction 7. Save the employee record - the error message should no longer appear Important Note The key is to replace the existing federal withholding deduction rather than having multiple federal withholding types assigned to the same employee. The system will flag this as an error because an employee can only have one federal withholding type. Tony Williams] Issue Kristi's Impresa has lost all of her favorites, settings, tabs, and is experiencing other issues that no other users are experiencing. Additionally, her receipt printer is not working, and her Impresa is running slower than everyone else's. Root Cause Analysis Based on the related tickets, this appears to be a combination of user profile settings corruption and possibly server performance issues. Looking at ticket #100619, there was a similar issue where Kristi specifically had problems connecting to the server after changes were made, and user settings were lost. This suggests that Kristi's user profile or settings files may be corrupted or missing. Proposed Solution The issue appears to be with Kristi's user profile settings in Impresa. Similar to what was done in ticket #100619, we need to check Kristi's roaming profile on the server for missing settings files and restore them if needed. Additionally, we should verify server performance and run maintenance tasks that have resolved similar slowness issues in other tickets. Steps to Complete 1. Check Kristi's roaming profile on the FS server for missing settings files: Connect to the server and locate Kristi's user profile Verify if settings files are present or missing Copy settings files from another user's profile if needed (as was done in ticket #100619) 2. Reset Kristi's Impresa settings: Clear user settings for Kristi in the database by running the appropriate SQL scripts Have Kristi log back into Impresa and reconfigure her favorites using the KB article instructions 3. Address server performance: Check if Windows updates need to be installed on the server Verify if the Inhance reindex SQL job is set up and running properly Clear temp tables and run efficiency scripts on the SQL server (as done in ticket #107608) 4. Fix receipt printer: Verify printer connections and settings Reinstall printer drivers if necessary Test printing functionality How to Set Up Favorites Again
Tony Williams] I'm going to clear out Kristi's roaming profile on both the Database and File Server tonight after Mtn Home closes to see if this fixes or helps this issue.
Sacha Pizzimenti] Can she keep her favorites and current settings?
Tony Williams] Sacha, It would wipe all that out. I can hold off until our Developers are back next week to have them look at this. - Tony

SAMANTHA GORDON] I moved a customer out and in but for some reason the usage was billed to the new resident and not a final bill to the old resident. How can I fix this?

Ben Aplin] Issue Customer moved a resident out and a new resident in, but the usage was incorrectly billed to the new resident instead of generating a final bill for the previous resident. Proposed Solution This issue occurs when a move-out process isn't properly completed before moving in a new customer. Based on the related tickets, the solution involves correcting the move-out process to ensure proper billing allocation between the previous and new residents. We need to make the previous customer active again, complete the move-out properly, and then adjust the billing for the new customer. Steps to complete Access the previous customer's account that should have received the final bill Make the account active again if it was marked ""Inactive Immediately"" during the original move-out Process a proper move-out for this customer with the correct disconnection date Ensure the ""Make inactive immediately"" option is NOT checked if you want to generate a final bill Verify the meter readings for the final reading of the previous customer If necessary, use Table Utilities to insert or correct meter readings for the new customer Re-compute charges for both accounts to ensure proper billing allocation Technical Details When performing a move-out, the ""Make inactive immediately"" checkbox determines how billing is handled. If checked, current non-finalized charges are deleted and no final bill is generated. For proper final billing, this option should be unchecked so the customer receives one more bill before being made inactive. The disconnected date entered during move-out is crucial as it's used for the customer stop date and billing-rates disconnected date. Key people that may be able to assist Susan Thomas
Susan Thomas] Reached out to Samantha, she is in the middle of something. She asked me to call back in 30 minutes.

Susan Thomas] Reached out to Samantha and connected remotely. She does not remember which customer it was and mentioned she might have already made an adjustment. I advised her that she can make the account active, then complete the move-out process using the correct disconnection date. If a final bill is needed, do not check the box for “Make Inactive Immediately.” After that, compute charges for both customers to ensure proper billing allocation."

Joe Sheehan] Issue Dana Klabenes from the City of Neligh needs assistance with processing payroll for Employee HSA contributions. The issue appears to be with employee deductions for HSA contributions that need to be fixed before Monday (12/29) when payroll processing needs to be completed. This is similar to a previous ticket they had on 12/16 with Mitch Dahl, but that was for Employer HSA contributions rather than Employee deductions. Proposed Solution Based on reviewing similar tickets, this appears to be an issue with HSA contribution setup in the Payroll module. The most likely issue is that the Employee HSA deduction is either not properly set up with the correct percentage or is missing a required GL account number for the withholding liability. This can be resolved by checking the employee's deduction setup and ensuring the proper percentage is entered, or by using the Payroll Edit function to manually adjust the deduction amounts if needed. Steps to complete Check the HSA deduction setup for the employee(s) in question to ensure it's properly configured Verify that the Employee Percentage field is correctly populated (similar to ticket #106148 where the percentage was missing) Ensure the HSA deduction has the proper GL account assigned for withholding liability (similar to ticket #98583) If needed, use the Payroll Edit function (accessible from Processing > Edit) to manually adjust the HSA deduction amounts Recalculate the payroll to verify the HSA deduction is now working correctly Process payroll as normal once the HSA deduction is correctly calculating Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Dahl - Previously helped with a similar HSA contribution issue for this customer Mitch Mullins - Has experience with HSA payroll deduction setup issues Elmer Armstrong - Has worked on HSA deduction error corrections Note This is a high-priority issue that needs immediate attention on Monday morning as the customer cannot proceed with payroll until this is resolved. The customer had a similar issue previously with Employer HSA contributions that was resolved by Mitch Dahl, so contacting him first might be the most efficient approach since he's already familiar with their setup. Mitch Mullins] Often update errors are caused by posted hours without a GL account. Sometimes this is caused by a blank line. I would start by reviewing posted hours for the employee. Susan Thomas] Customer’s Explanation: We are trying to reimburse the HSA amount that was originally deducted from the employee’s paycheck. The bank returned the HSA check to us, so the funds never reached the HSA account. Since that money was taken out of the employee’s paycheck, we need to pay her back. To do this, I processed an adjustment (AC) for the employee, Amy, to pay her the $32 we owe her (including $22 for the HSA). This payment was not intended to go through taxes or other deductions. I entered the amount as a negative adjustment and set all other fields to zero so nothing else would process. The adjustment was handled on the employee side under the deductions table, and I also zeroed out everything on the employer side.

Tony Williams] Issue Dana Klabenes from City of Neligh is having an issue with processing payroll for an employee named Amy. They need to reimburse an HSA amount ($22) that was originally deducted from the employee's paycheck but never reached the HSA account because the bank returned the check. They processed an adjustment (AC) for $32 total ($22 for HSA), but when trying to finalize the payroll, they're receiving distribution errors related to missing GL account information. Proposed Solution Based on the ticket details and related documents, this appears to be an issue with GL account setup for the HSA deduction adjustment. When making adjustments to reimburse employees for previously deducted amounts, proper GL account configuration is required for the distribution to calculate correctly. The error message indicates missing liability lookup values for certain deductions. Steps to complete Access the Payroll module and go to Processing > Edit to modify the employee's (Amy) deduction information On the Deductions page for this employee, locate the HSA deduction entry For this adjustment, uncheck the ""Compute"" box for the HSA deduction and manually enter the reimbursement amount ($22) as a negative value in the ""Total"" field Verify that the GL account for this deduction is properly configured in PR > Maintenance > Setup > Liability Lookup After making these changes, go to PR > Processing > Update and click the ""Distribution"" button to refigure the distribution Review the distribution report to ensure the amounts are allocated correctly If the distribution still shows errors, check for blank lines in the posted hours for the employee and remove them if found Finalize the payroll once the distribution errors are resolved Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Mullins - Has experience with HSA deduction setup issues and GL account distribution problems Mitch Dahl - Previously helped this customer with a similar HSA contribution issue Elmer Armstrong - Has worked on HSA deduction error corrections Susan Thomas - Currently assigned to the ticket and has been communicating with the customer Referenced Documents 88574: New Deduction Not Calculating 97532: Exception Payroll - $0 Pay 101515: Lyons - Help with PR transfers. 98583: Payroll Deductions 94177: From Email

Example Brandy Hulett has had a total of 1978.85 in SimpleCity. When I run the Journal Distribution report in Powermanager, it is only showing the amount the employee has received since performing payroll in Powermanager as of 10/1/2025. The other retirement related items seem to be ok. Its just the employer contribution that isn't showing. Additionally, the W2 isn't showing any employer contribution at all. It should be showing a second line on the W2 below the line I have highlighted that should show $2595.21 but nothing is showing. Therefore my W2 amounts are off. It should be showing $2162.69 as the 414H amount then there should be a second line below it that should show 1978.85+616.36 for a total of $2595.21. So two issues wrapped up into one here. The W2 is currently showing the employee and employer amounts added together of 2162.69+616.36. In our old system these displayed on two separate lines. The journal report does show employer contributions. It lists each earning type, employee deduction type, and then employer deduction type with totals after each section. The earnings section is concluded with the Total Hours and Gross Pay columns. Then it moves into employee deduction section, which ends with the Total Deductions and Net Pay columns. Then it lists the employer deduction section, which ends with the Total Employer and Total Payroll columns. Because Ashland has so many employees, their journal report lists as many employees as it can fit in the first page, lists all the sections, and then repeats the report for the next set of employees. As for the W2 not showing the ""second line that should show _____ but nothing is showing."" If she's referencing Box 12 or Box 14 on the W2, this designation comes from the Deduction setup. We need to run a ""dummy"" payroll to post these amounts into PM. When we update that payroll, the Post to GL option will be Never. As for the employee and employer deduction showing as a single sum on the W2, this is how W2s are in PM. In gWorks it might have been different but the IRS does not require them to be separate and PM's are not. For the deductions themselves, we need to review the Box 12 or Box 14 information. A change made to one of these deductions will ""ripple"" through the data and change/correct all the entries. So if something needs to change or a deduction needs to have a Box 12/14 entry and it doesn't, we can add the Box 12/14 selection and that should fix it.
