column1: 20938650,"Simple IRA Salary Deduction and W-2 Reporting Configuration","[2025-12-23 08:07
column2: cust] Simple IRA Setup and Tax Reporting Inquiry Customer needs assistance with configuring Simple IRA salary deductions in PowerManager's payroll system, specifically regarding: - Setting up percent of earnings deduction for Simple IRA - Employer contribution set to maximum 3% - Individual employee contribution setup - Correct W-2 reporting for boxes 1, 2, and 3 with Simple IRA contributions - Seeking clarification on how IRA contributions should affect wage reporting on W-2 A support ticket (108214) was created to get definitive guidance from the technical support team on proper tax reporting configuration. Message History set up a simple IRA salary deduction in payroll Hi there! I'm PowMan, your PowerManager Operator. I'm here to help you with any questions you have about using the PowerManager applications for your daily tasks. What can I help you with today? Whether it's finding an account, processing a payment, or generating a report, just ask! Let me find information about setting up a Simple IRA salary deduction in payroll. I'll check our knowledge base for you. I need to help you set up a Simple IRA salary deduction in PowerManager's payroll system. Based on what I found, you'll want to use the ""Percent Of Earning Deduction"" type for this. Here's how to do it: 1. Go to Payroll/Maintenance menu and select the Earning/Deduction option 2. Click Add to create a new deduction 3. Select ""Percent Of Earning Deduction"" from the list of pre-defined types 4. Fill in the description (like ""Simple IRA"") 5. Enter the percentage figure to be withheld 6. Set any minimum or maximum amounts if applicable 7. After setting up the deduction, you'll need to assign it to employees through the Employees menu Would you like me to provide more detailed information about any specific part of this process? should the W-2 show the same amounts in boxes 1,2 and 3? Looking that up for you... I don't find specific information about how Simple IRA deductions affect W-2 box values in the PowerManager documentation. This is an important tax reporting question that requires accurate information. To properly configure your Simple IRA deduction and ensure correct W-2 reporting, I'd recommend creating a support ticket so our technical team can provide specific guidance on tax reporting requirements for Simple IRA contributions. Would you like me to create a support ticket for you to get definitive information about how Simple IRA deductions should appear in W-2 boxes 1, 2, and 3? on the PR percentage deduction edit page, should the percentage box be 0? Let me check that for you. For a Simple IRA salary deduction using the Percent Of Earnings Deduction type in PowerManager, the percentage box should not be 0. You should enter the actual percentage figure that will be used to calculate the withholding amount. According to the documentation, in the PR - Percent Of Earnings Deduction Form: - ""Percentage - Enter the percentage figure used to calculate the withholding amount."" If you set this to 0, no money would be withheld. Instead, enter the appropriate percentage for the Simple IRA contribution (typically 1-3% or whatever your plan specifies). Additionally, you have options to: - Set minimum/maximum withholding amounts if applicable - Use employee percentage fields instead (check ""Use Employee Percentage"" or ""Use 2nd Employee Percentage"" if you want individual employees to have different rates) Would you like information about any other fields on this form? Employer information is set at max 3% That looks correct! For a Simple IRA, setting the employer contribution maximum at 3% is standard practice. The employer matching contribution is typically up to 3% of an employee's compensation, which aligns with IRS guidelines for SIMPLE IRA plans. This means you've properly configured the employer portion of the contribution. For the employee contribution part, you'll want to make sure employees can elect their own deferral percentage (usually up to the annual IRS limit) through the employee percentage option. Is there anything else you'd like to adjust with the Simple IRA setup? The employee's amount has been set up individually. The W-2 boxes 1, 2 and 3 show the same amount of wages. Should line 1 and the state be less the IRA contrubution? Let me check that information for you. I don't see specific information in the PowerManager documentation about how Simple IRA contributions should affect W-2 box reporting. This is an important tax question that requires accurate information. For a Simple IRA: - Box 1 (Wages, tips, other compensation) should typically show wages MINUS the employee's Simple IRA contribution - Box 3 (Social Security wages) may be different depending on tax treatment - Box 2 (Federal income tax withheld) would be calculated based on the reduced Box 1 amount Since this is a critical tax reporting issue and I don't have specific PowerManager documentation on this, I'd recommend creating a support ticket to get definitive guidance on proper W-2 reporting for Simple IRA contributions in PowerManager. Would you like me to create a support ticket for you to get accurate information about this tax reporting question? yes, please I'll create a ticket for you right away."

Ben Aplin] Issue Jordanna McCauley from the Village of Trenton is unable to email bills as she normally would. She is receiving an error message that says ""Could not load SSL library"" when attempting to access her email bills. This is preventing her from sending out bills that need to go out today. Proposed Solution Based on similar tickets, this appears to be an SSL library issue that can be resolved by adding or updating the OpenSSL files in the PowerManager application folder. This is a common issue that has been successfully resolved for other customers with similar error messages. Steps to complete 1. Verify that the OpenSSL files are present in the PowerManager application folder 2. If missing, upload the OpenSSL files (specifically ""ssleay32.dll"" and ""libeay32.dll"") to the PowerManager application folder 3. Ensure the files are named exactly as required: ""ssleay32.dll"" and ""libeay32.dll"" 4. If the customer is using Gmail, verify their SMTP settings and potentially create an app password (removing any spaces) 5. Test sending email bills again after these changes Additional Information If the OpenSSL issue is resolved but email bills still fail to send, we may need to check if there are any authentication issues with their email provider. Several similar tickets involved creating app passwords for Gmail accounts, so this might be a secondary step if needed. Joe Sheehan] Issue Karolyn McElroy is experiencing multiple payroll issues including: 1) problems entering Comp time for an employee, 2) difficulty entering retro pay for two employees, 3) paying an employee from two different departments, and 4) setting up employees to have their hours split between different departments (two employees at 50/50 and one at 60/40). Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and related tickets, these issues can be addressed through proper configuration in the PowerManager Payroll module. For the specific issues: Steps to complete For Comp Time: Navigate to Setup > Time Off Page and add Comp Time as a time off type. Configure the appropriate settings including number of decimals to track, maximum hours, and whether to use the accrual chart. For Retro Pay: Use the Post Hours function to enter retro pay. Select the employee, choose the appropriate earning code for retro pay, enter the hours/amount in the Posted Hours - Master section, and distribute to the appropriate GL accounts in the Posted Hours - Detail section. For paying an employee from two departments: In the Post Hours Entry Form, enter the total hours for the employee's earning, then in the Posted Hours - Detail section, distribute these hours between the two department GL accounts. For setting up employees with split hours between departments: This is handled during the posting of hours rather than in employee setup. When posting hours for these employees, distribute the appropriate percentage of hours to each department in the Posted Hours - Detail section (50/50 or 60/40 as needed). For the issue with the check button not working when moving between employees, ensure you've completed any required entries for the current employee before attempting to move to another employee. Additional Information The Post Hours function in PowerManager Payroll is the key tool for handling most of these issues. It allows for distribution of hours to different departments and entering various types of pay. For the Comp Time setup, you'll need to configure this in the Time Off settings before it can be used. Sreya Johny] Hi Marilyn, This was an intentional change made to the report format to better match the Nebraska 941N report requirements. The State of Nebraska now expects the 3rd month payment to be made with the submittal of the 941N report, which is why the report was modified to no longer display the third month figures separately. Sreya Johny] Hi Alex, We tried reaching you at 308-497-2416 and left a message. To calculate sewer charge based on the water usage: Go to UB > Maintenance > Rate > Calculate Customer Amounts. Select the Calculation Method as Usage Based and the dates should start from 10/1/2025 to 12/31/2025 and click Next. Select the correct Sewer Rate and the Flat Rate Components that need to be used for the calculation and click Next. Enter $14 as the Customer Charge according to your revised Sewer rate. Chris Holloway] Hi Annie, Yes, in the task scheduler under System Administration > System Configuration > Task Scheduler. Create a new task, select Penalties as the Task type. Enter a Code and Description, select your penalty group, Increment times penalized or not and the Due Dt Offset. Then select the cycle or cycles and save. If it complains, you may need to select a User to notify. Save the changes, then edit the schedule and save again. This creates a SQL job that runs on the schedule you select (or single execution if you selected that option).Be aware that this will run even if your normal billing is off by a few days. So if you bill on a specific day and run penalties directly after and you are running a little late on billing, you may need to adjust the schedule. Chris Holloway] Hi Annia, Due Date Offset Explained The Due Date Offset in the Task Scheduler for Penalties represents the number of days after the penalty transaction date when the penalty amount becomes due. If left blank: The penalty will be added as an unbilled balance with no due date (The penalties will show up in the detail of the next bill run) If set to a number (e.g., 30): The penalty will be due that many days after it's applied (The penalties will show up as a previous balance on the next bill run) Unfortunately, Impresa cannot currently automate penalties based on ""last unpaid due date plus one day"" as you requested. The system can only schedule penalties to run on specific calendar dates, not dynamically based on individual account's current due dates. Penalty Groups and Billing Cycles For your question about which penalty group would apply: You would need to create separate Task Scheduler entries for each combination of billing cycles and penalty groups that should run together When setting up each task: Select only the specific billing cycles you want to process in that run Choose the appropriate penalty group for those cycles Set the schedule to align with when those particular cycles' due dates occur Recommendation Since you print bills for different billing cycles throughout the month, I recommend: Create separate penalty tasks for each billing cycle or group of cycles with similar due dates Name them clearly (e.g., ""Residential Penalties"", ""Commercial Penalties"") Schedule each task to run one day after the due date of the balances to be considered for penalties.

Therese Magill] We are doing payroll, and we have a bunch of errors. Please see attached.
Ben Aplin] Hello Theresa, apologies for the delay. This should have been responded to earlier. We'll reach out shortly.

Susan Thomas] Hi Therese, Please review the steps provided to resolve the errors. Susan ""Try reposting hours with gl account numbers"" error: Problem This error occurs when employees have time off entries (like ""Out of pocket"") that are missing GL account number assignments, preventing payroll finalization. Resolution Steps Check Employee GL Account Assignments: Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Employee Edit each affected employee Check the Earnings tab Verify that GL account numbers are assigned to all earning types (especially ""Out of pocket"" or other time off types) Void Current Pay Period (if needed): Go to PR > Processing > Void Paychecks Void the current pay period for employees with 0 hours or missing GL accounts Update Distribution: Go to Payroll > Processing > Update > Distribution Select the affected employees This refigures the PR account distribution for GL Account numbers Finalize Payroll: After GL accounts are properly assigned, proceed with Finalize Select All Employees Error: Employer Contribution Expense error with deduction Root Cause Deduction was set up as Federal withholding There shouldn't be an employer contribution for Federal withholding The employee wanted extra Federal withholding, but the amount was entered in the wrong place (as an employer contribution instead of employee deduction) Check Setup: Go to PR > Maintenance > Setup Navigate to the Employer Expense tab Scroll down to deduction Verify the deduction type and employer contribution settings Un-print Checks (if already printed): Use PR Data File Maintenance option Select Un-print non-updated Payroll checks This allows you to make corrections without affecting net pay Remove Incorrect Employer Contribution: Remove the employer contribution from deduction #6 Ensure Federal withholding is set up correctly as employee deduction only Recalculate and Reprint: Re-calculate the payroll Print check on paper using the original check number You should now be able to Finalize the pay period successfully Key Point Employer contributions don't affect net pay, so this correction can be made even after checks are printed by using the un-print function. Net Pay error occurring in PM - Payroll module Net Pay errors are usually caused by a blank line in the Posted Hours - Detail area of the PR-Post Hours form for one of the employee's earnings. Access Post Hours: Go to PR > Post Hours Locate the Affected Employee: Bring up the employee experiencing the Net Pay error Find the Blank Line: Look in the Posted Hours - Detail area Locate the blank line in the employee's earnings Delete the Blank Line: Select the blank line Use the Delete Current Record button in the Navigator Control This will remove the problematic blank entry Process Payroll: Continue with payroll processing The Net Pay error should now be resolved This is a common payroll issue where empty records in the Posted Hours detail create calculation problems. Simply removing the blank line resolves the Net Pay calculation error and allows payroll to process normally.

Susan Thomas] We'll also need to make sure liability GL accounts have been added as well Use payroll update's ""Distribution"" button to re-figure the GL account number distribution after pay checks have been printed. (PR-Processing-Update).

If the item you're taking a payment for has only 2 general ledger account revenue account numbers, and it's easy to divide the amount in half, you could technically use cash register. To accomplish that, you'd want to make sure you have two items in you cash register setup. One item should go to the first GL revenue account, the other item should go to the other GL revenue account. In the transaction, you'd have two line items, each with their 1/2 of the payment. I would also like more information about the ""OWNER"" option under type of account. I need to know how that works in your system and if that is a location to add a landlord for that address.
Ben Aplin] Hello Tereas - we'll reach out to you next week to review. I'm reducing the severity of the ticket to 2.

Tony Williams] Teresa, Based on the related tickets with Customers and Documentation, we can provide an explanation for both items you are asking about: Regarding PRORATE %: The Prorate % feature allows you to apply a percentage of charges to a customer's bill when they move in or out mid-billing cycle. When a prorate percentage is entered (e.g., 50%), it is applied to any rate components that have the ""Prorate"" checkbox selected in their configuration. This prevents charging full amounts to customers who only occupied the property for part of the billing period. The prorate percentage automatically reverts to 100% after bills are finalized. Regarding OWNER option: Yes, the OWNER option under type of account is designed specifically for landlords/property owners. When a tenant moves out, you can perform a move-out transaction for the tenant and a move-in for the owner. This transfers responsibility for the utilities back to the property owner until a new tenant moves in. This feature helps ensure continuous billing at a property without gaps between tenants. Steps to Use These Features 1. For Prorating: Ensure the rate components you want to prorate have the ""Prorate"" checkbox selected in their configuration When processing a move-in or move-out, enter the appropriate percentage in the ""PRORATE %"" field (e.g., 50% for half a month) This percentage will only apply to components marked for proration (typically fixed charges, not usage or demand charges) After bills are finalized, the prorate percentage automatically resets to 100% 2. For Owner Accounts: When a tenant moves out, process their final bill Use the move-in function to activate the owner account for the property When a new tenant moves in, process a move-out for the owner and a move-in for the new tenant This ensures billing continuity for the property If this explanation and steps don't provide enough clarity and information for you, let us know and someone will reach out. Note that we are running with a smaller staff this week due to the Holidays.

It's probably set up there as a subtract metre. We would remove that reference > Add that solar received metre to the new solar received rate as an additive, as an add metre. If they already have a solar received rate set up then, assign our new credit component to that existing rate > Edit all customer accounts where this rate was assigned and change the solar received as an add meter in the billing meter section. (This is going to impact all customers assigned to that existing rate. So we want to make sure that that's going to apply to all customers and not just one specifically. If it's just for one specific customer, then add a new rate and not disturb the existing rate. This will then no longer subtract the received usage from the delivered usage like it's doing now.

Sreya Johny] Self Review: Issue Preston Public Utilities is experiencing issues with their solar billing setup. Currently, their system is subtracting the solar-generated electricity (kWh received) from the electricity used (kWh delivered) and billing customers for the difference at the retail rate. However, they need to change this setup so that customers are charged for what they use (kWh delivered) at the retail rate as one line item, and then credited for what they generate (kWh received) at the average retail rate (which is different from the standard retail rate) as a separate line item. Proposed Solution Based on the agent notes and similar tickets, we need to create a separate rate specifically for the solar received electricity with a negative component to provide the proper credit. This approach will allow the system to charge for delivered electricity at the retail rate while crediting received electricity at the average retail rate. Steps to complete Navigate to Rates > Add a new rate for ""Solar Received"" Add a component > Select ""Per Unit Charge"" type Enter the credit amount as a negative value (this should be their average retail rate) Select ""Positive Only"" for the ""Calculate When Usage Is"" setting Add the appropriate GL account number for this component Assign this component to a column in the billing statistics setup Remove the reference to the solar received meter as a ""subtract meter"" from the regular rate Add the solar received meter to the new Solar Received rate as an ""add meter"" (additive) If they already have a solar received rate, assign the new credit component to the existing rate Edit all customer accounts where this rate is assigned and change the solar received meter to an ""add meter"" in the billing meter section Note: If this change only applies to a specific customer, create a new rate rather than modifying an existing rate that might affect multiple customers

Sreya Johny] Self Review: Issue Preston Public Utilities is experiencing issues with their solar billing setup. Currently, their system is subtracting the solar-generated electricity (kWh received) from the electricity used (kWh delivered) and billing customers for the difference at the retail rate. However, they need to change this setup so that customers are charged for what they use (kWh delivered) at the retail rate as one line item, and then credited for what they generate (kWh received) at the average retail rate (which is different from the standard retail rate) as a separate line item. Proposed Solution Based on the agent notes and similar tickets, we need to create a separate rate specifically for the solar received electricity with a negative component to provide the proper credit. This approach will allow the system to charge for delivered electricity at the retail rate while crediting received electricity at the average retail rate. Steps to complete Navigate to Rates > Add a new rate for ""Solar Received"" Add a component > Select ""Per Unit Charge"" type Enter the credit amount as a negative value (this should be their average retail rate) Select ""Positive Only"" for the ""Calculate When Usage Is"" setting Add the appropriate GL account number for this component Assign this component to a column in the billing statistics setup Remove the reference to the solar received meter as a ""subtract meter"" from the regular rate Add the solar received meter to the new Solar Received rate as an ""add meter"" (additive) If they already have a solar received rate, assign the new credit component to the existing rate Edit all customer accounts where this rate is assigned and change the solar received meter to an ""add meter"" in the billing meter section Note: If this change only applies to a specific customer, create a new rate rather than modifying an existing rate that might affect multiple customers

REGINA WEBER] I need to change a Department Code on a Vendor that has already been paid. Regina Weber Accounts Payable Clerk City of Kimball rweber@kimballne.org

Tony Williams] Regina, Here are the steps to make these changes on your own. If you are unable to do so, please reply back and someone will reach out on a call. Based on our documentation, there are two main approaches to changing information on an already paid vendor invoice: Option 1: Create a General Journal Entry (Recommended for simple department code changes) Since the invoice has already been paid, you can make an adjusting entry in the General Ledger to correct the department code without affecting the vendor payment history. Steps to complete Option 1: Go to General Ledger > Processing > General Journal Entry Create a new journal entry Credit the original department code account that was incorrectly charged Debit the correct department code account Use a clear description that references the original payment and vendor Post the journal entry Option 2: Void and Reissue the Check If you need to maintain the complete vendor payment history with the correct department code, you can void the check and create a new one with the correct coding. Steps to complete Option 2: Go to Accounts Payable > Processing > Void Checks Select the check that needs to be voided Choose the ""Create a New Invoice"" option when voiding Edit the newly created payable to update the department code Go to Accounts Payable > Processing > Print Checks Print a new check (you can use the same check number as the original if the check has already been sent) Additional Information If you need to change vendor information rather than just the department code, you would need to go to Accounts Payable > Maintenance > Vendors and edit the appropriate vendor record.

Tony Williams] Mike, We'll need to ask our Developers to answer your questions, and they are out until next week. We'll tag them then for the answers. - Tony

Tony Williams] Mike, Until our Developers return next week, below is a proposed solution from our internal documentation. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and conversations, it appears that both iWEB and iNovah transactions are identified by their interface source in the system. Under normal circumstances, iNovah payments are reflected immediately in the system while iWEB payments typically go into a batch process. When viewing reports, these transactions can be separated by filtering by payment method, source, or audit number. Steps to complete Navigate to Utility Billing > Reports > Detail Transactions Wizard > Payment Select the appropriate Payment Option Enter the Beginning and Ending Transaction Dates for the specific date range when the mingled transactions occurred Filter by payment source - there should be options to filter specifically for iWEB or iNovah transactions If the transactions have different audit numbers, you can also filter by audit number to separate them Click Finish to generate the separate reports Additional Information The mingling of these transactions could have been caused by a server migration, SQL job issue, or a situation where the open transaction report screen was opened early and then more pending payments were processed without refreshing the screen. This is similar to what happened in ticket #106337 where a server migration enabled an SQL job that switched payments from live to batch, causing all payments to go into the view pending payments screen.

Joe Sheehan] I need to search for information about the payroll process to answer your question. Let me do that for you.# Payroll Processing Steps The 5 Steps of the Payroll Cycle: Posting Hours First step in processing a payroll cycle Use Post Hours from the Payroll/Processing menu Choose between Post (for daily entries) or Easy Post (for total hours in one day) Select employees and enter hours worked for each earning Calculating Amounts Second step - select Calculate from the Payroll/Processing menu Enter pay period ending date, check date, and payroll numbers Click Calculate button and select employees Printing Current Pay Period Reports Third step - generate reports for verification Hours Report - verifies posted hours Journal Report - lists earnings, deductions, net pay and employer contributions Printing Paychecks Fourth step - select Print Checks from the Processing menu Select bank account, starting check number, and check form Preview checks and print when ready Finalizing The Pay Period Final step - select Update from the Payroll/Processing menu Options for posting to GL/AP (Now, Later, Never) Click Finalize button to complete the payroll cycle Common Issues and Solutions: If you get ""no hours posted"" error, check for false records under Void Paychecks For out-of-order pay period records, use Void Paychecks to remove the problematic record To void a paycheck, use Processing > Void Paychecks and select the appropriate check

Mitch Mullins] We'll need to make a change for the 2026 W2, Box 12, Code TT. This is the code for no tax on overtime. We'll also need to recognize this code and perform the calculations to determine the amount to be reported.

Susan Thomas] Hi Adrianna, For the 2026 W‑2 reporting, a change is needed for Box 12, Code TT, which is used to report “No Tax on Overtime.” The system will need to recognize this code and correctly calculate the amount that must be reported for Code TT on the W‑2. Please ensure the necessary updates are made so that the system applies the appropriate calculations and includes the correct value on the 2026 W‑2 forms. Susan
ADRIANNA FIER] Is there a Power Manager update to provide the appropriate calculations and make sure box 12 will be filled in properly?

Susan Thomas] Steps to complete PowerManager will be updated to support the new tax code (Box 12, Code TT) for the 2026 W2 reporting. Our development team is currently working on implementing this change, which will include the ability to recognize the code and perform the necessary calculations. This update will be included in the next PowerManager release, and you will be notified when it's available. Once the update is installed, the system will automatically handle the ""No Tax on Overtime"" calculations and reporting correctly on the W2 forms. Continue with your normal payroll processes in the meantime, as the update will be implemented before the 2026 W2 forms need to be generated."

Example.Billed water $281,095.29 x 6% = $16,865.72, but the charge code calculated $16,827.83 that is a difference of $37.89. Kristina Stoki] All, I have done some updates to the initial setup for some of our earnings for box 14 and box 12 in the payroll setup area. Will those updates capture the full year or only after a change was made? I am getting nervous about this. Also do I need to order w'2s and 1099's through you or can I get them anyplace? Kristina Stoki City Clerk/Treasurer City of Ashland Ashland, NE 68003 (402)944-3387 (402)944-3386 fax
Tony Williams] Referenced Documents 106835: Help with Deductions Setup 94457: Payroll W-2/W-4 Tab 104362: ACH File-Payroll 98434: Earning/deduction setup 98916: Payroll

Tony Williams] Issue Kristina, I'll have Lee Gorman with our Forms team reach out to you for getting those forms ordered and what that would entail. Addressing your issue, for changes to W-2 box 12/14 settings will only affect how the earnings/deductions are reported on W-2 forms, not the actual calculations of those amounts. When W-2s are printed, the system will use the current box settings to determine where to place the annual totals. These settings can be modified at any time before printing W-2s at year-end. Steps to complete Verify the box 12/14 settings for each earnings/deduction by going to Maintenance > Earnings/Deductions and checking the W-2 configuration for each relevant item. For box 12 items, ensure the correct letter code is assigned (e.g., ""E"" for 403(b) plans, ""G"" for 457(b) plans, etc.) based on the type of benefit. For box 14 items, confirm that the appropriate description is entered that will appear on the W-2. Test the W-2 configuration by running a sample W-2 report (under W2's menu) for a test employee to verify the data appears in the correct boxes. Regarding forms, standard W-2 and 1099 forms can be purchased from any office supply store or online vendor as long as they meet IRS specifications. The software uses standard form layouts. Additional Information When printing W-2s at year-end, be sure to use the generic W-2 form design rather than any modified versions to ensure proper placement of data. The settings for box 12/14 are part of the earnings/deductions setup, not the W-2 design itself. Changes to these settings will determine where the annual totals appear on the W-2 forms when they are printed.
Ben Aplin] Closed as per K on call."

Susan Thomas] I reached out to Jill and Susan today. Customer Note: Minnesota Paid Family Leave Setup Blue Earth Light & Water will pay 100% of the Minnesota Paid Family Leave premium, including both employer and employee portions. However, the amount that would normally be the employee’s share must still be reported as taxable income on the W-2. We need the payroll system to: Create an earning code for the employee-share amount so it is included in taxable wages. Create a deduction code to offset the earning so net pay remains unchanged. Ensure the deduction does not appear on employee pay stubs. Apply this setup starting with the first payroll of 2026 and, if possible, retroactively adjust prior payrolls. This is a state-mandated program, and our approach differs from most employers because we are covering the full cost. Please confirm the Additional Customer Concern: This is the first year Minnesota has implemented this program, and we’re under time pressure because payroll runs Friday. We’re used to working with Mitch and the previous process, but with recent staffing changes and new responsibilities, this situation is stressful for us. We need clear guidance on how to proceed so we can remain compliant and avoid confusion for employees.

Mitch Mullins] This is similar to the discussion for Spring Valley MN. Apparently MN has a new state paid leave law. BELW is correct in the need to add a new earning and a deduction to be used to offset the earning. They paycheck design may be altered to hide the off-setting deduction as well as the earning if need be.
Susan Thomas] I spoke with Jill and Susan, and they confirmed they will update the ticket with all the requirements as soon as possible.

SUSAN SALISBURY] New starting in 2026 for MN is MN Paid Family Medical Leave.Blue Earth Light & Water is considered a small business, which put us at .66% for a rate. BELW will be paying for both the employee (.44%) and the employer (.22%) portions. The .44% amount needs to show on the W-2 in box 14 and gross up wages for that .44%. Our auditor said this ""First, PFML, you should set up the deduction as a after-tax deduction on the employee. If the employer is paying 100% then you will need to setup a “earnings” and “deduction” for the employee amount so you are still grossing that up in your payroll and including it for tax reporting. For 2026, the employee portion will go in box 14 of the W-2.""We think this has nothing to do with sick time, but sick/vacation time can be used to help supplement the PFML, since there is limit on the wage employee can receive based on income.Since BELW is paying the full amount, we were wondering if the earning and deduction would clear each other out and not need to be shown on a pay stub. Not sure if that is possible.Our main goal is that we need it to take the .44% and show in taxable wages, and in box 14 as the .44%.
Ben Aplin] Mitch has been working on how to make this work. Just need to show Susan how it's done. Susan to record session so we can share it out.

Susan Thomas] Hi Jill/Susan, Please follow the instructions below regarding the MN PFML setup: MN PFML Setup Do NOT add extra Hourly earnings for MN PFML (earning #48). The setup works the same for hourly and salary employees. Using the Salary tie‑earning is correct. No rate is required on the employee’s Earnings tab for MN PFML. The system will automatically calculate the PFML amount based on the deduction. You do not need to enter or update anything each month. 457(b) Option Do NOT check the “Employer 457(b)” box. This option is only used when employer 457(b) contributions must be based on taxable Social Security and Medicare wages. If checked, the system will look for W‑2 Box 12 codes G or EE, which you do not need. You could set the $0.44 employer deduction as a 457(b) temporarily to calculate PFML automatically, but: You must remember to switch it back to Box 14 before preparing W‑2s. Otherwise the amount will be reported incorrectly. Payroll Reporting & Overrides Earnings and deductions will appear immediately on the Payroll Journal—no need to wait until the pay period is updated. You can manually override payroll employee totals if needed: Enter your own amount. Uncheck the “Compute” box. This is useful for correcting prior pay periods. Please review this information and let me know if you need any additional assistance. I’m happy to help with anything further.

Ben Aplin] Hello - an analyst will get back to you shortly.

Sreya Johny] Understanding the AMR Export Errors Based on your ticket, you're encountering two types of errors during the AMR (Automated Meter Reading) export process in PowerManager Utility Billing: ""Unable to locate Equinox meter type"" and ""Mismatch in maximum number of meter readings."" These are common issues that occur during the meter reading export/import process. Error Explanations 1) ""Unable to locate Equinox meter type"" - This error indicates that one or more meters in your system have been assigned an AMR type code that doesn't match what the Equinox system is expecting. Essentially, the meter type configuration in PowerManager doesn't align with what your meter reading system recognizes. 2) ""Mismatch in maximum number of meter readings"" - This error occurs when the number of readings expected for a particular meter (as configured in the AMR type code setup) doesn't match the ""ReadNrMax"" value for that meter. For example, if you have a demand meter that requires multiple readings but it's configured with an AMR type code that only expects a single reading. Solution Steps For ""Unable to locate Equinox meter type"" error: Check if any meters are assigned to incorrect service types (e.g., an electric meter assigned to a water AMR type code) Verify that all your meters have the correct AMR type codes assigned Ensure your Equinox setup in PowerManager is properly configured for all meter types you use For ""Mismatch in maximum number of meter readings"" error: Identify the meters causing the error (they will be listed in the error report) Check if these are demand meters that require multiple readings Verify that the AMR Type Code assigned to these meters matches the expected number of readings For electric demand meters specifically, consider changing the AMR Type Code to one that expects demand readings (like Type 7, as mentioned in ticket #103392) After making these corrections: Try running the AMR Export process again Review the error report to confirm all issues have been resolved Additional Information The ""UB--AMR Export Errors"" report is actually a helpful tool designed to identify configuration issues before they cause problems in your meter reading process. By addressing these errors, you'll ensure more accurate meter readings and billing. Support Agents Who Have Solved Similar Issues Mitch Mullins (has extensive experience with AMR export issues and Equinox meter type problems) Mitch Dahl (helped resolve AMR Type Code issues with demand meters) Susan Thomas (assisted with meter reading verification and import issues) Referenced Documents 96573: Morrill - Why do some readings have a 10/21 date? 92168: Need Help With Meter Reading Export 102010: Arapahoe - Help with Neptune 360 94818: Itron export error 103392: meter reading

Ben Aplin] Issue Michelle from Sanborn Municipal Light Plant is reporting that payments were applied twice to customer utility accounts. This appears to be due to a new employee forgetting to update the batch number when posting in the Cash Register module, causing the previous batch to be applied to Utility Billing customer balances twice. The issue is affecting customer account balances and appears to be impacting their GL (general ledger) as well. The customer is urgently requesting assistance as they need to apply past due charges and prepare late notices before beginning meter reading. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and documentation, this is a case of duplicate payment posting that requires correction in both the customer accounts and potentially the GL. We'll need to identify the affected transactions and reverse the duplicate payments to restore accurate balances. Steps to complete Schedule a remote support call with Michelle to review the affected accounts and identify the specific batch that was posted twice. Use Table Utilities program to identify all duplicate payment transactions from the affected batch. Create a new payment batch with a unique batch number and use the ""Transfer Credits"" utility to reverse the duplicate payments. When setting up the transfer credits: Assign a unique batch number Use weekend payment/posting dates to keep these transfers separate from regular payment reports Set Type of Payment to ""Transfer"" Process the credit transfers for all affected customers Verify the GL transactions to ensure proper accounting of the reversals Run customer history reports to confirm balances are now correct Technical Details Based on the KB article ""Utility Billing Data File Maintenance – Transfer Credits,"" we can use the payment entry forms to post offsetting positive and negative payment amounts to correct the duplicate payments. This will help maintain accurate accounting while fixing the customer balances. The article specifically mentions that this approach ""can help increase late payment penalty calculation accuracy and remove customers with an overall zero balance from the Accounts Receivable report."" Tony Williams] Based on related tickets and documentation, this is a common issue when handling customer transitions. The solution involves properly processing the move-out for the previous customer and transferring the charges to the new owner through the correct move-in process in PowerManager's Utility Billing system. Kayla, Have you tried the following suggestions? Steps to complete 1. Make the previous customer active again if they've already been deactivated. 2. Process the move-out correctly using the Service Orders Move Out form: Uncheck ""Make inactive immediately"" option to ensure the final bill processes correctly Enter the correct disconnection date (when the previous owner moved out) Complete the move-out process 3. Process the move-in for the new owner: Use the Service Orders Move In form Enter the correct connection date for the new owner Complete the move-in process 4. If the previous bill has already been finalized, create an adjustment to remove the charges from the previous owner's account and apply them to the new owner's account. 5. Print and finalize the bills for both customers to ensure proper accounting. Additional Information From other related customer issues, it appears this issue commonly occurs when customers are made inactive immediately after a bill has been printed but before it's finalized, or when route book numbers are unusual and might be missed during the billing process. Making sure to follow the proper move-out/move-in sequence and checking that the billing is finalized properly will help prevent this issue in the future.

Tony Williams] Issue The customer is experiencing multiple issues with their utility billing system, including incorrect penalty calculations and problems with ACH bills for budget customers. The customer has attempted to follow instructions for penalty calculations but reports that many bills are not correct. Budget customers on ACH were only charged $17.33, and the January 1, 2026 bill has doubled for them. Proposed Solution Based on reviewing similar tickets and documentation, this appears to be a multi-faceted issue with penalty calculations and budget billing. The main issues involve incorrect penalty calculation, particularly for budget customers, and possibly issues with data transfer from Gworks. The solution requires reviewing penalty setup and calculation processes, as well as checking budget customer settings. Steps to complete Contact the customer directly at their provided number (402-349-5360) to discuss all the specific issues they're experiencing. Check if bills were properly finalized before penalties were calculated (as seen in ticket #87601, non-finalized charges will not be penalized correctly). Verify the penalty setup parameters by going to the Penalties form and clicking the Setup button. For budget customers with incorrect amounts, review their transaction history via the Adjustments process to check if penalties were applied incorrectly. For customers who have already been incorrectly penalized, show the customer how to remove incorrect penalties through UB-Processing-Adjustments, using the Fines tab to enter negative amounts for incorrect penalties. Check if multiple penalty calculations were run for the same period, which could cause duplicate penalties (as seen in ticket #97920). For ACH budget customers, verify if credits need to be transferred between services using the UB Data File Maintenance option to Transfer Credits. Verify data transfer integrity from Gworks to ensure all customer information and billing settings were properly imported. Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Mullins - Has extensive experience with penalty calculation issues Mitch Dahl - Has helped with penalty issues and budget customer problems Ben Aplin - Current agent on the ticket who provided initial assistance Referenced Documents 98882: Budget Customer 105380: Budget customer and penalty 97920: Tell City - Call Julie to discuss door knockers and penalty amounts 98339: Customer Utility Bill 87601: Battle Creek - Penalties

Unfortunately, there is no way to add to a previous day's payment file. When you enter a payment, there are 2 dates associated with that payment, the transaction date and the payment date. column2: Joe Sheehan] Issue Natalie Foulk from the Village of Palmer is experiencing two issues with PowerManager: 1) Her payroll was finalized twice and she needs one instance deleted, and 2) Due to the payroll issue, she cannot run any month-end reports. She has been trying to get assistance since December 23rd. Analysis Based on the ticket conversation and related documents, this appears to be an issue with payroll processing in PowerManager. When payroll is finalized twice for the same period, it creates duplicate records that prevent proper month-end reporting. This is a known issue that requires specific steps to resolve. Proposed Solution The solution is to use the Void Paychecks functionality in PowerManager to remove the duplicate payroll finalization. This will allow Natalie to properly run her month-end reports. From the documentation provided, we can see that the Void Paychecks feature is designed specifically for this purpose - to cancel out erroneous payroll amounts. Steps to complete 1. Access the Void Paychecks function through the Processing menu in the Payroll module 2. Select the employee(s) affected by the duplicate finalization by ID number 3. Enter an appropriate Posting Date for the void transaction 4. Select whether to post the voiding transactions to the General Ledger 5. In the Paychecks Grid, select the duplicate paycheck that needs to be voided 6. Click the Void button to complete the process 7. After voiding the duplicate payroll, verify that month-end reports can now be generated correctly Additional Information According to the PR help documentation, the Void Paychecks function is specifically designed to ""void printed paychecks or to remove current pay period amounts."" This is exactly what Natalie needs to resolve her issue with the duplicate payroll finalization. Customer Support Agents who have solved similar issues Based on the referenced tickets, the following agents have experience with PowerManager payroll issues: - Emelia De Smet-Bacon (worked on payroll setup and configuration issues in ticket #102385) - Mitch Mullins (assisted with various PowerManager issues including payroll in multiple tickets) - Mitch Dahl (helped resolve payment voiding issues in ticket #102040) Referenced Documents 102385: Brule - PM Payroll Add on Project 98268: Palmer - Help with UB customer bill design 102040: SEVERAL ERRORS - PLEASE CALL ME 103408: Clearwater - Not everyone penalized? 105740: Fiscal Year End - Unable to Complete

Joe Sheehan] I'll need to look up information about running a 941 report in the Utility Billing EZ system. Let me search the knowledge base for you.# How to Run a 941 Report in Utility Billing EZ To run a 941 report in PowerManager Utility Billing EZ, follow these steps: Access the Quarterly Tax report through the Payroll module Click the ""941"" button on the Quarterly Tax form to access the 941 Form options Select the appropriate output type: Federal Form 941 Nebraska Form 941N (if applicable) State 941 Worksheet (for other states) Click the ""Calc"" button to select employees and calculate Form 941 amounts Review the information on the tabbed pages (for Federal Form) or scroll through the form Edit any fields as necessary When satisfied with the information: For Federal Form 941: Click ""Preview"" to display the form in Adobe Reader for printing For Nebraska Form 941N: Click ""Preview"" to display the form in Adobe Reader for printing For State 941 Worksheet: Click ""Preview"" or ""Print"" to produce a report for manually filling out your state Form 941 Note: Adobe Reader must be installed on your computer to view and print the Federal and Nebraska forms, as they are generated using PDF format. Additional Options: Use the ""Select Employees"" button to specify which employees to include The form contains different tabs for various parts of the Federal Form 941 You can sort employees in different ways using the ""Sort By"" option Please let me know if you need any clarification or have additional questions about running the 941 report.

Paige Sperling] Adjusted a customers budget payment history amount and the system will not add a penalty for non payment to said customers account.
Ben Aplin] Susan, is this a legitimate S1 (severity 1)?

Susan Thomas] Hi Paige, Could you please review the setup and give it another try? Let me know if you need further assistance. Susan There is an option in the penalty setup screen to exclude customers with active payment arrangements from receiving penalties. How It Works: When this penalty exclusion option is enabled: The amount due after the due date remains the same as the amount before the due date Customers with active payment arrangements won't be penalized for non-payment Bill Display: The information on the lower-right portion of the bill will not change This information comes directly from the payment arrangement itself Account Summary Tracking: If customers stick to their payment arrangement, the ""Previous Balance"" in the ""Account Summary"" section should decrease each month by the monthly arrangement amount Budget Payment History: The system can adjust a customer's budget payment history amount and will not add penalties for non-payment to customers with arrangements.

Mitch Mullins] If a penalty isn't calculating for a budget customer we'd want to review penalty setup to make sure budget customer's are getting included. Amounts may not be considered if they are more recent than the due date that is being used to calculate penalty amounts. Disconnect letter printing may have the only include customers with penalties option checked. No penalty, no disconnect or reminder letter.

Susan Thomas] If a penalty is not calculating for a budget customer, we need to review the penalty setup to ensure that budget customers are included in the configuration. In UB-Reminders The Disconnect Letter Printing process has an option called “Only include customers with penalties (Penalized only).”If this option is checked, the system will only generate disconnect or reminder letters for customers who currently have a penalty applied. “Please verify whether the ‘Only include customers with penalties’ option is checked in Disconnect Letter Printing. If it is checked, customers without penalties will not receive disconnect or reminder letters. Adjust the setting if you want letters to be sent to all applicable customers.”

I do not believe it is a settings error, as there have not been any issues prior doing the disconnect process. The issue is the accounts were not correct before the initial report was ran, and after adjusting accounts to be as they should. The system still will not add penalty fee to those account. Susan Thomas] The account had multiple adjustments on 12/1/2025, which made it appear paid during the initial penalty run. After corrections, the account now shows the correct budget amount due. However, penalties will not apply automatically because the system processed the original cycle as paid. Please manually add the penalty fee for this account to ensure compliance. Penalties can be entered in UB-Processing-Adjustments-Select Customer-navigate to the Fines Tab Using the Fines Section for Penalties Fines - Used to adjust penalty/fine transactions. Enter new penalties - Enter the penalty/fine for each applicable service in the Adjustments section and save changes.

Tony Williams] Kristina, Based on the related issues and tickets with customers, and documentation, here is information about the retirement plan checkbox in the Payroll module's W-2/W-4 tab and an explanation on when it should be checked. If this doesn't solve your issue, please let us know and someone will reach out. Explanation of the W2 Retirement Plan Checkbox The ""W2 Retirement Plan"" checkbox appears on the W-2/W-4 tab in the employee maintenance area of the Payroll module. When checked, this box indicates on an employee's W-2 form (in Box 13) that the employee participated in a qualified retirement plan during the tax year. This checkbox should be marked for employees who are ""active participants"" in qualified retirement plans such as: 401(k) plans Qualified pension, profit-sharing, or stock-bonus plans under IRC Section 401(a) Annuity plans under IRC Section 403(a) or 403(b) Simplified Employee Pension (SEP) under IRC Section 408(k) SIMPLE retirement accounts under IRC Section 408(p) Government employee plans (other than 457(b) plans) When this box is checked on an employee's W-2, it notifies them that they may not be entitled to a full deduction for their traditional IRA contributions, depending on their filing status and modified adjusted gross income. Steps to configure retirement settings Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Employee > Edit Select the employee you want to configure Navigate to the W-2/W-4 tab Check the ""W2 Retirement Plan"" box if the employee participates in a qualified retirement plan For employer contributions, go to the ""Employer"" tab and set the appropriate percentage for the retirement deduction For employee contributions, check the appropriate settings on the ""Deductions"" tab Save your changes
